reviews.text:
Marvellous park with lots of attractions.
Great for people watching and listening to the musical performances.
A feast for the senses.
Lumphini Park is highly regarded as the "green lung" of Bangkok, consistently praised in reviews for being a peaceful oasis that offers a rare escape from the city's urban chaos. Visitors love its unique wildlife, particularly the large water monitor lizards, and the variety of free recreational activities available.

Hihglights
Wildlife Observation: A major draw for many is the resident population of Asian water monitor lizards, which can often be seen sunbathing or swimming in the canals. While harmless if left alone, they are a highlight for photographers and nature enthusiasts.

Recreation & Exercise: The park features a popular 2.5km paved loop used extensively by joggers and walkers. Reviewers frequently mention the free-to-use outdoor gym equipment and organized aerobics classes that take place in the late afternoon.

Lake Activities: Visitors can rent swan-shaped paddle boats to explore the central artificial lake. Many recent reviews note that these are sometimes available for free or for a very low fee (approx. 40 Baht for 30 minutes).

Local Vibe: Early mornings are praised for sightings of locals practicing Tai Chi and yoga, providing a glimpse into authentic Bangkok community life.

Lumphini Park is a beautiful oasis right in the middle of bustling Bangkok. Surrounded by skyscrapers, the park offers a refreshing break from the city’s traffic and noise.
The large lake, shaded walking paths, and open green spaces make it perfect for morning jogs, relaxed strolls, or simply sitting and enjoying the view. Early mornings are especially peaceful, with locals practicing tai chi, jogging, and enjoying the cooler air.
One of the most unique experiences is spotting the famous monitor lizards roaming near the water — a surprising but fascinating sight! Despite being in the center of the city, the park feels calm and spacious.
Whether you want exercise, relaxation, or just a quiet moment in nature, Lumphini Park is a must-visit spot in Bangkok. It’s clean, well maintained, and completely free to enter — a true urban sanctuary.

Park like this should be in every bigger city. Super clean, lot of attractions and lot of seating and chilling areas. Monitor lizard and other interesting creatures are just like a bonus. This beautiful green park is in the middle of concrete jungle but you dont feel like it. I recommend visiting

Huge well maintained park with loads of things to do and with such a lively ecosystem! Theres tons of water monitor lizards walking freely, loads of different birds species and even stray cats just wandering around. Even found a siamese stray cat here 😆
You can also rent a bike if you dont prefer walking around. Theres rental bikes at the entrance. This green one is electric and goes fast but is pricey.
The boats and kayak are also free of charge. You can use it for around 15mins (?) which is more than enough.
Love the ambience here. Great for chilling and just hanging around. I wish we have a third space like this back in the PH ☹️

I love this park most. It's really big and calm. You can find many animals you can't see in Vietnamese parks such as some birds, crows, giant lizards,.... Plus, this park is next to MRT Silom. I recommend you fo there for walking and running before 10am or after 4pm.

reviews.text:
Easily one of the most beautiful green spaces in Bangkok.

The park is very spacious, clean, and well maintained. Walking around the large lake was incredibly relaxing. The atmosphere feels peaceful despite being in the middle of a busy city.

I especially loved the dedicated paths for walking and cycling. It’s a perfect place for a morning jog, or just to sit and unwind.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for fresh air and a calm, scenic spot in Bangkok.

A well-maintained and spacious park in the heart of Bangkok, perfect for walking, jogging, or just taking a break from the city. The park is especially nice in the early morning or late afternoon when the weather is cooler and the atmosphere is calmer.

The skywalk and green bridge connecting different sections of the park are highlights, offering great views of the lake, greenery, and surrounding skyline. Plenty of space for casual exercise, cycling, or simply sitting by the water.

Facilities are clean and well-organized, including restrooms and seating areas. The park feels safe and is popular with both locals and visitors, without feeling overly crowded.

Very accessible by public transport, within walking distance from BTS and MRT.

Overall, a great urban park for relaxation, light exercise, or a quiet escape in the middle of Bangkok.

A wonderful park right in the center of Bangkok! I came here for a early morning run, and had such a nice experience. The park is perfect for running/walking and I truly appreciate the separate running lane! The park is also well maintained and very clean.

Not too busy when I arrived, around 7-8pm! I'd definitely recommend this for runners and people who just want to relax and enjoy some greenery and beautiful scenery in the center of BKK. ✨

Great park to relax, enjoy the greenery and exercise.

Good views of the city clean safe shaded environment for walking or jogging.

Opens early and closes late, about 10 pm.

Very accessible, close to BTS Asok or MRT Sukhumvit.

Suitable for all ages and families as well.
At the end your visit you can visit the convention center to have an Air Condition dose and have a drink or meal.

The elevated walkway is really nice and great place to take pictures and see people taking pictures as well.

Benjakitti Park is my favorite park in Bangkok. It is peaceful, green, and refreshing, like an oasis in the middle of a busy city. The park is very convenient because it is close to BTS Asok or MRT Queen Sirikit and other public transport. You can exercise, walk, jog, or enjoy a relaxing picnic with friends or family. I love the beautiful landscapes, modern architecture, and calm lake views. The wetland restoration of trees and birds make the park feel natural and alive. It is a must-visit place in Bangkok. The only negative point is that there are fewer restrooms and has to walk far.

reviews.text:
Had very good experience i completed 22K today this is my first time here, after 5 pm pleasant wind blowing and sun set reflecting on pond surface there have separate track for cycling just for one circle distance is 2 and half kilometres.
Visitor can enjoy the sport complex and public gym.
Specially perfect for running and family and friends picnic 🧺.
There have shop each main gate ‼️ There is no locker facilities.

State Railway Park opens daily from around 5am. It is walkable from both MRT Chatuchak and BTS Mochit.

Tremendously huge park with lots of nature sightseeing. From pond to trees and sunflowers. Many types of trees all around.

You can either jog, walk, run or cycling here.

Many wild animals can be seen here too. A lot of professional and novice photographers often come here for photoshoot.

Theres sport center, court, cafes, public toilets all around.

Highly recommend.
Such a beautiful park — it really exceeded my expectations! There’s a cute coffee shop at the entrance, lovely gardens, and lots of different trees and animals. I had a really nice evening there

Another surprisingly large and wonderful park in Bangkok, easily reached by MRT. The park appeared to be full of wildlife, with many people walking and running. Helpful locals pointed me to some beautiful owls high up in the trees, seemingly unbothered by all the people photographing them.

Right across the street from Chatuchak Park is Wachirabenchathat Park, one of my favorite places for a calm walk or a run. Highly recommend it.

The park has a large lake inside and
Next to the lake, there’s also a designated area where dogs can run around freely🐶🐾🐕🐩such a happy spot to watch😘

reviews.text: Appropriate for track running and it’s free! Some tracks are rough but still good enough. Clean and sufficient toilets. No lockers and vending machines. Have limited free parking.

I attended my first concert here, it was a Stray Kids dominATE tour and it was the best day of my life yet. The facilities around the stadium and staff help was really amazing. I really wish to see more of my favourite artists there. ❤️
went here for skz concert. this stadium is huge. nevertheless it’s easy to get in, and find our seat. didn’t get the chance to explore the facilities there. anyhow ++point it’s near to train station.

I watched the dominATE concert of Stray Kids here and the place was simple, but what I liked with it is its accessibility. MBK Center is just beside it and there are many hotels just across so very accomodating for foreigners like me. I don't have much complaints about the venue because I can see the effort to organize everyone considering the large crowd. If there's a concert here, I will definitely watch again!
DJ Deng and his co-DJ's were truly amazing there, be it day or night, thanks to everybody for our great holiday mood :)

reviews.text:
Beautiful park to enjoy an evening walk away from the heat of the day. It is very well manicured and it has several public restrooms throughout it. It is the perfect blend of nature and urban.

There were many runners this evening getting their exercise as well as couples and families picnicking in the park.

Beautiful park that is well connected to the MRT and BTS. There are also neighboring parks also connected including one with a butterfly garden. It's free to visit with free filtered water at the entrance and plenty of restrooms. The park has traffic noise but still feels peaceful and fully worth a visit.

A great quick escape from the busy city of Bangkok.

So peaceful and clean. There are areas where you can rest and think. I love how attentive the park staff are. They are also polite and answers questions.

It is big. People ride bikes here and I wished I have one, too.

I love how the animals here are thriving. They even have chipmunks here. So cute.

Will definitely comeback.

3.6 ⭐️
This park was very nice…
But very very crowded. I get why it’s so crowded though! So many people running in 90 degree heat 😮‍💨 a lot of people had some version of a marathon shirt on. Go everyone running, go! Plenty of restrooms and seats in the park. I would recommend if you’re in the area. But go on a day when it’s not as hot, and maybe during the week when it’s not as crowded.
The Thai sakura in early February is so special. I wish I had know before! What a well kept, maintained and clean park. It's beautiful and there is so much bird song. The squirrels love it too!

reviews.text: This small park has some treeless patches, so the best times to visit are morning or evening. Given the surrounding urban development, it feels a bit disappointing you’d expect more greenery in this city spot.

• A wonderful small public park in the city, thoughtfully designed to give off natural and refreshing energy.

• The wide green lawn, tall trees, and clean paths make it feel like a park you’d find in Singapore. It’s peaceful, well-maintained, and a great escape from the busy city.

• Highly recommended if you’re looking for a calm, natural spot in the middle of Bangkok.

Slightly elevated park, with views, large lawn area. Small pond with wooden bridge. Stone rockery with water feature on both sides. Mostly surrounded by mature trees and shrubs. A few benches in the shade. The art center building is currently empty at this time, under renovation as of May 27 2025. Restrooms are still open.

Outdated, old mindset. Why so wrong it’s just a little dog on a leash..The park is like other parks in Thailand. Seemed it‘s a pet friendly park. But it‘s not.
Restricted a dog must be walked only the area at the back. Many homeless, the area is unclean.

An excellent public park near Chulalongkorn University campus 🌳. The area is very well maintained, with neatly trimmed trees and grass 🌿✨. It’s clean, safe, and quite spacious 🧺.

People come here to exercise, run, or just relax 🏃‍♂️☀️. It’s a great spot for picnics or resting after errands or sightseeing around Siam 🛍️🍃. University students often use it for group activities as well 🎓👥. The atmosphere is especially wonderful on nice weather days ☀️😊.
There is also a small art gallery located within the park 🎨🏛️, adding a cultural touch to the relaxing environment.

reviews.text: Beautiful view point. Quiet area. Follow the signs for Bansuan Chomview when you get near the town. They also offer coffee/tea as well as a few cottages to stay overnight in.

The place offers a nice view of Ban Rak Thai Commune.
A quite place for enjoying country view.
They have coffee, soft drink, and food for sale here.
If you wanna stay overnight, they have room for rent too.
Entrance fee: 10b/pax

Driving about 30-40 minutes from town brings you to this scenic spot, which is perfect for capturing picturesque views and enjoying a tranquil atmosphere. The heart-shaped decorations and swings add a charming touch to the experience. The area is well-maintained, and the elevated platform offers breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. This is a peaceful retreat for families and couples who appreciate nature and scenic beauty.
We were only there for a quick visit couldn't comment on the rooms.  The view is very nice, though.
Just go for the view and had a quick lunch (so salty) T T

reviews.text: It's very beautiful.
I brought my child for vaccination on November 3rd. The air conditioning was good and the service was good.

With proper maintenance, it would be much better. It's a standard sports stadium capable of hosting a variety of sports competitions, including a futsal court, volleyball court, a meeting hall, and a small garden for light exercise. There's also a good amount of exercise equipment.
Came to get a special round of vaccinations. Very good service. The place is clean and beautiful.

Good place for a short outing with kids. Lovely quiet environment.

reviews.text: Tucked down Thong Lo Soi 3, Angoon’s Garden delivers leafy calm in the heart of city chaos. Expect soothing garden seating, modest coffee and drinks, and a vibe of quiet intentionality. Staff are warm, ambiance relaxed and unique among Bangkok cafés. Though menu options are limited, the setting alone makes it worth the stop.

A small beautiful gemstone in the middle of Thonglor. This lovely space felt like a little haven away from the heat. Cosy setup with warm energy. The coffee was strong and had character, like a bold espresso poured into a bigger cup. Not the smoothest but full of heart. I’m giving it 4 beans because it’s a charity shop and that changes everything. Worth supporting if you’re nearby

I think they serve the best cappuccino I've had in my life.  The foam is perfect - silky and smooth.  The coffee is not bitter.  And for sugar they use liquid brown sugar. Perfect cup of coffee.  We came here everyday for 2 weeks.  Highly recommend.

This little coffee shop is located within the beautiful Angoon's Garden. The staff were very friendly and made sure my friend & I had a lovely experience here. The coffee shop also has what I would call a thrift store inside and was pretty popular with local Thai. My friend got an iced coffee and I got a honey lime soda. Both were delicious and being able to sit and enjoy them in the community garden was wonderful! To me, this was a hidden gem that I am so happy to have found and I will definitely be returning in the future.
Amazing coffee and amazing service. Also coffee is high quality yet cheaper then everywhere else. + they do charity. Go support and be smart.

A pleasant park that serves as an attractive relaxation spot.

• Atmosphere and feeling: The park is very peaceful and shady. A highlight is the soft music playing along the walking paths, making exercise or relaxation much more relaxing.

• Facilities: Parking spaces can be limited, especially during peak hours. The restrooms could also be less clean; improvements to this area would be appreciated.

• Overall: While there are some areas for improvement, the tranquil atmosphere and overall management are well done. It's a recommended place for locals looking for fresh air and exercise.

The atmosphere is nice and quiet, with music playing all along the way.
The only downsides are two things: 1. There's limited parking. 2. The track lights are dim. Sometimes the bulbs are broken, and sometimes the bulbs are still on.

reviews.text:
I visit this park regularly and genuinely enjoy spending time here. I only visit after 5 PM, when entry is free :)

However, there are a few things that could be improved.
Staff move around on scooters with exhaust fumes — since people come here for fresh air, electric vehicles would be a much better fit for the park.
Also, the entrance is paid from 9 AM to 5 PM, so it’s a bit surprising to see some worn benches, dry flower beds, and fountains that aren’t working.
The park also closes quite early :(

Overall, I really like this park and hope to see it get even better over time.

It is a very large public park in Bangkok. It’s located a bit away from the main tourist areas, so the crowd is mostly locals.

The park has a small entry fee (around 10–20 THB). Inside, the area is filled with lots of colorful flowers, themed gardens, and shaded walking paths. The large lake in the center is a highlight, and acts as a nice landmark and photo spot.

There are plenty of photo spots throughout the park whether among the flower beds or scenic corners like the Japanese-style bridge. You’ll also see people of all ages walking, jogging, or sitting under trees and having small picnics, especially near the lake areas. Around sunset, many locals come here to relax, and you will see small boat rides on the lake.

If you visit in December, there’s usually a flower festival with seasonal blooms and colorful displays.

It can feel quite far if you’re visiting Bangkok for the first time since it’s not in the main tourist zone. But if you’ve already covered most city spots, you can consider adding this. It also works well if you’re visiting nearby areas like Train Night Markets, or if you have some extra time before heading to Suvarnabhumi Airport since it’s on that side of the city.

A really beautiful place!! I would recommend spending a whole day here to be sure to see everything! Very peaceful and quiet. It does not feel like you are in a city. There are many different bird species to view or photograph. A must if you have time in Bangkok.

A perfect city escape! Suan Luang Rama IX is hands down one of the best spots in Bangkok for both fitness and relaxation.
For runners, the paved tracks are spacious, well-maintained, and surrounded by lush greenery, making your morning or evening cardio feel effortless. The vibe is incredibly motivating!
For families, it’s a dream. There’s plenty of shaded space for a picnic, swan boats for the kids, and the botanical gardens are stunning. It’s the ultimate spot for quality time away from the city noise. Highly recommended!

Such a wonderful park! Highly recommend coming here during the winter - they have some flower displays and the weather is so much cooler.

Entry fee is 10 baht per person and 10 baht per vehicle. Plenty of parking on the weekday morning, could be packed during the weekend.

The park is super spacious, with a big pond in the middle. You can walk a full circle and it wont be too tiring. Plenty of space to set up picnic spots. They also have glass house and dessert plant section.

A cute little pocket park. There are restrooms, a playground, and exercise equipment. The playground is in the shade. 👍You can't find it right away, you have to follow the map.

It's a small but well-opened park.

If you're driving, parking is difficult to find in the alley, which is not recommended.

You can walk or cycle there. If you come via Vibhavadi Soi 20, walk along the canal and there'll be a pedestrian and bicycle entrance.

It's about a 200-meter lap.

There's a playground and an adult exercise area in the park.

There are restrooms available, which are relatively clean, but some of the taps are broken.

Overall, it's good.

It's a small park, really small, but it has everything you need, like exercise equipment, a lawn, children's toys, and it's beautifully decorated and very new. There are also restrooms, but there's no parking, so it's very difficult to find a spot.

reviews.text:
Nice,Quite,Atmosphere is good ,safe and clean…
The staffs are friendly,they put the lovely smile on their faces…There have 24 hour 7/eleven ,pharmacy,Dental Care,nice and delicious restaurants,Super market and nice coffee shop ..So,you can easy to buy anything you want in one place…There have  a gym and also swimming pool on 8th floor,So you can workout in there …The BTS station is pretty close .That place is also near Suvarnabhumi Airport…

It is good condo. Nice swimming pool and nice location to airport link huamak, near to max balu etc. But the Gym is very small... There is no machine, no babell 😰 And the fron of condo, the temple song is very cloud. Another building constructire sound is noise...

First, the rooms—most of them, I mean almost every single one, have zero soundproofing. If your neighbor talks loudly, get ready to have your ears blasted every time they open their mouth.

Facilities—Gym? Always packed, typical peak-hour chaos. Pool? Decent, nothing special. They have a sauna too. Library? Forget it, always full of people basically using it as a free electricity hub.

Service—Are the staff holding a grudge against humanity, or are they just done with life? Especially the ones in the delivery office—those faces scream, “You owe me money.” Like bro, chill, we actually paid to be here. Only a few of the older ladies at reception deserve praise—great service, good manners, and a professional mindset. Thank you to them.

Lastly, the rules—Strict on paper, but let’s be real, they only seem to care when a Thai citizen complains.

Location—There’s a 7/11, nail salon, laundry, hair salon, and ATM downstairs, plus a restaurant called Yummy (food’s actually good, and prices are decent). Nearby, there’s a Max Value supermarket.

Transportation—Close to Hua Mak ARL, the Yellow Line, and the train line.

Giving 2 stars for location convenience and the few nice staff. Thanks.
Near Airportlink Huamak Station, Swimming pool, 7-11 and not far from malls on Sinakarin Road.
Near to Busy Area, can access anything easily.

You need a  bathing cap to enter but you can rent it at the shop for 20 baths with other things as bathtube and swimmingboards. I would definitive come again 👍

Good small water park for family.

It is on the top of The Mall Bangkhae, the entrace fee is 220 THB for adult and 150 THB for child. There is some restaurant inside and cold drink,beer. If you rent swimming pool costume 70 THB per person. If you wanna go outside, Staff will stamp your hand, you are allowed to enter again and again. It was a good day with my family there.

Drove for an hour to get here with our child. What a huge waste of time and a disappointment! Very difficult to find in the first place. Then had to pay 250 thb entry and had to rent a swimmingcap. Our child could only use the slides for babies cause he is under 10, although he can swim very good. Most of the slides were closed and even the baby-slides were not all open. The slides he was allowed to use, we had to rent a tube for 80 thb as well. The slides are cracked, broken off and the walls are brown. The park is not maintained very well, the dressing rooms were also very dirty. They have a lot of staff sitting around, looking at their phone. Maybe they should let them clean a bit.
Asking the full price with more than half the slides closed, doesn't feel very good.
Also, there is no food or drink on the park. You have to get fully dressed again and walk through the shopping mall to get food. We got dressed and left after an hour of laying around in a babypool.
NOT RECOMMENDED!
Very big and very enjoyable place,can spend all day here.All you need is here. 😊

A decent way to spend a few hot hours in an afternoon but nothing special.  Far more interesting for kids or parents.  Affordable, clean, often not very busy.  A large pool to lounge in and some great seating areas.  You're allowed to bring your own food and drink in which is much appreciated.  Don't get too excited about the water slides: they're very simple.

Good service, delicious food, clean bathrooms, late check-out, very customer-friendly. Will definitely come back again.
The owner takes very good care of us. I'm very impressed. He makes the most delicious drinks.
It's really great. Lots of activities, fun, and hilarious. The owner is very handsome.
No written review.

reviews.text: When you get to the parking lot, you will see the ATV track there but you need to walk to the end of the narrow street to find the ATV rental place/office. Probably calling them first that you will be going because we didn't call and just dropped by. So, no one is there. Then, we heard people yelling and arguing so loud from the house right at the corner across to this ATV track. Then, we called and saw a small sign saying where to walk to find the office. We came here in mid July which is super hot, humid and full of mosquitoes. So be prepared to spray bug spray before your ride. The ride is not cheap but not too expensive. The guy will teach you how to drive and lead you for the first round around the track. Be aware: The red big ATV is quite heavy and uses a lot of arm muscle to maneuver or turn! He will give you 6 rounds of the ride.  I thought it was little for the price we paid but oh my, after two rounds around the narrow track, my entire arms and thumbs got super sore from turning and pushing the speed button. I have a 4 yo and 9 yo with me. Both kids can sit in the back of the red big ATV comfortably. I tried to have my 9 yo drive but it's not super stable and there are ponds covered with moss and Komodo dragons! So, I didn't trust my hyper active 9 yo boy to speed at corners and fall into those ponds. They ride with adults. The place also provided helmets.

very fun experience! first time doing this with my family and would definitely come again 🙂‍↕️ shop owners were welcoming and friendly, they even taught us on how to function the bike slowly ☺️ 100% recommended!

Nice and cosy place for your ATV experience. Not a very big trail but with different obstacles (exp: uneven muddy track, pot hole covered with muddy water). Definitely a fun experience.  Staff is friendly and able to speak simple english. He teaches us how to operate an ATV before entering the track, he will guide us for the 1st round and thereafter left us alone to enjoy another 5 rounds of trail.

Came here today. Everyone is sooo friendly and kind! Spent ages chatting to the wonderful lady here before we rode round on the quad. Great fun, great price, the GREATEST people!! Lots of love, from Scotland ❤️🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿
Very fun! 9 years old kids can drive alone

reviews.text:
Marvellous park with lots of attractions.
Great for people watching and listening to the musical performances.
A feast for the senses.
Lumphini Park is highly regarded as the "green lung" of Bangkok, consistently praised in reviews for being a peaceful oasis that offers a rare escape from the city's urban chaos. Visitors love its unique wildlife, particularly the large water monitor lizards, and the variety of free recreational activities available.

Hihglights
Wildlife Observation: A major draw for many is the resident population of Asian water monitor lizards, which can often be seen sunbathing or swimming in the canals. While harmless if left alone, they are a highlight for photographers and nature enthusiasts.

Recreation & Exercise: The park features a popular 2.5km paved loop used extensively by joggers and walkers. Reviewers frequently mention the free-to-use outdoor gym equipment and organized aerobics classes that take place in the late afternoon.

Lake Activities: Visitors can rent swan-shaped paddle boats to explore the central artificial lake. Many recent reviews note that these are sometimes available for free or for a very low fee (approx. 40 Baht for 30 minutes).

Local Vibe: Early mornings are praised for sightings of locals practicing Tai Chi and yoga, providing a glimpse into authentic Bangkok community life.

Lumphini Park is a beautiful oasis right in the middle of bustling Bangkok. Surrounded by skyscrapers, the park offers a refreshing break from the city’s traffic and noise.
The large lake, shaded walking paths, and open green spaces make it perfect for morning jogs, relaxed strolls, or simply sitting and enjoying the view. Early mornings are especially peaceful, with locals practicing tai chi, jogging, and enjoying the cooler air.
One of the most unique experiences is spotting the famous monitor lizards roaming near the water — a surprising but fascinating sight! Despite being in the center of the city, the park feels calm and spacious.
Whether you want exercise, relaxation, or just a quiet moment in nature, Lumphini Park is a must-visit spot in Bangkok. It’s clean, well maintained, and completely free to enter — a true urban sanctuary.

Park like this should be in every bigger city. Super clean, lot of attractions and lot of seating and chilling areas. Monitor lizard and other interesting creatures are just like a bonus. This beautiful green park is in the middle of concrete jungle but you dont feel like it. I recommend visiting

Huge well maintained park with loads of things to do and with such a lively ecosystem! Theres tons of water monitor lizards walking freely, loads of different birds species and even stray cats just wandering around. Even found a siamese stray cat here 😆
You can also rent a bike if you dont prefer walking around. Theres rental bikes at the entrance. This green one is electric and goes fast but is pricey.
The boats and kayak are also free of charge. You can use it for around 15mins (?) which is more than enough.
Love the ambience here. Great for chilling and just hanging around. I wish we have a third space like this back in the PH ☹️

I love this park most. It's really big and calm. You can find many animals you can't see in Vietnamese parks such as some birds, crows, giant lizards,.... Plus, this park is next to MRT Silom. I recommend you fo there for walking and running before 10am or after 4pm.

reviews.text: We had a lovely time at the park. We enjoyed walking the grounds looking at the flowers and water features. The water fountain is exquisite and the ravens seemed to enjoy bathing there. There is one building that they are petitioning for renovation due to it being a historic building. It should look beautiful when completed.

Nice park. Relatively small but there's an outdoor Thai style gym for free. Nice pond. Lots of people walking and jogging here.

Much less crowded than Lumpini Park. Would come back again.

Conveniently located near Wat Po and MRT line.

Lovely green space in the big city. Walking distance to my hotel. I ended up spending my whole morning here to get away from the chaos and tourists. The monitor lizards and exotic birds kept me entertained for hours.

Tranquil Green Space located right next to the Grand Palace in Bangkok

Extremely Soothing and Calming experience

Nice Park for a Walk or a Jog or just relaxing after a Tour of the huge Grand Palace
If you want to Work Out there are two soecified areas with massive weights to lift. Locals practicing too. Dont worry the Lizzards, they will Not eat you alive though this one Had a full belly

reviews.text:
Beautiful park to enjoy an evening walk away from the heat of the day. It is very well manicured and it has several public restrooms throughout it. It is the perfect blend of nature and urban.

There were many runners this evening getting their exercise as well as couples and families picnicking in the park.

Beautiful park that is well connected to the MRT and BTS. There are also neighboring parks also connected including one with a butterfly garden. It's free to visit with free filtered water at the entrance and plenty of restrooms. The park has traffic noise but still feels peaceful and fully worth a visit.

A great quick escape from the busy city of Bangkok.

So peaceful and clean. There are areas where you can rest and think. I love how attentive the park staff are. They are also polite and answers questions.

It is big. People ride bikes here and I wished I have one, too.

I love how the animals here are thriving. They even have chipmunks here. So cute.

Will definitely comeback.

3.6 ⭐️
This park was very nice…
But very very crowded. I get why it’s so crowded though! So many people running in 90 degree heat 😮‍💨 a lot of people had some version of a marathon shirt on. Go everyone running, go! Plenty of restrooms and seats in the park. I would recommend if you’re in the area. But go on a day when it’s not as hot, and maybe during the week when it’s not as crowded.
The Thai sakura in early February is so special. I wish I had know before! What a well kept, maintained and clean park. It's beautiful and there is so much bird song. The squirrels love it too!

reviews.text: Nice place where you can escape the busy city for a while and relax under the shade of the trees. Hoses are spraying the floral in the morning to keep everything green. There are two children's play areas, one either side of the lake. Likewise two areas of exercise stations. A number of sculptures decorate the park. No dogs allowed unless registered as guide dogs for the blind. Basketball and kick volleyball courts at the far end of the site.

I used to think Bangkok only had Lumphini Park in the city center, but I was wrong! 🌳 Benchasiri Park is another gem, and you can easily access it through EmSphere Mall.

It’s a great option if you want to squeeze in a workout after work and then grab dinner nearby 🍴. I went on a weekday and the park was filled with office workers just relaxing and chilling.

The view is beautiful — there’s a lake right in the middle, and at night you can see the lights from the surrounding buildings reflecting on the water ✨.

Highly recommended for a picnic, hangout, or just a peaceful stroll. 🌿 Please remember to keep the area clean and tidy so everyone can enjoy it!

One of my favourite parks with no doubt. Eventhough it's in quite a crowded area (next to Emsphere) it's a very quiet park. There are plenty of kids playing, an arra to do exercise and stay fit, to skate and if I'm not wrong... A swimming area? (Haven't check it yet). It's a great place to go sit, read and relax.
The park is beautifully landscaped and kept in pristine condition. I enjoyed a peaceful stroll and appreciated seeing workers actively cleaning and raking dried leaves during the day.
Not too small but very good place for morning walk jog or gym. Beautiful lake and the fountain in the middle. Surrounded by beautiful high rises gives a very nice view.

reviews.text: I didnt expect this from city like Bangkok. Little busy but beautiful park. Super clean and organised. Lot of people go for a run there. Maintained gardens, trees and palm through entire park. Really relaxing place.

A great park in the middle of Bangkok with many different areas to explore. It is located near JJ/chatujak market. There is a beautiful new zone where there is a large bird watching tower where you can climb up to see birds and a great view of  the city. The bird watching tower has a minimum height requirement of 130cm so it's not suitable for kids. The new zone also have different play areas with small streams, sliders and water pumps but not everything works. There is also a resident owl which local birdwatchers like to visit and photograph. Recommended to go early mornings as there is less people and traffic especially on weekends when the market opens.

I love spending time here for the peacefulness under the tree canopies. conveniently situated relative to the Main Railway station depending on where you stay overnight. worth exploring as it has a running track on the extended part as well.

Very well maintained and diverse park that offers lots of shade. Excellent for an early morning or late afternoon stroll. The recent extension is nicely done, but has decidedly less shade. Bikes are not allowed.
Beautiful park, tons of shade to stay cool even during the heat of the day. Wildlife throughout the park. Could easily spend a half day exploring all the paths. Well kept gardens and very safe.

reviews.text:
Had very good experience i completed 22K today this is my first time here, after 5 pm pleasant wind blowing and sun set reflecting on pond surface there have separate track for cycling just for one circle distance is 2 and half kilometres.
Visitor can enjoy the sport complex and public gym.
Specially perfect for running and family and friends picnic 🧺.
There have shop each main gate ‼️ There is no locker facilities.

State Railway Park opens daily from around 5am. It is walkable from both MRT Chatuchak and BTS Mochit.

Tremendously huge park with lots of nature sightseeing. From pond to trees and sunflowers. Many types of trees all around.

You can either jog, walk, run or cycling here.

Many wild animals can be seen here too. A lot of professional and novice photographers often come here for photoshoot.

Theres sport center, court, cafes, public toilets all around.

Highly recommend.
Such a beautiful park — it really exceeded my expectations! There’s a cute coffee shop at the entrance, lovely gardens, and lots of different trees and animals. I had a really nice evening there

Another surprisingly large and wonderful park in Bangkok, easily reached by MRT. The park appeared to be full of wildlife, with many people walking and running. Helpful locals pointed me to some beautiful owls high up in the trees, seemingly unbothered by all the people photographing them.

Right across the street from Chatuchak Park is Wachirabenchathat Park, one of my favorite places for a calm walk or a run. Highly recommend it.

The park has a large lake inside and
Next to the lake, there’s also a designated area where dogs can run around freely🐶🐾🐕🐩such a happy spot to watch😘

reviews.text: Nice place! There are the palace, the temple, the restaurants and the national museums.  They are located surrounding Sanam Luang. You can get the boat across the Chaophraya river to Wat Arun.

Officially Thong Sanam Luang (Thai: ท้องสนามหลวง), is a 74.5 rai (119,200 m2) open field and public square in front of Wat Phra Kaew and the Grand Palace, Bangkok, Thailand. Sanam Luang is in the Phra Nakhon District, the historic center of Bangkok
In the Royal Chronicle, it was written that, "In front of Wat Mahathat, Sanam Luang lies between the Royal Palace and the Front Palace. When royal cremation was held at the Phra Men Ground, the pyre set up in the centre with the Royal Palace Pavilion to the south and that of the Prince of the Front Palace to the north. The music from the Royal Palace and from the Palace to the Front would be played on opposite sides of Sanam Luang".

Sanam Luang was officially known as "Thung Phra Men" (the royal cremation ground) (Thai: ทุ่งพระเมรุ). It has been used as a site for the cremation of kings, queens, and high-ranking princes since the reign of King Rama I. In 1855, King Rama IV changed its name from "Thung Phra Men" to "Thong Sanam Luang", in common usage now shortened to "Sanam Luang".

The Fine Arts Department lists Sanam Luang as a historical site.

Sanam Luang is one of the most iconic open spaces in the heart of Bangkok.
Located right next to the Grand Palace, this large green field offers a peaceful atmosphere surrounded by history, culture, and beautiful architecture.

It’s a perfect place to take a walk, relax, exercise, or simply sit and enjoy the view especially in the late afternoon when the weather cools down and the sky turns golden. You’ll see locals jogging, families spending time together, and tourists taking photos with the stunning backdrop of the Grand Palace and nearby temples.

At night, the area becomes lively yet calm, with soft lights, street food nearby, and a gentle breeze. It’s a wonderful spot to experience real Bangkok life while being surrounded by its rich heritage.

Simple, beautiful, and full of charm Sanam Luang is definitely worth a visit.
I already visited this place too many times! But I never got bored! So many interesting things to do here! Many nice buildings and architecture.
Amazing...early in the morning when opening is the best....and in the evening take tuktuk a go at least around and make pictures

reviews.text: Giant statue of King Rama 1, walking distance from the flower market and Sky park. It is a giant open area and a well maintained park, great place to relax. There is also a big fountain at the entrance of the park, but there was no water in it while I was there. However the statue is very, clean, quite, and well maintained. Definitely worth checking out if you’re in the neighborhood.

The history of Bangkok and the monarchy start the statue behind me. We take a opportunity to go there to know the history’s itself and take some wonderful pictures. You should pay a respect to go there also. Some people pray and offer that time that’s why we took pictures of it far from the statue. It was a good place to visit.

This is an impressive statue of King Rama I just in front of the bridge across the river (this bridge is also a good place where a lot of people come to watch fireworks on special occasions). If you like to explore on foot you can reach this monument from Sam Yot or Sanam Chai MRT stations. Be sure to check out the nearby temple as well.
A great memorial to a great king. The carving is very realistic.

This is a pretty average looking statue. It features a seated king Rama, calmly staring off into the distance with a sword in his lap. Located in a nice park close to the river. No entry fee. The park provides some welcome shade, but the immediate environs of the statue has no shade to speak of. Overall, it's just ok.

reviews.text: I recently visited Thonburiam Park with my friend to take a break from our studies. The park feels calm and refreshing and is clearly well maintained. There are wide walking paths, plenty of greenery and several small ponds that make the place peaceful. When we visited, people were walking, jogging or sitting quietly and enjoying the surroundings. The outdoor gym area and table tennis space are a nice bonus for those who want to stay active. There is also a free water refill station, which is very convenient. Overall, the park is easy to access and a great place to relax and clear your mind. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a quiet outdoor space.

Beautiful is all I can say. The bird chirping, the water wheels spinning, they are all part of this park’s beauty. Once in a while, I come here to walk and reflect on myself whenever I feel low. I love this place even more in April! With Padauk flower coloring the park yellow, the wind carries the scent along, which makes my mind calm and reminds me of home.

This place compliments my KMUTT student life. Even if it is a study break or a short walk home, this place is relaxing.

However, the water smell sometimes disrupt my walk and some equipments are broken. But overall, a beautiful place.

A peaceful and well-maintained green environment with shady trees, spectacular scenery, walking paths and fresh, breezy air. Its location next to KMUTT makes it convenient for university students to relax, take short walks and do light exercises to clear their minds from stress and exhaustion built up throughout the school day. Being here feels genuinely refreshing, especially after long hours of classes, as it provides a sense of escape from academic pressure. The cats wandering around the park are also approachable, and being able to pat them adds to the overall charm. However, some of the exercise equipment looks rusty and outdated, which could be improved for better safety and comfort.

Palm trees lined up throughout Thonburirom Park. Rather than forcing things in, the park feels designed to preserve nature as it is.
Wow it was genuinely beautiful. I really loved it.

The park’s design is thoughtful, too. Walking paths and running tracks are clearly separated, so everyone can enjoy the space at their own pace. It’s a great park for jogging🏃‍♀️🏃🏃‍♂️

This was my first time visiting this park, after usually just walking past it. Inside, the park was more pleasant than I expected. The trees and flowers along the paths looked vibrant, and there was no litter. The paths were divided into lanes for running and walking, there were areas for exercising and playing sports, vending machines for drinks, and clean restrooms.
I'm glad I don't see the Big Lizard anymore; that was a major reason why I hesitated to go in at first. However, I felt the pond wasn't as clean as it should be; I wish the water was clearer. Sometimes, when walking past the edge of the pond, there was still a faint, foul smell. But overall, for me, this place is perfect for relaxing or taking an evening walk for exercise.

reviews.text:
I visit this park regularly and genuinely enjoy spending time here. I only visit after 5 PM, when entry is free :)

However, there are a few things that could be improved.
Staff move around on scooters with exhaust fumes — since people come here for fresh air, electric vehicles would be a much better fit for the park.
Also, the entrance is paid from 9 AM to 5 PM, so it’s a bit surprising to see some worn benches, dry flower beds, and fountains that aren’t working.
The park also closes quite early :(

Overall, I really like this park and hope to see it get even better over time.

It is a very large public park in Bangkok. It’s located a bit away from the main tourist areas, so the crowd is mostly locals.

The park has a small entry fee (around 10–20 THB). Inside, the area is filled with lots of colorful flowers, themed gardens, and shaded walking paths. The large lake in the center is a highlight, and acts as a nice landmark and photo spot.

There are plenty of photo spots throughout the park whether among the flower beds or scenic corners like the Japanese-style bridge. You’ll also see people of all ages walking, jogging, or sitting under trees and having small picnics, especially near the lake areas. Around sunset, many locals come here to relax, and you will see small boat rides on the lake.

If you visit in December, there’s usually a flower festival with seasonal blooms and colorful displays.

It can feel quite far if you’re visiting Bangkok for the first time since it’s not in the main tourist zone. But if you’ve already covered most city spots, you can consider adding this. It also works well if you’re visiting nearby areas like Train Night Markets, or if you have some extra time before heading to Suvarnabhumi Airport since it’s on that side of the city.

A really beautiful place!! I would recommend spending a whole day here to be sure to see everything! Very peaceful and quiet. It does not feel like you are in a city. There are many different bird species to view or photograph. A must if you have time in Bangkok.

A perfect city escape! Suan Luang Rama IX is hands down one of the best spots in Bangkok for both fitness and relaxation.
For runners, the paved tracks are spacious, well-maintained, and surrounded by lush greenery, making your morning or evening cardio feel effortless. The vibe is incredibly motivating!
For families, it’s a dream. There’s plenty of shaded space for a picnic, swan boats for the kids, and the botanical gardens are stunning. It’s the ultimate spot for quality time away from the city noise. Highly recommended!

Such a wonderful park! Highly recommend coming here during the winter - they have some flower displays and the weather is so much cooler.

Entry fee is 10 baht per person and 10 baht per vehicle. Plenty of parking on the weekday morning, could be packed during the weekend.

The park is super spacious, with a big pond in the middle. You can walk a full circle and it wont be too tiring. Plenty of space to set up picnic spots. They also have glass house and dessert plant section.

reviews.text:
A surprising face of Bangkok that influencers haven't found yet. It's part of the Lung of Bangkok and it's amazing the ride a bike on the narrow alleys. It feels like people live in communion with the wild nature in this little jungle in the middle of the metropolis.

I had a really good time watching animals, birds, reptiles and chilling in the gazebo or visiting the temple or the restaurants nearby.

We ended up here randomly after deciding to take a short public ferry across the river to discover what was on the other side. It's a nice, quiet place, not very big, and there aren't any impressive flowers or anything that we could see, but still, the walkway across the water was pretty and we enjoyed a respite from the crowds. We also saw a Komodo dragon swimming in the water, which was pretty cool.😎

Nice park with paved path for walking, running and cycling. Highly suggest feeding the fish in large pond - there are some big fish in there. Park has several spots to stop and take in the beautiful scenery and watch the wildlife.

Very nice green park close to the centre of Bangkok. I took a Grab from Sukhumvit very early in the morning and arrived at the park around 7:00 a.m. I walked around, spotted some lovely birds, and met friendly people. There are toilets and a nice café that serves good coffee.
To get back, we walked to one of the ferries and found another Grab on the other side. You can also reach the MRT within walking distance from the ferry point.

This is a great park, offering a calm and well-maintained environment that encourages relaxation and appreciation of the surroundings. The space is thoughtfully organized, clean, and welcoming, making it suitable for both short visits and longer stays.

Overall, it provides strong value as a recreational area and leaves a very positive impression, making it a place well worth revisiting and recommending.

reviews.text: It was a beautiful day and a nice walk in this park. Not sure if dogs were allowed here, I couldn’t find any signs. People in the park was either taking a run or taking photos.
Places is good for jogging at the same time ur kids can enjoy too . Love the environment. They maintain the places clean etc .. me and my husband went for jogging

Although rather small comparatively, this public park has what it takes for a family to get their daily exercise without leaving their neighborhood. A jogging track around a lake, children's playground (a bit shabby), fitness stations, basketball, takraw & footsol courts. If one has more time, though, it's advisable to go a bit furthur to the King Rama IX Park.
Nice park with kids playground. Truly heaven for the community. It is closed 9 pm which is great for exercise after school or work. The running track is longer than สวนวนธรรม.

Good place for family for a little gym and playground for your kids even a bit small probly because so much people in this area go to this park.. and no much place to park anyway is lovely  park half of it a lake have a fish very big and turtle that you can give a snack to him at sucurity they sale a food to feet like bread for 10 baht or kind of snack from corn and pillet fish food....

Is a very nice park for the neighborhood here, many tree to keep it very cosy and windy also. Not sure why, but this is what it feel here.
While is nice for those is doing usual activity here.
My activity coming here for the photoshoot. Bring my model here, and is quite peaceful and nice to take photo. Is a good choice for taking photo, but my group is small with us only 2 photographer and 1 model.
Entrance is free.
Nice park, good for running and walking 😊 even biking 🚴. There are a lot of big trees 🌳 , the total round is around 2 km.

Lush green forest park, was there around 8am for run and the sun was blazing everywhere around but inside the park it was so so cool. The trails are mostly under shades. It was 2km long per round and its totally worth a visit.
Beautiful Park, peaceful and picturesque. There is an outdoor gym as well as a children's play area. Not sure how long the path is to run but I would no problem. This is a worthwhile park.

reviews.text:
This park is only 1.5 km away from my hotel. It’s clean and beautiful, with peach blossoms everywhere, covering the ground like a pink carpet. Watching the flowers makes me feel calm and peaceful. Some of the trees are even shaped into different animals — it’s incredible to see.

A lovely place to relax and enjoy nature. 🌿

Great park, lots of greenery and nice designs by the gardeners. It’s clean no rubbish with lots of bins around and there is heaps of pathways to explore. I appreciate the ambient sounds of the birds… great if you want to meditate, catch up with friends or bring family.

The biggest pro is the natural shading from the tall trees so you can find literally anywhere to relax.
Nice place to spot monitor lizards and for the children to enjoy the play area. We saw a library and a pool but we are not sure how to access them as they seem closed at 8 am.
So pretty and clean, perfect getaway from the busy streets and traffic. So well maintained and not a lot of people!
Great park to visit if you are staying in the area. There are nice walking paths and outdoor exercise area. We even saw a few monitor lizards that are about 1 m long.

Beautiful and well maintained park! I loved it 😍
I came here for fly with my drone. It wasl ittle bit windy but this park is very smooth for chilling and good for jogging or walking.

Lovely & peaceful park close to paradise park.
I've just been here first time, there are place for picnic also love it.
Small but beautiful landscape.

Parking for free but limited as it’s not a big place

Come before 5pm and you will have parking small park

One round is 1km to walk went few times
Very good park especially if you have children. The area is large, lots of tree. Great playground.

Not too big But very beautiful park to do walking, cycling and running.

Kind playground,  adult exercise equipment, Green labrary for free..

Circle around the pond and beautiful flower blossoms make good  atmosphere .

4gates to access to the park and also have parking lots.

Great little park, not too crowded.
Lots of turtles and a nice kids playground with sand and a variety of cmimbing activities (can get crowded and kids get rough). They have a bike lane, basketball courts and an elder exercise area. Some nearby food options.
Parking is not too easy, better come with motorbike. Toilets don t smell so much.
We had 4 hours between our flight and our train and nothing to do, so we went here! A very nice park. Walking trails, 2 playgrounds, and wild turtles! A great time

reviews.text: Lots of gym equipment and kids playground.　A place to relax and feed the fish from 20B. Bicycles are not allowed. It is right outside the station. I will come back for relaxation~!

It’s a park for everybody! Kids can play at the playground, everyone can exercise at the track, old people can use the exercise machines, & everyone can relax at the benches while enjoying the scenery or even feed some animals. The animals like birds & turtles roam freely around as well. It’s such a nice place to exercise & relax.💯

A very Very big park in the middle of the city. Running track, water pond, garden, birds, exercising equipments, clean and fresh air makes it unique to be there in evening. Playground area with sliding and other equipments is available for children. Parking space is ample. Have seen many people post after work hours running jogging and exercising there. Well connected by road, sky train. Just beside the a tesco lotus and esplanade mall. Good place to take kids for weekend.
The jogging track is about 1km, it has a beautiful lake and sitting areas. If you’re around and want to have a fresh breath, do come here. Accessible by BTS and bus.

It's a nice park to spend some time at, the parhs are rubber so its soft on your feet.
Big pond in the middle of the park with bridges going over so you can look at the fish and birds.

This is a good place for outdoor exercise, running and walking
Good facility such as patking lots, toilets.

Shady atmosphere with big trees around the park, it is a nice place for relaxation and exercises also got the playground for children. There is a restaurant with great seafood and Thai food. The park have been organized the events from time to time such as OTOP products, food festival, Loy Krathong festival etc. Normally it has a lot of people on weekend especially on Sunday because of workers from country side and neighbouring countries.
The environment is good. There are a lot of rest room, vending machine but no food store around here in the morning.
big tree and large pond , fresh air for exercise in the morning.  You can walk ,run , Taichi  etc. with comfortable parking .

Not many big trees but enough, beautiful flowers,vegetable grown and herbs. If you stay in Rajavithi or Rangnam area, this is a place to relax and joy😊 Entrance: Rajavithi road and Soi Rangnam. Open daily 5 am till 9 pm.

This park is very beautiful and relaxing. There is a playground. Lots of people walk or run along the circular path. There are monitor lizards basking in the sun. Look up and you may see a flying squirrel.

A beautiful relaxing time spent with friends walking and visiting. The gardens are well maintained. There is a security monitor at the entrance.
Some people use the paths as their workout going around the park more than once!
Beautiful flowers and potted plants adorn the walk way!

Only BMA buses and Songthaew trucks serve as public transportation to this location. The finest recommended mode of transportation is a personal vehicle. In addition to the playground, there is a jogging track and gym apparatus. Highly recommended for those who prefer exercise space, as it is not overly crowded even in the evening or after work.

A good place for exercise in the evening.

You can jogging in this park (estimate 800 m / 1 round). Some parent and their kids do some activities in the weekend.

Quite nice and shady . There are a few courts for different sports like basketball , football.   Also has A play ground for kids. I loved the huge trees covering the park . It's like a little oasis in the centre of very busy roads!
Beautiful park for exercise. Beatiful bathroom. Have many different sport including basketball, takraw, even badminton. The only bad thing is badminton court is old and a little bit windy sometimes.

reviews.text: Plenty of Reptile

There are two entrances, one directly opposite Thanommitr Market and the other on the Land Department side. It's suitable for a walk or light exercise, but not ideal for serious running as the path is sloping and uneven. There are places to feed the fish and turtles, but no children's playground. Plenty of parking is available.

There are two entrances to the park; you can enter from either side. One is opposite Thanommitr Market, and the other is next to Thanommitr Condominium near the Land Department office. The evenings are pleasant, the atmosphere is shady and relaxing, with the sounds of birds, trees, and flowers. Some of the paths are slightly sloping, so it's recommended to wear sneakers for a walk.
The atmosphere is nice, but the place needs improvement. It's overgrown, and some of the playground equipment is broken. The running paths should also be smoothed out to prevent accidents.
It's a great place to take a walk; the atmosphere is wonderful.

reviews.text: Calm and peaceful. Good for a Sunday morning walk at the park.

Super small and quiet park at Siam.
Not a lot of places to walk, but also it is not really crowded, even on the weekend.
Nice trees at the entrance.
Some sitting is available in multiple locations.
Also there is an Amazon Cafe right in the park, so you can get coffee or something for your visit.
Also a nice shrine is located on the front side.
One can escape the madness of Siam square and recharge in this idyllic “pocket park”. Much needed break to stay sane in the hustle and bustle of Bangkok.

While exploring the Siam shopping district, I came across a delightful little haven called Park@Siam. Just a quick five-minute walk from the modern marvel of SIAMSCAPE, this park is a perfect spot to rest your feet and recharge.

Though one of the smaller parks in Bangkok, Park@Siam offers a charming and well-maintained escape from the city's hustle. The park's design is simple yet inviting, with manicured lawns, shaded pathways, and ample seating areas. The greenery provides a refreshing contrast to the concrete jungle surrounding it.

One of the park's greatest assets is its proximity to the larger mall's in Siam. After spending hours shopping and exploring the high-energy environment of Siam. I found Park@Siam to be a welcoming retreat. The short walk is convenient, making it easy to transition from the bustling shopping areas to this peaceful oasis.

Upon entering Park@Siam, I immediately felt a sense of calm. It's the perfect place to take a break from the busy streets and relax. I found a comfortable bench under a tree and enjoyed the serene atmosphere. Despite its size, the park offers plenty of spots to sit and unwind. The gentle breeze and the sounds of nature provided a much-needed respite.

The park’s atmosphere is wonderfully tranquil. It's popular among both locals and tourists, yet it never feels overcrowded. The blend of natural beauty and quiet corners creates an ideal environment for relaxation. Whether you’re people-watching, reading a book, or simply enjoying the peace and quiet, Park@Siam delivers a soothing experience.

If you’re exploring the shopping areas of Siam and need a quick break, Park@Siam is a must-visit. Its convenient location makes it an easy stop, and its serene setting offers a perfect escape from the city's pace. Whether you need a moment to rest or just want to enjoy some green space, Park@Siam is the ideal spot. Highly recommended for anyone needing a little respite in the heart of Bangkok!

This is a small park located in the back lane of Siam square area. Inside the park there is only one Amazon Coffee shop and a water body filled with Koi fish and trees. It's like a breath of fresh air in the busy Shopping area.

reviews.text: Fantastic Park area. There's lots of little animals running around as well which is nice some peacocks and monitor lizards. And I was the only westerner I saw here so it's a good little place if you want to check out a local walking spot.

Even though the pier was flooded when I visited today, on November 26, 2025, the temple sanctuary remained as shady and beautiful as ever. The serene atmosphere persists, and people continue to gather there for their daily exercise.

Love this park. It's not big, but is very beautifully landscaped and a wonderful place to walk around for an hour or two. The traditional style buildings are stunning, there's some hydraulic gym equipment, and also some peafowl that roam around near the car park.

Wonderful place to visit. Beautifully kept with lovely trees and flowers. The walk along the Chao Phraya river is very interesting. Lots of fish to feed including some huge Chao Phraya catfish estimated weight up to 30 kilos each. I'mGreat views of the bridge. For those interested it is easy to follow the path out of the park into another car park where Nonthaburi Street Art is on the walls.  Follow the road out and you will see a silver turnstile leading to the stairs leading to the pavement either side of the bridge, out one side and back the other side if you want. The views from the bridge are amazing upstream and downstream . A dream location for keen photographers. Lots of barges and other boats. I think this is one of the best places to visit in Bangkok. Also a famous Wat is next door. Very hard to see it all in one visit. Remember to buy food by the entrance to feed the fish, and fruit or vegetables for the turtles by the entrance. I think bananas are one of their favourite foods, with lettuce a healthy alternative. Many thanks to all the staff who work hard to keep this park. There is no smoking allowed and I have not seen any litter.

A serene riverside park, perfect for a peaceful escape. Stunning views of a beautiful bridge and Thai architecture especially during sunsets. Multiple sections with charming canals and local Thai plantations Well-maintained and almost never too crowded. Ample parking space available, but access can be tricky without a car.

reviews.text: Santichaiprakarn Park (Thai: สวนสันติชัยปราการ, RTGS: Suan Santi Chai Prakan, pronounced [sǔan sǎn.tìʔ t͡ɕʰāj prāː.kāːn]) is a small urban park by the Chao Phraya River within the grounds of Phra Sumen Fort on Phra Athit Road. Overlooking the river, the park is set in an ancient area of the city and has a long history of its own. Shaded by a mix of small and large trees, it serves as a venue for many events and activities such as aerobics, Tai chi dance, chain spinning, and juggling. In the evenings, both local residents and travelers staying near Khaosan Road enjoy spending time here.

Public spaces are the precious right of people specially the poor. In Thailand good food is not necessarily  in expensive restaurants  but in streets. You bur the food in the street and you arrive in Parks like Santi Chai Prakan stay by River eat and drink (but no alcohol). You have company of Koyal singing from trees, boats sailing in River, good music being played for free by amateurs. I salute this Thai sense of being near Nature and enabling even the poor greater joys through public spaces than the rich who pay to have such serene spaces.
Love you Thailand!
Visited in June 2025. It is a small park beside the river-side and near to Khao San Road. Can get nice view of the Rama VIII bridge. The park is well-maintained and really nice.

A small park, yet well worth a visit with epic views of the Rama VIII bridge, day and night. Watch the boats on the river while relaxing on a bench in the shade. Well cared for lawn and landscapes. Security cameras and personnel make this a very safe park. The temple can only be viewed from the outside. There are restrooms to the right of the fort.

really enjoy the cozy vibe，u can immerse yourself in the meditation and resonate with the rhythm of nature,like the freeze,river,trees,birds,squirrels……and let the conversations with strangers happen spontaneously

reviews.text: Very nice well maintained park sitting along the waterside. There are many walking paths and a running path. Lots of trees and foliage. Plenty of benches and places to sit. Outdoor gym area, kinds play area, bathroom facilities, small ponds etc.
A small but well maintained park in Bangkok. Great for jogging and children. 1 round is 850m.

The park may not be very large, but its landscape is truly stunning. Part of the park lies along the scenic banks of the Chao Phraya River, offering breathtaking views, especially during the chilly winter sunsets. This place is perfect for nurturing both your physical and mental well-being. For photography enthusiasts, it’s a must-visit spot you won’t want to miss!

All bathrooms are wheelchair accessible.

Small park but well organized. It can get pretty crowded. Great for a little run or to get a bit of green. Would not go out of my way to go here again but not many other parks in this part of bkk.
Great view but sad at the state of the river :(

It's such a nice place to unwind and relax. The park is well kept and there are a lot of benches lining up, inviting you to sit down under a big tree. Right next to the river, the atmosphere is tranquil and the view is beautiful. I enjoyed the tropical landscaping with native plants and trees. There's also a vegetable gardening project in the park. I got here by BRT bus. The station is called "Rama IX Bridge" and it's about a 5 min walk from the park entry (bus ticket was only 15 baht  from Sathon).

reviews.text: This restaurant offers a delightful combination of delicious and health-conscious salads paired with perfectly cooked steaks. The service is impressively fast and efficient, ensuring a smooth dining experience. However, seating is limited, so arriving early or planning ahead is advisable. A great spot for a wholesome and satisfying meal!
Good food with fresh vegetables. Plate size is good. One salad can eat 2-3 people.Good for your health. Shop size is a bit tiny but it os ok if you come with 3 or 4 people.

The menu of the shop is diverse. Fresh vegetables are served in a beautiful way. There are options to choose from many varieties. And all the ingredients look bright and healthy Making vegetables have a rich and delicious taste. In addition, there is an option to add protein from meat or truffles or soybeans. Making it suitable for everyone, the service here is good and friendly. The staff is happy to help to choose the right item. Fast in food service And the cleanliness here is one of the highlights that makes me want to come back again This is satisfactory for people who want good health and delicious taste in the Salad Factory.
Good price, huge portion. Food tastes amazing here...recommended!!

reviews.text:
This mall is a great place to spend a few relaxed hours. There are plenty of good restaurants and coffee shops, making it easy to find something for everyone.

One of the highlights is the large, safe outdoor public area with a spacious lawn where kids can play freely. It feels open, clean, and family-friendly. There are also many shops to browse, so there’s always something to do.

It’s an ideal spot for a half-day visit, especially for families looking for an easy and enjoyable stop before heading to the airport.

I’ve posted about Town Square before over summer but this is Christmas time! I absolutely love this area, still amazing and great place to go OFF STRIP. Many great restaurants, cute play areas for kids, there’s a fun jewelry making store, there’s even babysitting! You can take Painting classes @ Pinots Palette or escape the escape rooms @ escapology… It’s beautiful, and has something for everyone :)
Very cool place with everything you could want from shopping, to dining, coffee and deserts.

Little area within the town where complex! One of the corner feature playground. The majority of the area is built with chairs and artificial turf. Restaurant music playing. Lots of restaurants around the perimeter. Bathrooms and drinking fountains easily accessible!

Small gated playground with security. It's is really nice and safe playground. Bathroom is just next to it, which is great. They also have a big open grass area for kids to run. They have coffee shop, aunt Annie,pizza and potato. My daughter likes the noodles restaurant there.
Last night we were there to watch Christmas lighting and parade. It was beautiful. They were also giving candies to kids.

reviews.text: Parking is not many you have to park side of the road. Well-landscaped design, beautiful sunset and view from the bank of the river as well as the lit-up bridge. It’s Rama VIII bridge. There is connecting walkway to Gita Rajanagarindra building where is music school located on. There are music in the park events on some weekend. Love it!

Tired of boring runs? Come here for the ultimate cat show! Watch them climb and sleep in trees, perform high jumps, or simply show off their fluffy bellies to distract all the runners.

Love this place 😍

It's a nice green area to walk around and it also has lots of space for activities on the concrete. There are a few covered areas by the water where you can sit and be out of the sun or rain. Plenty of motorbike parking and there are street food vendors around too.

A very lovely park with a nice track for walking and running. You can get to the base of the huge bridge and hangout next to the river without being blasted by the sun. There’s also a smaller park area with everything you normally get at Thai parks (workout equipment, jungle gym, tables, benches, pavilions, etc).

The cherry on top is the historical significance of the area and the presence of the 8th king memorialized in statue form.

I rode here by bike and I’d come here again!
Coincidentally see an amazing light show with beautiful music. Many people running or exercise in the park. Good vibe!

reviews.text:
Some places remain quiet not because they lack beauty, but because they are lovingly protected by silence.
Just before reaching the HappyLand Center at Bang Kapi, there is a lakeside garden that even many locals rarely speak of.
A simple circular walk… yet deeply calming.
What moved me most was not only the cleanliness,
but the care behind it. More than ten workers quietly tending flowers, trees, grasses — nourishing life, sweeping paths, preserving peace. Their unseen dedication is what makes this place breathe.
Across the lake, a row of European-styled buildings appears — restaurants, a lighthouse, residences, and structures with stories untold.
For a moment, it creates a gentle illusion:
as if no flight was needed to feel far abroad.
Sometimes, the most meaningful journeys are found at familiar junctions — when we slow down enough to notice.
~ JPtravel Without Flying 2026
Good place for walking, running and exercise workout. There have not only 2 km walk way but also specific workout area like Aerobic ground, Taichi ground.

Good for walking exercise and jogging.  Each lap is 2 km.   The track is mostly shady due to the tall trees lined alongside it.  Restrooms are readily accessible.  You just have to find a parking spot near this park.

IG: adell.travel
This park is spacious and clean, many point to bottles recycle. A lot of kids playing and professionals are running here.

Awesome experience walking and running here. Views are superb with big pond at center. This is the big park that you can come for exercise, relax or picnic.
It's free intrance, open 5 am - 9 pm. Just one thing that inconvenience is no proper parking lots provided. Many people park along footpath of Siburapha road which has no parking sign along the way. Or you may try find some parking space in soi across that road.

The person who designed the basketball and running trail needs a brain scan.

Why would someone put a kerb right outside of a basketball court and between 2 running trails, and it is covered by the white marking line!!!

The court itself is fine. But I twisted my ankle by trying to save a ball and stepped into the kerb!!! It is 3-5cm height!!! It is ridiculous

For runners, the park offers about one-km strecth of red and grey lanes amidst highways and noises. The inner part should be more quiet when the traffic is light. Standardized basketball court and decent workout equipment suitable for beginners are provided. And quite surprisingly, the restroom is clean.

We played basketball here. It was a place. Outdoor gym, kids playgroud and a jog track. Parking is quite limited. However if you arrive like 8am onwards, pollution starts to kick in as it is surrounded with main road.
Still on renovated the Garden.. Not very good for running but well.. it's a closest place around here...
So good

Somdet Saranrat Maneerom Public Park offers a green oasis amidst the urban landscape, providing a peaceful retreat for locals and visitors alike 🌳🏞️. The park features well-maintained gardens, walking paths, and recreational areas, creating a serene environment for relaxation and exercise 🚶‍♀️🧘‍♂️. You can expect to see families picnicking, joggers running, and people simply enjoying the fresh air and greenery 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦🍃. The park is a welcome escape from the hustle and bustle of city life, offering a moment of tranquility and calm 😌✨.

The park's design often incorporates elements of Thai landscaping, with beautiful flowers, shady trees, and sometimes even small ponds or fountains 🌸⛲. You'll find benches and seating areas scattered throughout the park, providing opportunities to sit and enjoy the surroundings 😊👍. It's a great place to take a leisurely stroll, read a book, or simply watch the world go by 📖🚶‍♂️. However, let's be real, sometimes a public park can feel a bit like a social experiment in human behavior – you might encounter everything from enthusiastic dog walkers to impromptu yoga sessions, and the occasional person practicing their flute skills at full volume 🐶🧘‍♀️🎶.
Decent skatepark with a smooth floor. Some of the ramps are old and broken, but the ledges and rails are still very skateable. Lit up at night too.

reviews.text: For such a small park there's a lot of activities going on. The park is under the highway bridge right next to a river, giving the park lots of protection from rain and keeping the park cooler with lots of shade. A short jogging, walking track is made from rubber adding some cushion. There is an exercise area with weights, basketball courts, soccer court, badminton, volleyball, muay thai ring, and takraw. Children's play area, and restrooms. Car park.

A clean and  pleasant medium sized park. The park is filled with walking/jogging paths and basketball, volleyball, soccer, and tennis courts (all in playable condition). The park sits on the river and you can sit on the wall and watch the water or walk along by the boats. There are stairs down to the park at the end of the bridge.

Great place to go visit if you want to experience how the locals socialise and exercising. It’s a small little green area in the middle of a busy area of the city and once you find yourself here you won’t hear traffic and the loud noise Bangkok provides. The vibe is friendly and as a foreigner you can easily get invited to take part of the locals different sports activities

Best park but watch out during morning time have open karaoke very loud and after work hour there open loud music for exercise. Other then that love the Big tree and good wind feel at peace here cause of greenary. If it rain don't worry cause of bridge overhead is like roof.

A cute little park. The whole park is covered by trees or the bridge so you always have shades and are covered in case of rain. The running track is rather small. 480m. But you can exit the park and run some sois to bring some variety to your run.

A lot of Thai kids use the park for ball games. There are also aerobic classes like in most other Thai parks.
