reviews.text:
Nice Park to visit.
It was a bit too chaotic for me as a school trip was visiting but should be a tranquil place to visit.

Though it's a memorial park related to King Rama II and is the birthplace King Rama II used to be born here in the past more than two centuries ago, it has nothing much to do with the keepsakes of King Rama II.

The buildings seem to be newly built,with waxworks demonstrating what the culture and lifestyle used to be like during the childhood period of King Rama II and during the reign of King Rama II.
It also has a small museum explicating the history of Samut Songkhram.

Not really an antique museum like Bangkok National Museum.
The 60 baht entry fee I think is very  reasonable as the place is well maintained. Clean and tidy.

Museums and Thai history is a big part of their culture. This museum, which was the home of Rama II, is kept in pristine condition. The boats are original while the fruits aren't. The spoon looking thing out of the rabbit's mouth is what they used to scrape the coconut flesh out of the coconut shell. Very interesting indeed. It's a lovely day out.

It is a spacious park with Thai traditional buildings, statues, ponds, trees and flower gardens. It was a pleasant experience to see these unique Thai architectures.
The atmosphere was very peaceful. I visited during the day where the sun was bright, and it was blazing hot. If you travel during day time, you may have to bring an umbrella and wear thin casual clothes.
Entry fee: 20 Baht per person.
Time of travel: September 2018.

This is a very clean and well kept park with some cultural attractions and a riverfront rest area. Only 30 baht for adults and free for kids it's a good deal. The toilets are vert clean. You can see lotus ponds, orchids, gardens and traditional Thai wooden houses. These contain displays of how traditional desserts are made. You can also rent a bike for 40 baht and cycle around.  There is a pleasant shaded rest area overlooking the river which gets a nice breeze. Highly recommend.

reviews.text: A very nice small park with a beautiful modern building in the centre. Around it there are numerous water lilies and small islands on which various trees and plants have been planted. The highlight is this wonderful footbridge through which you can get from one side of the park to the other. There is also a small playground for children. If you are in the area and want to relax, this place is very special.
Beautiful, quiet and peaceful. This is a great greenspace, for a respite from the urban jungle!

Small park beautiful ambiance with lake in middle can feed the fish & turtles. Good place for jog but small have kids playground. Surrounding full of trees and good wind. Small space for parking so come abit early to park cars otherwise come by public transportation or taxi.

A great, calming and green workout space. This is easily one of the best parks in Bangkok. The runway is clean and supporting. There are some workout equipments provided, but don't get your hopes up. There are enough parking lot for your car and bike. After workout, you can have a decent meal in many restaurants nearby and it's cheap.
My running session was calm and effecrive since there were not much people blocking the way. I will definitely go there again. Love it!
This was very nice park to jogging with family and this place has flesh air which is found very hard in Bangkok.

reviews.text: The park is very small. There is a net for playing sports like badminton. The track going around is about 400 - 500m, so quite small. There is also an outside gym, football area and a shop to buy snacks. Good for if you need something close by and quite.

Little park in the middle of highway. It’s located in front of the mall. Small running track, basketball court and Pétanque.
Overall is ok but the gardening can be better. The number of tree around the park  couldn’t filter all smell of the pollution from the highway.
There is car park available but need to look at the entrance carefully.
good place to exercis
Exercise place right in front of The mall. The place is old and scary but good enough for exercise. There are exercises machine, football and takrov court.
This place is good for running and relaxing yourself have a exercise machine and free weights around park.

reviews.text: A tiny park in front of a government building and the river where the boats dock. In front of the docks are 2 staircases to cross the river. Later in the morning the government building opens its gates and allows a peaceful walk or jog in their garden. The park is clean but in need of a little maintenance. The train station and ticket office are up the stairs to the right when you enter the park. At the base of the stairs are 2  kittens which live in the bushes and they're always hungry (brings food packets from the 7-11).

A Calm Riverside Escape in the Heart of Bangkok

Saphan Taksin Park is one of those hidden gems in Bangkok that surprises you with its peaceful atmosphere despite being right in the middle of a busy urban zone. Located near Saphan Taksin BTS Station and overlooking the Chao Phraya River, the park offers a refreshing break where you can slow down, breathe, and enjoy the city from a quieter angle. It’s small compared to some of the larger parks in Bangkok, but what it lacks in size, it makes up for with charm and tranquility.

The riverside walkway is the highlight, offering beautiful views of boats gliding across the water, the sunset reflecting off nearby buildings, and the overall rhythm of river life in Bangkok. Whether you're visiting in the early morning or during the golden hour in the evening, the scenery feels soothing and picture-perfect. The park is clean, well-maintained, and ideal for a short walk, light exercise, photography, or simply sitting on a bench and watching the world go by.

Because it’s not overcrowded, the environment feels peaceful and safe. It’s a great place for solo travelers needing a quiet moment, couples taking a casual stroll, or anyone wanting to enjoy nature without leaving the city center. The proximity to the BTS and river ferry makes it very accessible too.

Overall, Saphan Taksin Park may not be a major tourist attraction, but it delivers something equally valuable a calm, scenic escape where the beauty of Bangkok’s riverside comes alive in the simplest and most relaxing way.

Easy to find and navigate to a variety of destinations along the blue line and interchange where needed. Also, only a few meters from the pier to experience the local ferry, the tourist boat across the river and to popular malls. Don't forget the long tail boat tour for an intimate view of local living and to see the Big Buddha 😀
People come and dance. Most enjoy with other s. Eat drink and dance. Make good communication with everyone.
Very convenience to go Sukumvit from Charon krung area.

reviews.text: The learning garden has many benchs, seat, and work place. Areas suitable for activities usually have shade from trees. This garden features a variety of trees, both flowering and deciduous. There are seating areas overlooking a pond with fountains and waterwheels, creating the constant sound of flowing water. Smaller fountains and aquatic plants are also planted throughout the garden. Even though it's a garden, it has strong Wi-Fi. On days with pleasant weather, it's ideal for working, reading, or engaging in activities that require concentration. If your brain is stressed, seeing, hearing, or touching a natural environment can help relieve stress. The atmosphere in the morning and evening is perfect for sitting around and having conversations. I really enjoyed doing the activities there in the morning because it was very relaxing. However, I think that even with the shade from the trees, it's not ideal for activities at midday or in the afternoon because the very hot sun in Thailand can disrupt concentration, making it difficult to look at electronic device screens, and also causing discomfort. In the evening and at dusk, there are a lot of mosquitoes, which are very annoying. However, I still like this place.
Good for let's chilling and take a photo
Beautiful view, but it would be better if there was a roof over the seating area.
Beautiful place, atmosphere conducive to learning.

reviews.text: Thankyou

The security guard was extremely rude. He spoke without any polite language, was rude, and extremely rude. I don't know if the district office will see this comment, as I've seen other people talking about this guard, but he still behaves the same way.

People in this profession should have a better service mindset. If you can't change his behavior, I recommend replacing him.

The security guard had terrible manners, was rude, and had a negative mindset. On Sunday, I was driving near Soi Phetkasem 77, following GPS directions, and got lost. I almost reached the soi entrance, so I turned around to find my way out. I saw the gate was open. An elderly security guard yelled, "What are you doing here?" and before I could even finish speaking, continued, "This isn't a U-turn area. Back off!" He yelled it all out. I apologized repeatedly, explaining that the GPS had led me astray. The security guard looked like the world was ending, staring at me intensely with a hard, unyielding gaze, then shook his head. A security guard should have a much better mindset. People are lost; otherwise, why would they go in there? He doesn't even need to be overly polite, but such rude behavior is unacceptable for a security guard. He should be more open-minded, helpful, or at least empathetic towards those who are lost. Honestly, I was very disappointed with this rude and untrained security guard. It's strange that someone with such a mindset is hired.

The security guard who looked older spoke very rudely. He spoke in a harsh and abrupt tone. He should improve his attitude. If someone spoke like this to work, it would make people who used the service feel bored and unsafe all the time.

reviews.text: This small park has some treeless patches, so the best times to visit are morning or evening. Given the surrounding urban development, it feels a bit disappointing you’d expect more greenery in this city spot.

• A wonderful small public park in the city, thoughtfully designed to give off natural and refreshing energy.

• The wide green lawn, tall trees, and clean paths make it feel like a park you’d find in Singapore. It’s peaceful, well-maintained, and a great escape from the busy city.

• Highly recommended if you’re looking for a calm, natural spot in the middle of Bangkok.

Slightly elevated park, with views, large lawn area. Small pond with wooden bridge. Stone rockery with water feature on both sides. Mostly surrounded by mature trees and shrubs. A few benches in the shade. The art center building is currently empty at this time, under renovation as of May 27 2025. Restrooms are still open.

Outdated, old mindset. Why so wrong it’s just a little dog on a leash..The park is like other parks in Thailand. Seemed it‘s a pet friendly park. But it‘s not.
Restricted a dog must be walked only the area at the back. Many homeless, the area is unclean.

An excellent public park near Chulalongkorn University campus 🌳. The area is very well maintained, with neatly trimmed trees and grass 🌿✨. It’s clean, safe, and quite spacious 🧺.

People come here to exercise, run, or just relax 🏃‍♂️☀️. It’s a great spot for picnics or resting after errands or sightseeing around Siam 🛍️🍃. University students often use it for group activities as well 🎓👥. The atmosphere is especially wonderful on nice weather days ☀️😊.
There is also a small art gallery located within the park 🎨🏛️, adding a cultural touch to the relaxing environment.

‘Not much to see there!’ said the driver. Perfect for me. It is a charming park beneath an overpass. It has got basketball courts and some other kind of volleyball court. It is a cool place to walk and sit near the Chaopraya river. There is a nice Hainan Chicken and rice place opposite.
Still need to renovate the ground many hole on the ground. Floor is uneven. Too many garbage at the gate before entering the park.
I accidentally found a treasure playground in Bangkok, and I happened to meet a group of lovely students and played an interesting football match with them.
The park is closing down the jogging area for MRT subway-related construction work which will last several years.

reviews.text: I've encountered many troublesome neighbors. The company managing the project handles complaints without verifying evidence, constantly spreading rumors and issuing warnings. The management company doesn't check evidence or security camera footage; they just accept any complaint that's raised. When they wanted to sell, they could do whatever they wanted. After the sales started, they created numerous rules and prohibitions. Residents have to deal with these neurotic neighbors. The project isn't even fully sold yet, and they haven't established a management company; it's still managed by a company. House prices have dropped significantly. If you prefer peace and quiet, I suggest you move away. Even though the village has few residents and seems deserted, the neighbors are incredibly troublesome because the company doesn't manage things properly and just blindly follows anyone's orders.

reviews.authorName: Zinkan 888 ไลน์ป๊านามบัตรสีฟ้า
reviews.text:
The village is comfortable, quiet, and not noisy. Security is strict. There are plenty of food options in front of the village, including several 7-Elevens and a fresh market.

It's only 500 meters from Triam Udom Suksa School.

There's a Tops supermarket, Lotus DIY, and a hospital nearby. The clubhouse and swimming pool are a bit small.

reviews.text: As a tourist your free to walk through the grounds of the University, the path following the river has good views and lots of seating. A good place to grab some food and drink from the university food hall. It's a nice place to relax under shaded trees to cool off.

One of the most well respected university in Thailand. There's several Thammasat campus around Thailand, however the Thapra Chan is the original campus of Thamnadat university. The university is well equipped with learning facilities and all kinds of activities for the students to participate in.
Licationwise it's unbeatable,  just pray that you don't drive as a student, or you'll be wishing you are living in other places.

Thammasat University is a public research university in Thailand with campuses in Tha Phra Chan, Rangsit, Pattaya and Lampang Province. As of 2024, Thammasat University has over 39,000 students enrolled in 33 faculties, colleges, institutes and 2,000 academic staff. Thammasat is Thailand's second oldest university.

the second oldest and most prestigous university in Thailand, Thammasat University has a long and proud history of activism and academia. the Tha Prachan campus on the banks of  the Chao Phraya river near the Grand Palace is lovely. the main campus is Rangsit which is on the northern outskirts of Bangkok

reviews.authorName: ดวง_ พาตะลอน
reviews.text:
The day we went, it was almost dark in the evening, and the sky was overcast, looking like it was going to rain, but it didn't.

We went on a high tide day, and the water along the dam was at its peak. The view was beautiful as usual, but there was no sunset, as the clouds covered it, creating a different kind of beauty.

I heard the dam is being renovated. I'm looking forward to seeing it. I've been wanting to see it renovated for a long time. It's a famous landmark in Samut Prakan, and it should be renovated to make it a nice place to walk.

reviews.text:
Nice quiet park! Not many people, cozy atmosphere, nice view to the nearest condos. Minuses:
- the park has only one entrance (!) which is really hard to find because you need to go through the construction area to get to the park
- due to the fact that there is a construction area right next to the park - there is a lot of construction waste at the entrance, I think it is unsafe and creepy

This Healthy park is really amazing with a big running track through big and small trees.

I really enjoy visiting here to fo exercise as this place is not near to the public place and you can get a good relaxation from the   Carbon or air pollution.

Wheel chair is also accessible and have a small parking outside the park.

You can do yoga as well under the trees or many other good places as well.

A good place to do your daily routine. I recommend this place.

Visited on April 9, 2025.
The construction is still ongoing, and the entrance is hard to find since the access road is right next to the construction site.
Some streetlights are not working, making certain areas quite dark.
There are restroom facilities available.
The track isn't very wide, so if you're running, you need to be cautious of people walking or taking a stroll.
Also, I spotted a large lizard (called hia in Thai) in the area.
One lap is approximately 1 km.
Excellent, 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️ never too crowded love it
A nice little workout park. It would have been good if they had maintained the park a bit more. The entrance is through the active construction site so not very easy to find.

reviews.text: A really nice park to take a break. Lots of seating available in the shade and in the sun. Love the bridges connecting parts of the park together. Although, it is in the middle of the city somehow the traffic noise is dampened in the park and you can hear the birds singing. It was a nice respite.

Pathum Wananurak Park is a peaceful hidden green space right in the middle of Bangkok’s busy Ratchathewi area. It’s not a huge park, but it’s beautifully maintained with lots of trees, shaded walkways, and benches where you can take a break from the city heat. The atmosphere feels calm and safe, making it a nice spot for families, joggers, or anyone wanting a breather after shopping or sightseeing nearby.

There’s many open lawns that are great for a picnic or just relaxing. The park is also very clean and has public toilets on-site. The grass was being cut as we entered- leaving the beautiful fresh-cut grass smell for us to enjoy. Because it’s close to CentralWorld and other big malls, it’s easy to pop in for some greenery between activities.

You'll also spot many water areas - some modern and sculpted, some more natural and wild. Little bridges provide perfect photo spots for those with time and patience to pose in the heat.

It’s not a “destination park” like Lumpini or Benjakitti, but it’s definitely worth a visit if you’re in the area and want a quiet, shady escape from the Bangkok traffic.

Keep in mind that there are 2 entrances, and therefore only two ways you can exit the park. We hoped to use is as a shortcut away from CentralWorld to a quieter area where we could arrange a Bolt ride. Unfortunately, we weren't able to do this but very much enjoyed the park either way.

Green space in the middle of a shopping district. There are many shade areas for you to sit and some interesting landscapes around the park.

However there seems to be some homeless people sleeping in the area but nothing scary. Staffs / gardeners are working around the park most of the time but then again the park still seems to be lacking in maintainance and cleaning.  The park is not crowded so it's not a bad place to get away from people.

There are 2 entrances one in the front next to the main road and another on the side connecting with the Central world. The entry is free.

A lovely, peaceful park in a very busy area. It's right next to the skywalk but you have to use ground access, so don't go up the stairs if you plan to visit the park. Definitely worth visiting if you need to relax for a while. You can obviously also walk and jog for some exercise here. It's clean and well maintained.  Check the photos and judge for yourself. Entry is free.

Nice new park in a very busy and central part of the city. It's usually fairly quiet so it is a good place to escape from the crowds. Not a lot of shade which would be important for the middle of the day

reviews.text:
I visit this park regularly and genuinely enjoy spending time here. I only visit after 5 PM, when entry is free :)

However, there are a few things that could be improved.
Staff move around on scooters with exhaust fumes — since people come here for fresh air, electric vehicles would be a much better fit for the park.
Also, the entrance is paid from 9 AM to 5 PM, so it’s a bit surprising to see some worn benches, dry flower beds, and fountains that aren’t working.
The park also closes quite early :(

Overall, I really like this park and hope to see it get even better over time.

It is a very large public park in Bangkok. It’s located a bit away from the main tourist areas, so the crowd is mostly locals.

The park has a small entry fee (around 10–20 THB). Inside, the area is filled with lots of colorful flowers, themed gardens, and shaded walking paths. The large lake in the center is a highlight, and acts as a nice landmark and photo spot.

There are plenty of photo spots throughout the park whether among the flower beds or scenic corners like the Japanese-style bridge. You’ll also see people of all ages walking, jogging, or sitting under trees and having small picnics, especially near the lake areas. Around sunset, many locals come here to relax, and you will see small boat rides on the lake.

If you visit in December, there’s usually a flower festival with seasonal blooms and colorful displays.

It can feel quite far if you’re visiting Bangkok for the first time since it’s not in the main tourist zone. But if you’ve already covered most city spots, you can consider adding this. It also works well if you’re visiting nearby areas like Train Night Markets, or if you have some extra time before heading to Suvarnabhumi Airport since it’s on that side of the city.

A really beautiful place!! I would recommend spending a whole day here to be sure to see everything! Very peaceful and quiet. It does not feel like you are in a city. There are many different bird species to view or photograph. A must if you have time in Bangkok.

A perfect city escape! Suan Luang Rama IX is hands down one of the best spots in Bangkok for both fitness and relaxation.
For runners, the paved tracks are spacious, well-maintained, and surrounded by lush greenery, making your morning or evening cardio feel effortless. The vibe is incredibly motivating!
For families, it’s a dream. There’s plenty of shaded space for a picnic, swan boats for the kids, and the botanical gardens are stunning. It’s the ultimate spot for quality time away from the city noise. Highly recommended!

Such a wonderful park! Highly recommend coming here during the winter - they have some flower displays and the weather is so much cooler.

Entry fee is 10 baht per person and 10 baht per vehicle. Plenty of parking on the weekday morning, could be packed during the weekend.

The park is super spacious, with a big pond in the middle. You can walk a full circle and it wont be too tiring. Plenty of space to set up picnic spots. They also have glass house and dessert plant section.

reviews.text: A small public park located below the new Phran Nok elevated road intersecting with Kanchanaphisek Road. It's a shady, clean park with lots of trees. There's a sports field in the middle. Across the road at the U-turn, there's also exercise equipment. This field is quite small, so it's probably more suitable for people who want to walk around comfortably. You can park on the side of the road.

[🎄] Atmosphere: Amazing! Very shady, extremely clean, well-maintained by the staff, full of flowers, refreshing, grass neatly mowed, no litter, surrounded by roads.

[💰] Price: Free!

[🛬] Landing Point: Follow the map.

[💝] Service: Self-service.

[👨‍👩‍👦] Suitable for: People who enjoy walking, running, playing sports, and a quiet environment.

[🏖] Value for money: Excellent.

[🈁️] Summary: You MUST go!

⚠️⚠️⚠️ Reading reviews carries risks. Readers should research before deciding to visit. ☢☢☢

The atmosphere is good, there is a lot of running space, many running friends, there are only good people, it makes us have energy, there is a badminton court, there is a place to sit, there are many fish.

Today, I took my medium-sized dog, weighing 22 kilos, for a run in the evening on Tuesday. It was very crowded. Many stray dogs, both with and without collars, came in. Why doesn't the park manage this? There are so many stray and local dogs, begging for food and even antagonizing my dog. I don't want to go there again; it's dangerous. We tried to chase away the local dogs, but they barked back. My dog ​​wouldn't cooperate, pulling on the leash with all her might. You wouldn't understand unless you've experienced it. It was exhausting. Instead of taking the dog for a run, it's like a dog fighting competition!
- I suggest this park is more suitable for owners of small dogs. There are small dogs everywhere. Maybe it should be renamed "Small Dog Park," and you should avoid going on weekends because there are too many people and dogs.
The landscaping is beautiful, and the runway under the overpass is shaded, so it's not hot.

reviews.text: Visited in June 2025. The park is in Siriraj Hospital area, next to the Siriraj Bimuksthan Museum in one side and Chao Pharaya River on the other. There is a temple like building and an old locomotive here also. The park has a very wide open space with good air.

IMO, this place is optimal for cardio exercise and outdoor activities both in the morning and evening. The vigorous one should be avoided in the afternoon, unless you think heatstroke is a joke. At the center of park, there’s a magnificent pavilion you’ll probably need to see with your own eyes.

Most of the other reviews and pics are about the park across the river. Not this one.
My video captures the entirety of the park.  Don't bother going here.

Park looks cool and quiet.

I think it would be perfect for a lite jogging.

It's a bummer that they don't allow any bike here. I brought my 3 year old with a tiny balance bike. By the park's rule, it is a no-no.

Now I address it here on the internet.
So you don't repeat my mistake.
A quiet place to relax. Nice view along Chaophaya River.

My greatest childhood memory from kindergarten was when my teacher took us across from the kindergarten school to run and play. In elementary school, I enjoyed Children's Day events and sports day there. Personally, I preferred the area before it was converted into a sports field; the wide grassy field was much better. The worst thing I did was climb on the park's name sign, slide down, and then step on the thorny rose bushes – I remember it hurting so much!
Also, during sports day, I saw a green snake in a tree; luckily, I didn't get bitten.
In high school, something happened that changed me. The park was a gathering place for teenagers from various schools. Since I was growing up,
I wanted to impress a girl, so I started practicing basketball. Turns out, I could play basketball, but the girl already had a boyfriend. It was a terrible feeling, but at least I gained height from playing basketball.

reviews.text: Nice little water park. At little far into the countryside. But it's OK. Cheap enough to get in 50thb thai 100thb Farang.  Food and drink prices ok. Some areas a little bit of repair needed. But overall a nice day to relax
Oh, today the price spiral seems to have reached the countryside too! Children and Thais pay 1/4 of the Falang price! When will this injustice finally end?

Great place for such a remote location.  Not as modern as some others in big cities like Bangkok or Phuket. But good enough for the local kids. They've added a normal swimming pool which adults can swim in which is good. Food is ok and cheap as with beers and drinks. 50 bht entrance and 50 bht swim ware hire. If I'm staying in the area I will visit most days

Lovely water park in the middle of nowhere, between Khon Kaen and Chaiyaphum. Has 3 main areas to swim in. Toddlers, juniors and teens and adults. Lots of choices of slides. There is a small animal enclosure. Only 50 baht entrance fee. Lots of choices of food at reasonable prices. Well worth the money. Will definitely be coming again.
A lot of fun for kids, two pools with sliders and many tables/chairs for sitting. Entrance 50 Baht. Sometimes music too loud. Overall good with kids on a hot day, might be crowded on thai holidays

reviews.text: Disappointment. The basketball court looks very nice. Especially in videos and photos. In reality it is very slippery there. The coating is clearly not for the street - just some kind of glossy paint. I can't imagine how people play there. It is very easy to get injured.

A hidden garden in Soi Wat Dan features a pond, walking, running, and cycling paths. It seems to be still under construction. I never expected a public park in such a deep alley. The atmosphere is nice, with a cool breeze. Many people come to play basketball and football, while others run around the pond. It closes at 9 PM.
Very busy but good to see people excerising. More construction of more paths going on. Please keep to running lane as bike riders go so fast in the next lane.
Great place to walk

Good park for watdan samrong. wish they will have more park in my neighborhood.
they have bike lane, ruuning lane, small futsal and aerobic space.
restroom and parking lot are available.

Part of a huge sports center complex at the Ministry of Health, well maintained, you can play lots of sports here, from Sepak Takraw, tennis, soccer, ping pong to basketball. Lots of people jogging. I love this area, almost everyday there, highly recommended 👌

reviews.text: Good
There are 4 rubber-floored badminton courts, a fitness center and a ping pong table, along with a shop selling drinks and badminton balls.

The indoor sports facility includes a 4-court basketball court, a 5-table ping pong area, and a fitness center. Parking is available around the building (no parking on the street, your car will be towed).
An indoor fitness facility for all ages, from youth to seniors. Instructors are available, and parking is reasonably convenient.

reviews.text: Dept. of Thai Traditional is very helpful and informative. I came here for meeting attendance and discussion with the professionals. The parking lot is very limited, recommend to park the outside nearby a little walk but the better choice.
Paranoid Schiozophrynia
Frankly, even high school students seem more capable of managing public relations. It's sad because this is government management, and it should be of much higher quality.
On October 29, 2023, I attended the closing ceremony of the Royal Monument of King Rama III, "The Father of Thai Traditional Medicine," on National Thai Traditional Medicine Wisdom Day 2023.

Department of Thai Traditional and Alternative Medicine. Just walking past, you will gain knowledge of Thai wisdom in treating diseases, such as the properties of herbs, Thai physical exercises, 15 Rishi Datton poses. There is a Thai massage clinic on the upper floor (Thai massage service).

reviews.text: Lakes are separated into two parts. Main lake is normal. I saw many wild water lizards which are longer than 1.5 meters. I could approach to them to take a picture.

Nice place for exercising and walking. 4:30 PM onwards it’s possible to rent bicycles. It’s 30 Baht per bicycle. There are some dedicated spaces for Badminton and gym instruments. Kids can feed the fish in the park.
Came here whilst tere was lights . So beautiful 😍
Beautiful park, a bit crowded and almost well maintained.

Nice public park at the outer parts of the city. There are pathes for running, and some fitness gear.

You also can sit at the water to chill and feed the fish. Bread for the fish is sold at small mobile shops close to the water.

reviews.text: Great location for kids, even though it’s close to the highway. The area is closed to cars, and only joggers use the road next to the playground, which makes it much safer for children to play. Also great for the picnic!
It's a good park for walking, it's very small but very clean and people can play basketball and there's a space for children to play

This is a small-sized park with a playground for football, and it's a great place to relax. I find the park even more peaceful and relaxing at night. It is conveniently located next to the train station.

Piya Phirom Park is a public park located in the Hua Mak area of Bangkok, Thailand. Residents here like running, walking, and evening local community gether here. Its location in Hua Mak makes it accessible to local schools, shopping centres, and public transport, which is convenient for people working or studying in Bangkok.

reviews.text: A very nice small park with a beautiful modern building in the centre. Around it there are numerous water lilies and small islands on which various trees and plants have been planted. The highlight is this wonderful footbridge through which you can get from one side of the park to the other. There is also a small playground for children. If you are in the area and want to relax, this place is very special.
Beautiful, quiet and peaceful. This is a great greenspace, for a respite from the urban jungle!

Small park beautiful ambiance with lake in middle can feed the fish & turtles. Good place for jog but small have kids playground. Surrounding full of trees and good wind. Small space for parking so come abit early to park cars otherwise come by public transportation or taxi.

A great, calming and green workout space. This is easily one of the best parks in Bangkok. The runway is clean and supporting. There are some workout equipments provided, but don't get your hopes up. There are enough parking lot for your car and bike. After workout, you can have a decent meal in many restaurants nearby and it's cheap.
My running session was calm and effecrive since there were not much people blocking the way. I will definitely go there again. Love it!
This was very nice park to jogging with family and this place has flesh air which is found very hard in Bangkok.

reviews.text: This is a fun and unique park! Aftet dark, when the park lights up, it's a lot of fun. Its spread out enough that the little kids can play without being trampled by big kids. It's got a fence going all the way around the park, so that's a plus. The bathrooms were decent, as well.
It's very pretty park with lot of rides and activities. Good for day time but night time is super fun. They have lot of  slides of different ages. Swings, scooter path and lot more fun.

Super cool night park with lights that the kids enjoyed! The metal slides were a little scary for them to come down but they were fine! They loved the spinning seats and climbing the main structure. So nice with warmer wear to play outside closes at 10pm, at which time the glow lights turn off and then flood lights above turn on so it’s not dark and kids can continue to play a few more minutes
We absolutely loved it, especially at night when it started to glow. The only thing this park needs, are swings!
This park is great because it’s fenced in! There’s so many different things to play on. The recommended ages are 5-12.

Good for joking
Goods

This old village park has a pleasant atmosphere and serves as a central location for the Sena community and nearby residents. I've heard that it's been given over to the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration (BMA) for management. However, I've noticed some problems:
1. A group of people use it as a place to drink and smoke every day (making loud noises and disturbing park users).
2. There are several stray dogs (they haven't bitten anyone yet, so there haven't been any issues).
3. The equipment is dilapidated and hasn't been repaired, but they're using the budget to build a fence around the park (for what?).
This is it... This is all the bureaucrats can think of.

reviews.text: I recently visited Thonburiam Park with my friend to take a break from our studies. The park feels calm and refreshing and is clearly well maintained. There are wide walking paths, plenty of greenery and several small ponds that make the place peaceful. When we visited, people were walking, jogging or sitting quietly and enjoying the surroundings. The outdoor gym area and table tennis space are a nice bonus for those who want to stay active. There is also a free water refill station, which is very convenient. Overall, the park is easy to access and a great place to relax and clear your mind. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a quiet outdoor space.

Beautiful is all I can say. The bird chirping, the water wheels spinning, they are all part of this park’s beauty. Once in a while, I come here to walk and reflect on myself whenever I feel low. I love this place even more in April! With Padauk flower coloring the park yellow, the wind carries the scent along, which makes my mind calm and reminds me of home.

This place compliments my KMUTT student life. Even if it is a study break or a short walk home, this place is relaxing.

However, the water smell sometimes disrupt my walk and some equipments are broken. But overall, a beautiful place.

A peaceful and well-maintained green environment with shady trees, spectacular scenery, walking paths and fresh, breezy air. Its location next to KMUTT makes it convenient for university students to relax, take short walks and do light exercises to clear their minds from stress and exhaustion built up throughout the school day. Being here feels genuinely refreshing, especially after long hours of classes, as it provides a sense of escape from academic pressure. The cats wandering around the park are also approachable, and being able to pat them adds to the overall charm. However, some of the exercise equipment looks rusty and outdated, which could be improved for better safety and comfort.

Palm trees lined up throughout Thonburirom Park. Rather than forcing things in, the park feels designed to preserve nature as it is.
Wow it was genuinely beautiful. I really loved it.

The park’s design is thoughtful, too. Walking paths and running tracks are clearly separated, so everyone can enjoy the space at their own pace. It’s a great park for jogging🏃‍♀️🏃🏃‍♂️

This was my first time visiting this park, after usually just walking past it. Inside, the park was more pleasant than I expected. The trees and flowers along the paths looked vibrant, and there was no litter. The paths were divided into lanes for running and walking, there were areas for exercising and playing sports, vending machines for drinks, and clean restrooms.
I'm glad I don't see the Big Lizard anymore; that was a major reason why I hesitated to go in at first. However, I felt the pond wasn't as clean as it should be; I wish the water was clearer. Sometimes, when walking past the edge of the pond, there was still a faint, foul smell. But overall, for me, this place is perfect for relaxing or taking an evening walk for exercise.

reviews.text: Such a beautiful dog park! 🌿 It’s very clean and well-maintained, with lots of lovely ponds that make the whole place feel peaceful and scenic. One of the best dog parks. Go early morning or later evening to avoid the heat! Big open spaces for dogs to run around.

free and accessible park. nice atmosphere and really well-maintained.
pet(dog)-friendly zone available.
recommended coming in the evening.

reviews.text: Great eateries on two levels. One can sit anywhere and order from all the restaurants on display. Menu was quite thick so be sure to take a look before hand. Great variety of choices and delicious too

The Mahanakhon Cube in Bangkok is an impressive destination for food lovers, offering a diverse selection of restaurants and cafes that provide unique culinary experiences. Located at the base of King Power Mahanakhon, Thailand's tallest building, the Cube features a striking glass façade and contemporary design, making it a standout landmark in the city.
With a vibrant atmosphere, visitors can enjoy a variety of dining options, ranging from local Thai delicacies to international cuisine, ensuring there’s something to satisfy every palate. The combination of good taste and aesthetic appeal creates a delightful setting for both casual outings and special occasions.
In addition to its dining experiences, Mahanakhon Cube also offers a vibrant shopping scene with retail spaces and an art gallery. For those looking to take in breathtaking views, the accessible skywalk provides stunning panoramic sights of the Bangkok skyline.
The Mahanakhon Cube is conveniently located near the Chong Nonsi BTS Station. This station is just a short walk (2 minutes) away, making it easily accessible for visitors using the Bangkok Skytrain system.
Chagee first opened at King Power Mahanakhon. I ordered at 1 p.m. and didn’t get my drink until 5 p.m., but the tea’s taste and aroma were rich and refreshing.

I ate here before visiting King Power Mahanakon. A not very big food court. You can't pay directly at the merchants, you have to buy a card at the cash register, which you load with whatever amount you want. You pay at the stalls with this. The card is refundable, but for a tourist it's just an extra hassle.

Had dinner there. The service was good. Free soft drink or a bottle of water for Instagram follow.

It's a food court. Could order any food items available in the menu. The crab omelette was not recommended. Too oily and the crab meat was not fresh. It's hard rock and tasteless.

reviews.text: Huge complex. Been to 2nd floor of west tower and 10th of main tower. Both were very modern.
Siam Commercial Bank Headquarters is a huge complex and host many restaurants and shops as well

The Siam Commercial Bank headquarter is huge and historic on Ratchadaphisek Road. Inside the area have many restaurants, shops, and offices. Expect heavy traffic during peak time in the morning and after work hour. The parking space is quit limited and fees is quit high.
The theme of the meeting rooms is full of energy.

SCB Park Plaza is a seriously huge place, with the West Plaza, Main Building, and the East Building. It is the place where SCB carries out most of its operations, as well as innovation.

There are many random bazaars being held, between the main building and the east plaza, and it is interesting to find things that you least expect in such bazaars.

It is also located closely to Boxspace and what the locals call 'Tent', which is a hawker selling local dishes. Most employees of SCB head over to this hawker for a plate of sumptuous meal for lunch

reviews.text: Very nice place for food & drinks with good balance and asian and western outlets.

A Hub of Modern Convenience and Connectivity
SCB Park Plaza West stands out as a premier business location, offering a sophisticated and modern environment. The complex is praised for its contemporary design and excellent facilities, creating a truly pleasant and productive atmosphere for both employees and visitors.

One of its key strengths is its strategic location on Ratchadaphisek Road, providing seamless access to major city routes and public transportation, including the nearby BTS Phahonyothin 24 station.

Beyond its prime accessibility, the plaza boasts a wide array of amenities. With a variety of shops, cafes, and restaurants available on-site, everything you need is just a few steps away. The well-designed common areas and green spaces further enhance the experience, making SCB Park Plaza West not just a place to work, but a dynamic and convenient urban hub.

Staff are helpful on site, but if you call their customer service number in English, expect to wait over 24minutes. Sometimes they even cut off automatically after 25 minutes waiting, I think. Maybe because they bought Citibank credit cards in Thailand, I guess. But should have prepare better.

I went to this bank branch, which is located near their main office. They used to have a machine where you could deposit coins automatically, but it's not there anymore. The line was quite busy when I visited. The parking on the B1 floor is free for 30 minutes, and they can validate it for longer if needed.
Tops, 7-11, boots ,MC Donal  resturant, Coffee shop.What else do you need?

reviews.text: Sleepy little park with a 900 meter circular sidewalk around it. A lot of half broken exercise equipment available. Might be okay for kids with adult supervision.
No written review.

It's a small park at the very end of the alley, located in the middle of a roundabout. It has nice shady trees, a place to exercise, a children's play area, and food and drinks for sale. There are also cats and dogs that come to rest and escape the sun under the trees. When I went, I caught a glimpse of a cat burying its poop – it was so cute and natural. It's worth visiting to experience the power of nature. Even though the place isn't huge, I feel like I want to visit again. The image of that cat burying its poop is still vivid in my memory. I recommend you visit. If you see that cat, please pet its neck for me! (It was white and black).
It's a community public space where you can come for walks, play badminton, or do aerobics. It's shady and safe.
The alley is narrow and not suitable for large vehicles.

reviews.text: Great place to escape from busy bangkok city.not too far. I go from hotel near bbk shopping mall 600 baht as toll 50 baht. Better tell the taxi to come back to pick up. Or use grab. As inside small street hard to get taxi.

One of the most beautiful places in Bangkok! Extremely romantic and the food is excellent. The extremely hospitable Soda was our server and he took some amazing pics of us too! Just beyond great service. We did the dessert class with Joy and he was a great teacher and such a sweetie. After, Joy sent us on our way for a boat excursion. Won’t forget this experience!!

Came here for a weekend date — romantic vibes, beautiful garden with wooden arches and sculptures, and a cool breeze. Had an orange Americano while chilling by the river view. Great photo spots, friendly staff, good service, and reasonable prices. Definitely coming back!

I never thought a place like this existed in Bangkok! Today we arrived by boat and it was so convenient with its own pier. The atmosphere is calm and refreshing, not hot at all, with plenty of trees and shaded areas. There are different zones to explore and so many activities to enjoy. It feels warm and welcoming...

a perfect garden spot for the whole family  time :)
LOVED our visit to Poomjai for a delicious brunch. And then the coconut farm boat tour. Brilliant experience of learning about coconut production and life along the river. Thank you so much

reviews.text:
i went to Phangan island from Samui and also back to Samui with them.
⭕️😇feeling peaceful.

Do you have one day trip ticket from Samui-Pha-ngan-Samui?!

Petcharat Peir on Phanagn island is in middle between Rin Beach and Thong-Sala pier. Very flexible to plan my one day trip 😘

Booked via “ferrysamui” web. Paid by card. Tickets for “songserm” to fast boat.
On time departure and arrival.

We booked our ferry with Songserm via 123Go and paid easily using Apple Pay. The trip was on time and operated with a speedboat, which brought us to Koh Phangan in just 20 minutes, exactly as stated. For short distances like this, the speedboat is convenient and fast. However, for longer routes such as to Koh Tao, I would recommend taking one of the larger ferries, as they handle rough seas better and the ride is noticeably smoother.
I booked my speedboat ride to Koh Pha-Ngan here. It’s easy and fuss free and I arrived to KP within 25 minutes.
Convenient pier with restaurant overlooking the bay. Very easy access to Koh Phangan from here. Parking for boats and jetskis available.

reviews.text:
Beautiful bird watching tower.
I went mid day and didn’t see a single bird but that’s not the towers fault. Primetime is usually dawn and dusk for birding.

Note: getting directions directly here from the pier/bike rental shop brought me to the wrong location in a jungle trail outside of the park (see photo). I’d recommended taking the main road into the park and following the trails and signs.
Didn’t see any birds but had a right adventure finding it (it was wrong on Google maps) which made it so much more fun. Really beautiful views and bird calls!

It's a must-see when you are staying in Bangkok and have some extra days! The perfect contrast to the busy, loud and hot city - a nice place to cool down, enjoy nature and learn something about Thai forests and animals. We finally heard some animal sounds and not only the sound of engines, honking and noise (like in the rest of Bangkok).
I recommend renting a bicycle and driving around the island of Bang Kachao. Really enjoyable route - 5/5, would recommend!

This tower isn't for birdwatching, but for cyclists and TikTokers taking their photos here. It's so noisy that you can't even hope to see any birds. And that's despite the fact that we were there early in the morning.
If you're coming here to see birds, don't waste your time.
Easy to find and to climb. Not much to see without a proper binocular. And not high enough to get a view over the trees of the park. Could not even see the buildings on the other riverside.

reviews.text: Visited in June 2025. The park is in Siriraj Hospital area, next to the Siriraj Bimuksthan Museum in one side and Chao Pharaya River on the other. There is a temple like building and an old locomotive here also. The park has a very wide open space with good air.

IMO, this place is optimal for cardio exercise and outdoor activities both in the morning and evening. The vigorous one should be avoided in the afternoon, unless you think heatstroke is a joke. At the center of park, there’s a magnificent pavilion you’ll probably need to see with your own eyes.

Most of the other reviews and pics are about the park across the river. Not this one.
My video captures the entirety of the park.  Don't bother going here.

Park looks cool and quiet.

I think it would be perfect for a lite jogging.

It's a bummer that they don't allow any bike here. I brought my 3 year old with a tiny balance bike. By the park's rule, it is a no-no.

Now I address it here on the internet.
So you don't repeat my mistake.
A quiet place to relax. Nice view along Chaophaya River.

reviews.text:
We came for the playground,which was safe.As far as for fun, you'd have to ask my child lol. It seemed fairly standard but safe to a degree. We've come back a hand full of times and there is this one standing swing apparatus which is supposed to be tied down with a little leeway to build a little strength and balance as you walk on it to get to the next one beside it. But when I noticed my daughter swinging on it. It seemed odd that it would swing out too far when the adjacent ones on either sides of it were properly tethered/chained down to prevent it from a) running into a walk way, b) colliding into the other c) tipping someone over.
They had not addressed it and taken care of that potential hazard yet.
I was raised in the 80's. So I figured," hey just let her be!" But when younger or less physically rambunctious children were trying to hang on and find their equilibrium her center apparatus banged into them knocking them off balance and often times right off 🤭.
So I pulled her right off to show some care for the safety of other people's children.
We moved on to other parts of that area as we watched in horror as some of the unregulated kids would just haphazardly an improperly use the particular playground equipment dangerously without knowing. And watching a Parent show how it's done and fall right off hurting their pride 🥹🤭. When the equipment has to be properly attached.
Moving right along, obviously, where there's ducks, and or geese, there will be their feces all over the place that's just the way it is. It's that's prolific. For the most part they stay by the water, but when a lot of them have ventured out further inland they leave their happy droppings of course.
it's a dog-friendly Park that has dog poop all over. I thought, hey, it's Manhattan Beach, surely some of these folks have a reasonable decorum of cleanliness and will pick up after their own dog. 🤷..
Maybe, it could be people not from around the area that are being disgusting not caring, usually is. That's not the parks fault. But it does need a little more attention to keep it from getting a little more out of hand which is coming close to.

We stumbled upon Polliwog Park and absolutely loved it. Such a beautiful park with a peaceful pond and lots of open space to explore. My granddaughter had the best time—she especially loved the ducks and learned how to say “duck” for the first time here! 🦆

The toddler play area is enclosed, which made it feel super safe and relaxed for little ones to run around. You can tell this place is very family-friendly—everyone we met was kind and welcoming. A perfect spot for a sunny afternoon with babies and young kids. We’ll definitely be back!

Beautiful community park with a lovely lake, plenty of open space, and free street parking nearby. The playground is very well equipped, and there’s also a solid outdoor workout station for adults. Bathrooms are available and generally convenient.
The main downside is the is the goose poop around the lake area — while the geese add to the scenery, there’s quite a bit of goose poop concentrated near the water, so watch your step.
It's a very well-maintained and clean park,  It's a large and wonderful park. The only inconvenience is that there aren't many restrooms.

one of the best chill parks in manhattan beach, hands down. super peaceful vibes with the pond, ducks just living their best life, and plenty of open space to relax or walk around. the amphitheater is a cool touch and actually feels alive when events are happening. great spot for families, casual walks, or just zoning out for a bit. the disc golf course is a fun bonus, and the park is clean and well-maintained. dogs are allowed, which is a huge win. parking is usually manageable too.

overall, perfect balance between calm and community energy. yeah twin, this place slaps

reviews.text: Although half the trails were closed when we went it was still a very lovely place to explore. There were no bear encounters. It would appear that the wildlife are used to humans though we didn’t bring food because we don’t usually do that unless it’s specified that it’s allowed but seemed like they wanted food. Very beautiful park. Definitely returning when they reopen the other side.

Nice and clean park. Although I was disappointed that only one of the trails was open. It appears they are not attempting to fix the other trails because they are all overgrown. Based on the pictures, it looked like you can reach the lake and the gazebo which looks very pretty. I was unable to find any route to see any of that.

This little park is just the bees knees.

Since moving to the area a few years ago, I’ve been lucky to live just 2 minutes away from this hidden gem.

There’s a lot of foot area for exploring on their trails (most of which are on risen wooden boardwalks), plus the playground and of course the lake towards the back of the park where there’s always someone fishing. There’s a butterfly garden near the parking/playground areas where I’ve seen massively cool looking caterpillars, incredibly deep colored flowers, and a plethora of butterflies. My one complaint about this park is that it’s closed on weekdays (it’s closed Mon-Wed). I used to have another complaint regarding the trolley, but now that the pandemic has affected everything, there is no more trolley (yay!). You used to have to park on the other side of the main street to ride a trolley if you wanted to get in the park on weekends. Never made sense to me.

If you want to disconnect from the noise of the city, the news, and everything else, but can't go too far from home? Come here. If you easily find the magic in nature, this place is quite the adventure compared to most other parks in the area. You will be absolutely delighted.

We’ve been visiting Lake Lotus Park for quite a few years now and it’s a great place to take your family. The rangers are attentive, fishing is always a great option and the tragic, albeit short, is an educational experience. Nice park for a picnic too.

Trail run? Fishing? Nature view? A breathe of fresh air?
They all have it here!
I am really amazed as to how they made the running route. The route is made of wood which is to protect humans for wild animals.  ( creaking and old but I did not encounter a broken one, but still watch out!) ( you may see a lot of warning signs about alligators inside)
In the middle there is what they call the eye or window of the lake where in you could view the whole lake!
Furthermore, fishing in the area is allowed but make sure you have the license for it.
There are also some playground for kids to play in the middle of the park.
The comfort room is decent and clean.
And they have a map! So we won't get lost!
5 stars for this and this is by far the best i've seen in altamonte. 😊

reviews.text:
I visit this park regularly and genuinely enjoy spending time here. I only visit after 5 PM, when entry is free :)

However, there are a few things that could be improved.
Staff move around on scooters with exhaust fumes — since people come here for fresh air, electric vehicles would be a much better fit for the park.
Also, the entrance is paid from 9 AM to 5 PM, so it’s a bit surprising to see some worn benches, dry flower beds, and fountains that aren’t working.
The park also closes quite early :(

Overall, I really like this park and hope to see it get even better over time.

It is a very large public park in Bangkok. It’s located a bit away from the main tourist areas, so the crowd is mostly locals.

The park has a small entry fee (around 10–20 THB). Inside, the area is filled with lots of colorful flowers, themed gardens, and shaded walking paths. The large lake in the center is a highlight, and acts as a nice landmark and photo spot.

There are plenty of photo spots throughout the park whether among the flower beds or scenic corners like the Japanese-style bridge. You’ll also see people of all ages walking, jogging, or sitting under trees and having small picnics, especially near the lake areas. Around sunset, many locals come here to relax, and you will see small boat rides on the lake.

If you visit in December, there’s usually a flower festival with seasonal blooms and colorful displays.

It can feel quite far if you’re visiting Bangkok for the first time since it’s not in the main tourist zone. But if you’ve already covered most city spots, you can consider adding this. It also works well if you’re visiting nearby areas like Train Night Markets, or if you have some extra time before heading to Suvarnabhumi Airport since it’s on that side of the city.

A really beautiful place!! I would recommend spending a whole day here to be sure to see everything! Very peaceful and quiet. It does not feel like you are in a city. There are many different bird species to view or photograph. A must if you have time in Bangkok.

A perfect city escape! Suan Luang Rama IX is hands down one of the best spots in Bangkok for both fitness and relaxation.
For runners, the paved tracks are spacious, well-maintained, and surrounded by lush greenery, making your morning or evening cardio feel effortless. The vibe is incredibly motivating!
For families, it’s a dream. There’s plenty of shaded space for a picnic, swan boats for the kids, and the botanical gardens are stunning. It’s the ultimate spot for quality time away from the city noise. Highly recommended!

Such a wonderful park! Highly recommend coming here during the winter - they have some flower displays and the weather is so much cooler.

Entry fee is 10 baht per person and 10 baht per vehicle. Plenty of parking on the weekday morning, could be packed during the weekend.

The park is super spacious, with a big pond in the middle. You can walk a full circle and it wont be too tiring. Plenty of space to set up picnic spots. They also have glass house and dessert plant section.

reviews.text:
I finally had the opportunity to see one great neurologist who asked smart questions and did check my whole body with various test, he answered all my questions in the best way he could and very calmly. I went to few hospitals but he was by far the best. I can’t remember his name but he stay in consultation room 105.
All the nurse and receptionist are great and friendly too. They don’t speak english tho but thats okay, you can use a translator.
Thank you for everybody working in that hospital. It was a great experience :)
It is located at the opposite side of the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities on Ratchawithi road. The staff are nice and friendly.

Dr. Tippakorn was very caring and went out of his way to find the cause of my fainting spells and tremors. He has been the first doctor I have seen in the 28 years I have lived in Bangkok that actually showed compassion and cared.  He also spoke to me not at me or to my husband who is Thai.  Most doctors in Thailand that I have seen are to afraid to speak English but Dr. Tippakorn was not. Which made me feel he actually wanted to help me.  Because of him I was diagnosed with Autoimmune Disease.   Thank you Dr. Tippakorn.

Very good hospitality. All officers serve patients with willingness and smiling faces. Doctors listen carefully and answer all questions with good explanation. Very happy to come here despite the sickness.

Prasat Neurological Institute is the Government hospital. Actually here is not join The Golden Card of the government. But if you are Thai citizen and didn't register to any hospital. You can go to see the doctor here without pay. You pay only some medicine.

reviews.text:
overall had a good experience. The park is well organized and you get to see a variety of animals, especially in the safari drive and the shows, which were quite entertaining.
One thing to keep in mind is that the photo sessions with animals inside the park are quite expensive. If you are specifically looking for animal photos, you might find better and more affordable options in Pattaya.
That said, the park itself is enjoyable and worth visiting, especially if you are traveling with family or kids. Overall, a fun outing in Bangkok with good shows and a nice safari experience.

Safari World is an absolute blast! The shows are fantastic—high-energy, well-produced, and genuinely impressive. They are definitely the highlight of the park and worth the visit alone.
However, I would recommend skipping the Indian restaurants inside the park. The food didn't really live up to expectations, and the chaos inside the buffet is too much. Better to go in other restaurants and grab a chilled beer or plan for a nice dinner back in the city instead.

Safari world bangkok is really another best place to visit in Bangkok. If you are travelling with children then this is the best place to visit. They have ample parking space. There are two seperate Park inside this world which is safari park and marine park. I visited marine park which is quite big and has many shows which are enjoyable. You can even click pictures with many animals like Tiger, orangutans etc. They have giraffe feeding as well. Overall its a  good place to visit.

Best for family
one of the absolute highlights of my trip to Thailand. From the moment we arrived, the excitement was palpable, and the entire experience far exceeded our already high expectations. This isn't just an attraction; it's a full-day immersion into the wonders of the animal kingdom, brilliantly split into two distinct and thrilling sections.

We began our adventure with the iconic Safari Park drive-through. It felt surreal to be driving through expansive landscapes with majestic giraffes gracefully wandering past our windows, herds of zebras and antelope grazing peacefully, and powerful lions lounging just a few meters away. It was like being transported directly to the heart of an African savanna, all while being in Bangkok. The animals appeared healthy, active, and well-cared-for in their spacious enclosures. After the drive, we entered the Marine Park, which is a vibrant hub of activity. The shows here are nothing short of spectacular. The Dolphin Show was a beautiful display of agility and intelligence, while the Spy War stunt show had our entire group on the edge of our seats with its high-octane action and incredible special effects

Our visit to Safari World Thailand with my family was truly a memorable experience. From the moment we arrived, we felt excited and welcomed. The safari drive was the highlight of our trip, as we were able to see animals like giraffes, zebras, and lions up close, which was especially enjoyable for the kids. The animal shows were fun, entertaining, and perfect for the whole family. The place was well organized, clean, and safe, making it easy to relax and enjoy the day together. It was a wonderful bonding experience for our family, and we left with happy memories that we will always cherish. Highly recommended for families visiting Thailand.

reviews.text: good place
It looks shady and has a lot of places to exercise, but it's not very organized.

Many people come here to exercise, and many are focused on maintaining their health. As Toon Bodyslam said, "We reduce the burden on doctors by exercising, which means we don't get sick."
There are 12 exercise machines here to exercise every part of the body. There's a circular running track, smaller than a football field. Within the circle, there's also a basketball court, a takraw court, and even a petanque court, offering other options for those who exercise."
The spacious field is shady and has some exercise equipment, but the running track is made of interlocking paving blocks, making it a bit difficult to run on.

reviews.text: Nice

Wisesuk Nakhon Village, Pracha Uthit 79, is a large village with two 7/11 convenience stores and a Lotus Express before the village entrance. There's also a flea market and several delicious food carts. Traveling is convenient, accessible via either Pracha Uthit Road or Suksawat Road.

There's a morning market in the middle of the village along the way.
There's a lot of food and snacks. I really like it.

It's a large village, but most of the buildings are old. The great thing about it is that it's easily accessible from many different routes. It's close to several markets, gas stations, shopping malls, and schools.

The village is quite large, has a variety of restaurants with reasonable prices, and small four-wheeled vehicles can pass through all the time. There are many ways to enter and exit, and the road is wide.

reviews.text: Old and run down.Needs maintenance. Many Cars and bikes use the park road as a shortcut. No washroom or toilet here.

It's nice to have this park nearby but the path is very narrow and is now pretty worn out and uneven. Just like other parks, the concept of staying left and being aware and considerate to other park users appears alien to many patrons. If you are walking two or three across you block the path for others, and this happens. One lap of the main running path is about 800m. Not sure who owns and operates this park, but some attention to finer details would make it more enjoyable and safe for everyone.

Small exercise park in the city which is nice just to get away from busy traffic and use free exercise machines and walk around. Not very big but glad to find a park while staying in the area as not much greenery otherwise nearby
The single place for my parents to have exercise in that area. Thank you so much for to Sena's vision to open for public.
Really good place for a jog. Just next to the Highway but its quite peaceful.

reviews.text: The garden is quite narrow, but it's shady and nice. It lacks a little decoration, but it's quite clean.

This small park is a great place to relax and escape the heat outside. It's a pocket park concept integrated into the community. If it were more creatively designed with additional functions and activities to attract people, it would be even better.
The garden has a small area, not many trees or flowers planted, and there is one pavilion for sitting and relaxing.

reviews.text: Well-located with a pleasant trail for walking and running, both in the open and wooded areas. It offers great facilities for leisure, rest, and sports, with clean, well-maintained bathrooms. Staff are always on hand to ensure cleanliness and safety. My only tip: use mosquito protection in summer, as insects can be plentiful.

Great park -lots of parking -great scenery
It would be perfect to have a family day since it’s so big even can BBQ since there is a pavilion to cover you with seating.This park is huge with a natural trail. Volleyball net and basketball is available Great for nature- family time. It’s well kept and clean.

Spacious park with one covered shelter. Lots of Parking space. Also has a trail [walking path] around the park along with a volleyball court. The open space is used for soccer. There is a kids play area too

Not too familiar with suburban parks. It’s clean. There’s that. It’s got a small wooded trail. Huge field, big enough for football, soccer and the like. They have fries bee golf course here, I think that’s what it’s called. There’s a few grills and picnic tables so cookouts are an option. Toilet facilities are decent. Plenty of parking. I think.

Man. The suburbs are depressing.

Nice walking path a little bit over a mile all the way around the long way. There is a short way, probably one half mile.  Lots of good shade. There's a playscape for kids.  I did not notice many picnic benches if at all. But I wasn't looking for them.

reviews.text: Jardin  de poche. Very quiet , a variety of  rare trees and plants with name cards. Birds sing different dirtiest. No crowds. Other than employees I found 2-3 visiters. Clean and free toilets. Sculpture and exhibition of Princess Mother who was born a commener but become Royality.

A Quiet Tribute to a Remarkable Woman

We found the Princess Mother Memorial Park completely by accident – and what a serene discovery it was. Tucked away from Bangkok’s hustle and bustle, this peaceful and lovingly kept garden feels like a sanctuary. The entire space is dedicated to Somdet Phra Srinagarindra, the Princess Mother of King Rama IX, known for her modesty, deep compassion, and tireless social work, especially for Thailand’s rural and mountain communities.

The small museum and traditional-style buildings give quiet insight into her life and values. Everything about the park seems to reflect her gentle character: unpretentious, nurturing, and full of grace.

Entrance is free, and there were barely any other visitors when we went – a true hidden gem. It felt like walking through someone’s story, carefully preserved and shared with deep respect. Highly recommended if you’re looking for a meaningful, peaceful stop during your stay in Bangkok.

Took a steady walk from Chinatown across Chao Phraya Sky Park and through a local neighborhood to find this lovely park. No fee to stroll through well landscaped gardens, a small oasis and very peaceful. There is a small charity shop, restrooms, and a museum commemorating the Princess Mother. Lots of places to sit, relax in the shade.

Princess Mother Memorial Park (Suan Somdet Ya) Soi Somdej Chao Phraya 3 community
This is the 1st time for me
It is a museum suitable for studying history. and the royal duties of Her Royal Highness Princess Maha Chakri Sirindhorn, beloved by all Thai people. The museum has 2 building.It is a place to learn with the next generation.
The park within the local community, in memory of the late Princess Mother. Its quiet and peaceful with nice greenery.

The Park was well-maintained and theres even the house gallery that showcases the background and history interior thai style.

reviews.text: I used to pass by during the day that it was beautiful. At night, when the lights are reflected in the building, it’s even more beautiful which gives you a different mood.

I accidentally stumbled upon the Royal Pavilion Mahajetsadabadin while walking around, and I’m so glad I did. At night, the place looks absolutely stunning, with the lights making everything glow beautifully. The temple beside it just adds to the whole magical vibe.
Incredibly beautiful garden. I was there before lower planting but I am sure it will look even better once they plant the flowers in the front. Nice back drop of the Wat (temple).

This square was really beautiful!! Of course the traditional type house looks gorgeous but not only it, when I visited there are full blooms!! It was awesome!!!! With the temple  in the back I took really wonderful pictures!!! It’s free everybody can stop by any time you come!!! Sometimes you can see monks it also great with the scenes!! Enjoy!!

Royal Pavillion Mahajetsadabadin is an absolute place of cultural and curiosity - certainly worth investigating whilst you are in Bangkok - and if this is what you are looking for.
Try it at night time with the night light on it looks really beautiful.
Day time if you visit you can click lots of pictures as very scenic and mind you entry is free!!

reviews.text:
Any bit of green, no matter the size, is welcome in Bangkok and Mahanakhon Fort Park (MFO) is a great little space near Wat Saket (the Golden Mount temple).

As well as housing a small fort, the park is full of beautifully manicured trees and shrubs, and colorful flowers.

While many people stroll through the park onto the main road to the famous Jay Fai restaurant the park now joins onto a walking path alongside the klong (canal).

This newish canal side walk offers another side of the city that’s rarely seen and a number of cafes and bars have already opened in the area.

A stroll along the canal is a nice way to reach the nearest subway station (Wat Samyot station) and I look forward to seeing how this area develops in the future.
The park looks lovely and is well maintained. It seems to be popular with joggers in the evenings. Sadly there was a bit of construction going on near the Fort when we visited.

Lovely fort with recently revived Park right in the historical center if the capital and along one of Bangkok's picturesque canals, separating Rattanakosin Island from newer parts of the city. Really great that the City Hall has finally found funds and manpower to re-plant and refurbish this lovely area!

I stumbled upon Mahakan Fort's park on my way to other attractions, and it turned out to be a nice pit stop to unwind so I kept coming back. It was pretty empty every time and has good views of the surrounding area. The park is filled with lush greenery, but it can get pretty hot during the day since there's not much shade around. The park is well-maintained and has comfortable benches. You'll also notice a bunch of noisy crows hanging around, but they didn't really bother me.

After the old wooden houses had regrettably been demolished and some big old trees cut down the area became a rather dull public park consisting mainly of a big lawn. Its saving grace are the Mahakan fort, the remains of the old city wall and it's location next to a junction of canals. However, when I passed by end October 2020 I noticed gardeners were busy planting lots of flowering plants, making the park looking more attractive. What's still missing though is something that would make it worthwhile to stay a bit longer. Why not convert the old "Royal waiting room" into a cafe with a terrace in front where one could enjoy a view of the park and another one on the back where one could watch the activities on the canal?

reviews.authorName: ปภาดา วรธรรมโภคิณ
reviews.text:
Located along Pracha Rat Road,
this market features various food stalls and famous local dishes such as:
noodle soup, som tum (papaya salad), seven-color chive dumplings,
traditional rice noodle rolls, and Western desserts.
Nonthaburi Bakery is also famous for its wide variety of freshly baked bread, cakes, and other pastries.
Across the road, there are shops selling clothes, shoes, bags, and other items for visitors to browse.
Not far away is Nonthaburi Pier, a central transportation hub in Nonthaburi province.
It serves as both the starting and ending point for various journeys, including buses, the Chao Phraya Express Boat, and other ferry services. From Nonthaburi Pier, you can see the clock tower and the old provincial hall, landmarks of Nonthaburi that still retain their original architecture and design. Restaurants in the Nonthaburi Pier market have varying opening and closing times, but most are open daily from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM and 4:00 PM to 10:00 PM. The evenings are particularly nice, offering a good atmosphere for children to enjoy fried rice, feed the fish, and play in the fresh air.
Parking is available at the old provincial hall.
Address: Muang Nonthaburi, Nonthaburi 11000
Thank you for reading.

reviews.text: Pretty decent park although there's quite a lot of construction occurring right now. Good amount of greenery and flowers. Best part is this serves as the entrance to the sky park which is stunning.

Park tucked away near a police station!  Very easy to miss this park due to a renovation project that obscures the entrance.  This is actually part of a complex called Sky Park to link different commuting sides of the Phraya river and should have different types of lanes.  However, it is clear the renovation project is behind schedule (should have been done by 2020).  I am sure once completed Sky Park will be glorious.  I strongly recommend climbing the stairs (there is a lift too) either before or after viewing the green section of the park.  The view of the Chao Phraya River, the sky above, surrounding structures and buildings on its banks is lovely.
A lush, green park that offers convenient motorcycle parking and serves as a primary access point to the scenic Chao Phraya Skypark, thus the 5-star rating
BEAUTIFUL SUNSET'S SKY VIEW ON PHRAPOKKLAO BRIDGE OVER PHRAPOKKLAO GARDEN
Nothing much here, just a good pit stop under the shades.

Pet Tolerant. Yes.

Having 1 or 2 pee stations doesn’t mean a place is pet friendly. Yes the outdoor area you can walk with your pup and enjoy. But dining options, space to seat etc with your pup? Minimal.

Nice spot to come for meals or just to wander around. Probably not enough to warrant a random trip here without purpose, so I'd plan to come to visit a restaurant or particular shop within the complex.
