reviews.text:
Sirirukkhachati Nature Park is located within Mahidol University, Salaya campus. One of its notable buildings is called Baimai Sam Bai (Three Leaves Building). According to the staff, there is an exhibition inside; however, walk-in visitors are not allowed to enter unless they come as part of an organized group.

The park itself is a herbal garden, featuring informative signs that explain the names and medicinal uses of various plants. If you are interested, some herbs are also available for purchase to take home.

This is a spacious and peaceful public park with plenty of greenery and shade. Parking is available and convenient. For visitors traveling with elderly family members, wheelchairs can be borrowed by contacting the staff, making the garden accessible and comfortable for everyone.

Beautiful park just in the corner of Mahidol campus. 150baht for foreigners or free for Mahidol students.
A nice place to relax in the middle of busy salaya

Even though there's an entry fee for Mahidol graduates, the experience is totally worth it. There are so many trees that you can enjoy a walk in the shade. My kids and I really enjoyed our time here exercising together.
Great Nature park inside mahidol University.. Good for healing and take a breath fresh air.. So many herbs and flowers with beautiful scenery. Also rent a bicycle around 30 baht..
Still my peace place for me.

The village is located opposite Pruksa, offering a peaceful, shady, and safe environment.
Security systems include security guards and CCTV cameras. You must exchange your ID card before entering the village. The village's size is just right, offering single-family homes, duplexes, and townhomes. The introductory price is reasonable. Accessibility is available via multiple routes, including Pu Chao Saming Phrai Road, Sukhumvit Road, Sanphawut Road, and Samrong Road. Access to the expressway is also convenient. However, the entrance and exit of the village can be congested at times due to heavy traffic and a bustling crowd. A 7-Eleven, Tesco Lotus, CJ Supermarket, and a fresh market are all within walking distance.
The house offers ample living space. The townhome boasts a 6-meter frontage, allowing for parking for two cars, although only one is actually parked. There is a master bedroom with high ceilings and two smaller bedrooms that can be converted into other rooms. The total living space is three bedrooms and two bathrooms. A Thai kitchen can be added to the rear of the house.
The overall atmosphere feels like a real home.
This village is the best! It has restaurants, a garden, and a grocery store. Payao's restaurant is delicious, the prices are reasonable, and they give generous portions!
Organized in an orderly manner, pleasant to live in, not crowded, good.
It is a project that has both single houses and twin houses. There is a guard at the entrance of the village and there are shops in the village as well.

reviews.text:
The excellent location to release your emotions and excersier along and family. The location quite convenient as per rest room there but some where need attention to fix.

Location is safe with neat and clean the whole areas. However the toilet areas need to be look after. As per damage point a lot. However is good place to visit under pervious King Rama 9 victory monument.

Well maintained park catering to families and fitness enthusiasts... Dedicated to King Rama the 9th.  Walking / running trails, bespoke play areas for kids and 4 bathroom areas...(Only two appeared operational 22 July 2024) With security posted at the entrance and sheltered by the expensive Bumibol bridges flying overhead it is certainly catering to locals.

Out of the way for tourists and not well connect from the northern parts of the city. But still picturesque.

Lat Pho Park
this is the Park for Outdoor Exercise Lovers , or you can just relax.

The industrial architecture of the Lat Pho Canal is impressive. The Bhumibol Bridges 1 & 2 curve and soar above you, testament to the ingenuity of engineers. There's a tranquil park below and here you'll find a wonderful little museum that celebrates this legacy of the late King Bhumibol Adulyadej.
Nice and quite park. Great for running.  Serious Photography need a permission. There is toilet near the facility but not allow to use. You have to go to another small building.

Some green around the bridge, very difficult to find a taxi also with grab to come back on bts direction, just 5km from home but isolated. Nice for a Walk and a snack around. If you visit Bangkok for the first time don't lose time to go there.

reviews.text: On Friday, March 6, 2026, I parked here to rest. There are several shops here, including a pharmacy and a 7-Eleven, which is convenient because there are no gas stations to stop at on On Nut Road.

Located between Soi On Nut 46/2 and 48, there is a large 7-11, various restaurants, a pharmacy, a dentist, an electric car charging station, and a large laundry shop. Some people park their cars and walk to eat beef noodles at the entrance of Soi 46/2.
It's a small community with restaurants, a pharmacy, yakiniku restaurant, pet food store, dental clinic, 7-Eleven, hair salon, and coin-operated laundry facilities. There's also parking available.
A new Japanese crepe shop has opened, and it has multiple branches. The shop is super cute, and the homemade fillings are incredibly delicious!
I went to a barbershop and a 7-Eleven; there was plenty of parking.

reviews.text:
Golden Gate Park is absolutely beautiful and a must visit in San Francisco!

It’s a huge park, so make sure to wear comfortable walking shoes, carry water, and dress for the weather. The wide, open roads are perfect for running, walking, biking, or rollerblading. Bike rentals are available, and shuttle bus rides make it easy to get around.

You can easily spend an entire day here there’s so much to see and do. Parking lots are available, and public transportation makes visiting very convenient. I spent about two hours walking and enjoyed every single minute.

The water fountain area is a great spot to sit, relax, and enjoy the blue sky. The Japanese Tea Garden adds a peaceful, cultural touch and is definitely worth visiting. Food trucks are available to satisfy your appetite, and there are plenty of kid-friendly areas throughout the park.

One fun highlight is the bright yellow chairs scattered all over the park the large seating area in the center is especially inviting.

If your trip includes the Golden Gate Bridge, this park should absolutely be on your must-visit list!

⭐ Highly recommended for nature lovers, families, walkers, and anyone exploring San Francisco.

Golden Gate Park is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in San Francisco. The park is massive and has something for everyone — scenic walking trails, biking paths, gardens, lakes, museums, and plenty of open green space to relax. It’s perfect for a morning run, a picnic, or just a quiet escape from the city. The Japanese Tea Garden and Stow Lake area are especially beautiful.

Ocean Beach, right at the edge of the park, offers stunning views of the Pacific Ocean. The sunsets here are absolutely breathtaking. It’s a great place to take a long walk, unwind, and enjoy the cool breeze. The sound of the waves and the wide, open shoreline make it very calming.

Whether you’re visiting as a tourist or living in the Bay Area, Golden Gate Park and Ocean Beach are must-visit spots for nature lovers and anyone looking to recharge outdoors. Highly recommend!

We visited here on our last day in town.    It's a very large park (1000 acres, I believe) that includes nature trails, a beach area, a Japanese tea garden, a botanical garden and more!  We spent a few hours there and did not even begin to see everything.  The park is very popular with joggers, bicyclists, persons walking their pets, etc.  There are places to picnic, by the ponds, in the shade, etc.  We can't wait to return and see more of the park.

I had a great experience here. The park is massive; it likely takes over thirty minutes to reach the opposite end if you were located on the other end. I couldn’t explore the entirety of the park on the same day, which was also impressive. I may revisit to explore the rest of the park in the future. Overall, I had a great experience and would recommend this place to anyone visiting San Francisco.

It's 1108 acres of park and includes lots of parks. It has shuttle as well. Make sure you have plan to do things so you are using time correctly. There is museum and also lots of activities to be done so plan accordingly. Overall great place.

Building made of paper no isolation in those huge windows, no  soundproofing around the door, the walls are paper thin, I hear the noise from the road, my neighbours all day long  slamming their doors! I wake up everyday at 5:30 am because  someone is leaving apartment and the sound from their door closing comes to my bed!!!

When I stay home and try to study I can here everything outside my apartment and every door slamming make me jump on my seat and heart squeezing.

I have no more patience, Im forced to sleep with earplugs and avoid staying at home during daytime! It's not normal!!!
I complain many time to management. They put notification in elevator to close door quiet. But no use. People stuck in their phones in elevator don't see anything! And I don't bother to notify my neighbors by myself because I noticed that tenants change all the time.

reviews.text:
Chao Phraya Sky Park completely blew me away. I’ve walked past this area so many times before, and never imagined it could be transformed into something this beautiful and meaningful. The moment you step onto the park, it feels like Bangkok revealing a softer, more thoughtful side of itself.

What makes this place special isn’t just the views (though they’re stunning). Walking above the river, watching boats pass below, feeling the breeze, and seeing the city stretch out on both sides is genuinely magical. Sunrise and sunset here are something else — calm, golden, and quietly powerful. It’s one of those rare spots where you naturally slow down without trying.

The design is simple but smart. It feels open, airy, and very human — not flashy, not overdone. You’ll see people walking, cycling, taking photos, sitting quietly, or just pausing to enjoy the moment. It’s not a tourist trap; it feels like a space made for the city and its people.

What I love most is the idea behind it — turning old infrastructure into a public green space that connects neighborhoods and gives the river back to the people. It’s thoughtful urban planning done right.

Honestly, this is one of Bangkok’s best surprises. If you haven’t been yet, go. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and quietly unforgettable.

Walked the C.P. Sky Park and found it a nice break from Bangkok traffic. It's located between 2 lanes of opposing traffic, and takes about 10 minutes to cross, various flowers and plants throughout.

The park is elevated above traffic, which gives you panoramic views of the Chao Praya river and City in both directions. Entrance to the park and walkway are via the King Prajadhipok Park, and not from the main street.

Best viewing time would be near sunset.

Really stunning amazing park. Yes there is a lot of traffic on either side but it's a beautiful way to cross the river and the maintenance and amount of greenery, flowers, and the views of the river are quite amazing and definitely world class.

This is worth the visit if you are nearby and indeed a great idea. It's excellent if you need to cross the bridge as a pedestrian. But if you want to use it as a park, it's too many loud vehicles and too much pollution to fully relax.
if your into iconic places, this sky park should be in your list. but you need to be patient as reaching the park can be tricky. but nevertheless watching sunset here can be remarkable.

The best time to visit is during sunsets. You can watch the sky slowly dimmed and the birds flying back to their nest on the tree beside the lake.

reviews.text:
Noble Remix in Thong Lo is a standout residential option for anyone looking to live in one of Bangkok’s most energetic and stylish neighborhoods. What immediately sets this condominium apart is its unbeatable location—just a short walk from BTS Thong Lo station, making daily commutes and city exploring effortless. The convenience of quick access to trendy cafés, diverse restaurants, boutique shops, and lively nightlife means there’s always something to do right on your doorstep.

The building itself offers a variety of unit layouts, from cozy studios to spacious two-bedroom apartments, catering well to young professionals, couples, and small families. Interiors are thoughtfully designed with modern touches, and large windows let in abundant natural light and offer great city views. While some aspects of the building reflect its age, the overall upkeep feels solid, and regular maintenance keeps the communal areas tidy and functional.

Facilities such as the pool, gym, and landscaped common areas provide welcome spaces to relax and socialize, though they aren’t as lavish as those in newer developments. Security is reliable and adds a comforting sense of safety.

In short, Noble Remix excels in location, practicality, and lifestyle convenience. It’s ideal for those who want to be in the heart of Thong Lo with easy transit access and vibrant urban living.
Lucky to stay here with my cousin. Most beautiful. Insane view!

Love the location, the views, the fact that it's connected to the BTS. The aparment feels spacious. And the people working here arefriendly and always try to help. With had a minor issue which got fixed fast (in a matter of days, pretty sure something like this would've taken weeks to fix in Europe).

The condo offers a great view and excellent location, with easy access to public transport and close proximity to EmQuartier and other renowned malls.
However, the building shows its age, with a smelly lobby and elevators, broken gym facilities, and a door in good condition but lacking modern appeal. While the location is convenient, the high rent doesn’t match the outdated amenities, making it less appealing for the price.
Good Condo Good Trasportation BTS Thong-lor Skywalk direct to building and Convenient store. The best in Thong lor prime zone.

Much less crowded than Lumpini Park. Would come back again.

Lovely green space in the big city. Walking distance to my hotel. I ended up spending my whole morning here to get away from the chaos and tourists. The monitor lizards and exotic birds kept me entertained for hours.

Tranquil Green Space located right next to the Grand Palace in Bangkok

Extremely Soothing and Calming experience

reviews.text:
Easily one of the most beautiful green spaces in Bangkok.

The park is very spacious, clean, and well maintained. Walking around the large lake was incredibly relaxing. The atmosphere feels peaceful despite being in the middle of a busy city.

I especially loved the dedicated paths for walking and cycling. It’s a perfect place for a morning jog, or just to sit and unwind.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for fresh air and a calm, scenic spot in Bangkok.

A well-maintained and spacious park in the heart of Bangkok, perfect for walking, jogging, or just taking a break from the city. The park is especially nice in the early morning or late afternoon when the weather is cooler and the atmosphere is calmer.

The skywalk and green bridge connecting different sections of the park are highlights, offering great views of the lake, greenery, and surrounding skyline. Plenty of space for casual exercise, cycling, or simply sitting by the water.

Facilities are clean and well-organized, including restrooms and seating areas. The park feels safe and is popular with both locals and visitors, without feeling overly crowded.

Very accessible by public transport, within walking distance from BTS and MRT.

Overall, a great urban park for relaxation, light exercise, or a quiet escape in the middle of Bangkok.

A wonderful park right in the center of Bangkok! I came here for a early morning run, and had such a nice experience. The park is perfect for running/walking and I truly appreciate the separate running lane! The park is also well maintained and very clean.

Not too busy when I arrived, around 7-8pm! I'd definitely recommend this for runners and people who just want to relax and enjoy some greenery and beautiful scenery in the center of BKK. ✨

Great park to relax, enjoy the greenery and exercise.

Good views of the city clean safe shaded environment for walking or jogging.

Opens early and closes late, about 10 pm.

Very accessible, close to BTS Asok or MRT Sukhumvit.

Suitable for all ages and families as well.
At the end your visit you can visit the convention center to have an Air Condition dose and have a drink or meal.

The elevated walkway is really nice and great place to take pictures and see people taking pictures as well.

Benjakitti Park is my favorite park in Bangkok. It is peaceful, green, and refreshing, like an oasis in the middle of a busy city. The park is very convenient because it is close to BTS Asok or MRT Queen Sirikit and other public transport. You can exercise, walk, jog, or enjoy a relaxing picnic with friends or family. I love the beautiful landscapes, modern architecture, and calm lake views. The wetland restoration of trees and birds make the park feel natural and alive. It is a must-visit place in Bangkok. The only negative point is that there are fewer restrooms and has to walk far.

reviews.text:
Thana City Country Club offers a rewarding and enjoyable golfing experience just outside of Bangkok. It strikes a good balance between challenge and playability, making it suitable for a wide range of golfers.

🏌️ Course & Conditions (Moderate Challenge)

The course is designed with a moderate level of difficulty, featuring strategic bunkering, water hazards, and subtly contoured fairways that keep you thinking about your shot placement. It's a challenging yet fair test of golf.

Greens: The greens are a definite highlight. They are maintained to a high standard of quality, rolling true and at a consistent pace.

Main Restaurant: There is a main, general restaurant offering a range of Thai and international dishes.

Special Feature: Chinese Restaurant: Uniquely, the club also features a dedicated Chinese restaurant. This offers a great option for those looking for a different dining experience before or after their round.

Restrooms/Locker Rooms: The restrooms and shower facilities should be updated to incorporate a proper separation of wet and dry zones. Currently, the lack of this division can make the area less comfortable and less efficient for changing.
I like it because the fairway is in cart and the green is fast. The scenery is beautiful and planes fly around often because it is near the airport. It's a good golf course.
One of my favorites in Bangkok. Nice vibe and well maintained. Even you could find owl in the day time.
Very clean facility. Reasonable price. I went there for tennis and badminton. Call in to book ahead.

A decent golf course but I had a bad experience with my caddy (albeit the others in my group were very nice). The pro shop ladies were very nice and helpful.

I could write a long list of complaints about my caddy but I'll summarise. Overall she was assertive and bossy, whilst giving the strong impression she had better things to do. During the round she was repeatedly on her phone and drank beer (which presumably they're not allowed to, I wasn't drinking). I stopped to buy a drink on I think the 7th, at this point I was giving her the benefit of the doubt but she came and stood right by me as I ordered so I asked if she wanted something, she ordered a drink but put it in her bag to take home!

Generally there was a disregard for the rules of golf, telling me to pick and drop anytime I wasn't on the fairway. On a few occasions she tried to incorrectly tell me where my ball was very assertively. There was no collaboration on club choice, just telling/giving a club and getting annoyed if I disagreed.

At the end of the round it was dark, I went to hand her 600 baht (500+100 notes) as apparently the standard tip, she thought it was 200 and aggressively said "no, no!!!", before realising it was 600 and snatching it from me.

The course itself is challenging and generally good condition. I was quite surprised to see people swimming in every water hazard to retrieve balls.. presumably not part of the club and people just trying to make a crust.

reviews.text: One of the best park in this area. Allow kids scooting and biking with beautiful and well maintained garden. Also pet friendly with provided bag and bin to clean after your pet. Nice small playgrounds, one for small toddlers and one for big kids. Basketball hoops also available here. Central fountain will be operated in the evening for children to enjoy little splash. Parking is plenty and free for up to 8 hours. 10/10 would recommend.
The park is kinda feels nature there's a slider for children and also pet-friendly👍
Very nice place for kid to hangout, ride a bike, running, walking around and place the water fountain. For parent running, walk around and photo shoot😅

There is a playground and a treehouse for the kids. A huge dog park with sprinklers, stadium seating to enjoy and relax while taking in the sights. There are so many dog lovers who bring their animals here to get out and romp around. There is a quieter side where there are benches and plants near a waterfall wall. They even have an ice cream vending machine just for your dogs lol, we got a kick out of this and we love how they cater to the dogs at this park.
Great park for kids and dogs. A lot of space to run around and some small slides

reviews.text: Santichaiprakarn Park (Thai: สวนสันติชัยปราการ, RTGS: Suan Santi Chai Prakan, pronounced [sǔan sǎn.tìʔ t͡ɕʰāj prāː.kāːn]) is a small urban park by the Chao Phraya River within the grounds of Phra Sumen Fort on Phra Athit Road. Overlooking the river, the park is set in an ancient area of the city and has a long history of its own. Shaded by a mix of small and large trees, it serves as a venue for many events and activities such as aerobics, Tai chi dance, chain spinning, and juggling. In the evenings, both local residents and travelers staying near Khaosan Road enjoy spending time here.

Public spaces are the precious right of people specially the poor. In Thailand good food is not necessarily  in expensive restaurants  but in streets. You bur the food in the street and you arrive in Parks like Santi Chai Prakan stay by River eat and drink (but no alcohol). You have company of Koyal singing from trees, boats sailing in River, good music being played for free by amateurs. I salute this Thai sense of being near Nature and enabling even the poor greater joys through public spaces than the rich who pay to have such serene spaces.
Love you Thailand!
Visited in June 2025. It is a small park beside the river-side and near to Khao San Road. Can get nice view of the Rama VIII bridge. The park is well-maintained and really nice.

A small park, yet well worth a visit with epic views of the Rama VIII bridge, day and night. Watch the boats on the river while relaxing on a bench in the shade. Well cared for lawn and landscapes. Security cameras and personnel make this a very safe park. The temple can only be viewed from the outside. There are restrooms to the right of the fort.

really enjoy the cozy vibe，u can immerse yourself in the meditation and resonate with the rhythm of nature,like the freeze,river,trees,birds,squirrels……and let the conversations with strangers happen spontaneously

Great place to spend time with loved ones!
Strong community. Very nice area of urban reforestation project.
so serene, calm and peaceful
Good practical knowhow & Awareness of environment secure

reviews.text: This place is fantastic. Located close to the BTS station it’s a short walk on the skywalk and definitely will not disappoint. It’s hard to believe that underneath you is a canal. So many well established bushes and trees. The perfect place to sit by day or night and watch the world go past.

(23 /8/2022) Nice spot for photo shooting especially after sunset. Easy access by public transport both BTS sky train and BRT. Hope this project will extend to another phase and I want to see the result that is this project successful or not.
That is very good idea to have nice garden in the center of city, even it's small in the business area..

Not your "normal park"... Think more along the lines of a late afternoon lovers promenade..  an urban green space that offers the backdrop of plants and sky scrapers against the artificial slopes built over a canal.  Different - yes. Photo opportunities galore. Child friendly? A flat no, unless the adrenaline rush of saving your child from every jagged rock, open water source and unprotected drop is your thing. (Did I mention no swings, slides or sandpit either?)
Do make an effort for an early morning or sunset shoot..   zero shade and with the heat radiating of every surface.. your glamour would be on the pavement in a puddle of sweat as soon as of you step of Chong Nonsi BTS - the closest stop as well.

Hats, water and umbrellas.. come armed for the weather.. there is no shelter from a sudden downpour either.

Happy photo-shooting!!
Actually, it looks great and, surprisingly, provides quite some distant for walking or jogging, though looking as a narrow park strip.

reviews.text: Very nice well maintained park sitting along the waterside. There are many walking paths and a running path. Lots of trees and foliage. Plenty of benches and places to sit. Outdoor gym area, kinds play area, bathroom facilities, small ponds etc.
A small but well maintained park in Bangkok. Great for jogging and children. 1 round is 850m.

The park may not be very large, but its landscape is truly stunning. Part of the park lies along the scenic banks of the Chao Phraya River, offering breathtaking views, especially during the chilly winter sunsets. This place is perfect for nurturing both your physical and mental well-being. For photography enthusiasts, it’s a must-visit spot you won’t want to miss!

All bathrooms are wheelchair accessible.

Small park but well organized. It can get pretty crowded. Great for a little run or to get a bit of green. Would not go out of my way to go here again but not many other parks in this part of bkk.
Great view but sad at the state of the river :(

It's such a nice place to unwind and relax. The park is well kept and there are a lot of benches lining up, inviting you to sit down under a big tree. Right next to the river, the atmosphere is tranquil and the view is beautiful. I enjoyed the tropical landscaping with native plants and trees. There's also a vegetable gardening project in the park. I got here by BRT bus. The station is called "Rama IX Bridge" and it's about a 5 min walk from the park entry (bus ticket was only 15 baht  from Sathon).

reviews.authorName: ปราณนต์ สินสอน

reviews.text:
The place is spacious, the rooms are clean, the air conditioning is cool, and the beds are comfortable. The food tastes good and the portions are generous. The swimming pool is clean.
I was impressed with the service from the owner and the reception staff.
We went up to Phaen Thung mountain and returned after breakfast time. We called them and asked them to save some for us, and they prepared a hot breakfast for us very well. We will definitely visit again if we have the opportunity.

I stayed in the "Rak Jung Bey" room. Let me talk about the room first.
Pros (you can see from the pictures):
1. The room is beautiful and spacious.
2. The bathroom is quite good, with a great separation of wet and dry areas. The shower area hardly gets wet around the toilet, which is excellent.
3. The bathroom amenities are well-prepared and look very high-end.
4. The towels and blankets look nice and are wrapped in plastic, making them look very clean. Excellent!
5. The air conditioning is very cold. Superb!
6. There's a hairdryer, but it's not prominently displayed; you have to open the cupboard to see it.
7. There are several bottles of water provided, plus water for boiling, a kettle, and packets of coffee and Ovaltine for you to make in the room.
8. The bed is incredibly wide.
9. The room is very close to the swimming pool; it's just a short walk away.

Cons:
1. The air conditioner leaks water, but it doesn't make the room wet.
2. The satellite TV signal is unreliable; it keeps cutting out.
3. The stairs to the room are a bit steep; it might be difficult for elderly people.
4. The room key... The toilet is very difficult to clean and very hard.
5. The tissue box in the bathroom is quite cramped, and the swing is a bit awkward for larger people (see the picture). It would be much better if the tissue box moved forward a bit more.

Next, I will describe other aspects of the hotel.
Advantages:
1. The admin staff member (I think) is lovely, friendly, and quite attentive to guests.
2. You don't need to wear a swimsuit to use the pool; a t-shirt and shorts are fine. They also provide life jackets in various sizes, and they're free... excellent!
3. The hotel restaurant is right by the river, the atmosphere is great, the food is delicious, and the prices are reasonable.
4. There are many photo opportunities, and the hotel is beautiful.
5. The swimming pool has two types of slides: one single-level and one three-level.
6. Breakfast is a buffet.

Disadvantages:
1. If you eat breakfast very late at night, there are a lot of insects.
2. On days with large tour groups, there are rarely enough seats for breakfast.
3. Breakfast food takes a little while to be refilled, and clearing plates after guests have finished eating is also a bit slow.
4. The third-level slide isn't very slippery, especially for larger people. Because personally, when I was sliding halfway, my body got stuck and I had to slide on my own.

reviews.authorName: น้องฮานะ พาเพลิน
reviews.text: The rooms are beautiful, clean, and very comfortable. The price is also very reasonable. It's located right by the river with a swimming pool and playground equipment. The accommodation is private, the staff are friendly, and the food is delicious. The only downside is that the electrical outlets by the bed are loose on both sides; I hope they'll fix that. Parking is convenient.

This is a resort-style accommodation with a swimming pool and a large waterslide. The water park is great. The resort is located by the river, so the atmosphere is nice. However, you might need to be prepared for crowds during peak tour season. The room prices aren't too expensive. There are individual bungalows, and parking is available in front. The resort offers a variety of food to order, and they also serve drinks with many options on the menu. Overall, I think it's quite good.

reviews.text: Quiet and peaceful. Regardless of hours or day of the week, not once I arrived and seen this place busy. There are plenty of sitting options, and although the sunset is not straight in, it's still a beautiful place to observe how the sky changes its colours. I've been around for a while now and only recently discovered this gem.

This is a relatively niche photo spot in central Bangkok that not many people know about, and there aren’t many tourists either. I visited around 5 PM, and it was quiet, making it perfect for taking photos, enjoying a drink, and admiring the scenery.

The location is in Samyan, just one MRT station away from Silom (MRT Silom Station), close to Silom, Siam, and Chulalongkorn University, making it very convenient to access. After getting off the MRT, you can directly enter the Samyan Mitrtown mall through the connected passageway and take the elevator to the 5th floor.

The area is designed with several steps, seating areas, and tables for visitors’ convenience. From here, you can enjoy views of the iconic “Corn Tower” and other high-rise buildings, the traffic at the Samyan intersection, and a nearby temple that feels close enough to touch. The photos turn out beautifully, and the best part is that it’s not crowded, offering a comfortable and relaxing atmosphere.
Surprisingly, the room in the garden is not a cafe but an library-styled English learning space for the tutoring school called 'McBrown'. This has turned the Sky Garden even more unique.
Very nice view of the skyline of Bangkok. No entrance, it's free. It's on the 5th floor. Highly recommend visiting.

The late afternoon when this area is covered by the shade of the building is a good time to relax here. Nice view of the city and environment cool breeze and a good place to meet your friends, talking, reading, photo shooting.

reviews.text: Good environment and atmosphere. But the time to visit limits of Thai food that we can order. The taste does not reach up to the price. I'm not happy about this meal.

Stopped here instead of going to the Bangkok Tree House for lunch, and very glad we did! The food is delicious -- I particularly enjoyed the stir-fried chicken and cashews. Another standout dish are the traditional spring rolls which we all loved. Portion sizes are generous and the service is friendly and quick.

The location and atmosphere also makes Pobrak worth a visit. Lots of wood (floors, walls, etc) along with a couple of outside decks you can choose from with river views. Highly recommended!

We arrived at the restaurant by parking the car at the temple and walking, the car would not be able to come in. The distance is a bit far. The weather was quite hot because we arrived around noon. The food is ok. The atmosphere is great surround by mangroves forest. After the meal we ask the Resturant to get motorbike taxi.

Oh my Buddha, this place has everything! Situated over the Chao Phraya River, yet in the jungle it's well worth to long walk or bike ride.  The fabulous river view, wonderful food, and service, makes this a must do experience!

Wonderful restaurant with a killer view. Good taste and cute smoothie. Try while you are in area. Recommended for everyone. Friendly staff and good covid and hygiene. Sitting next to the huge river is really a nice feeling. Homestay also available. Enjoyed very much.

reviews.text: This is a park belonging to the Phra Nakhon District Office, but it's a blind spot. To access it, you have to go through the Kin Lom Chom Saphan restaurant and walk right through the entire restaurant. The ground is all dirt, with no grass left. It's a public space that doesn't feel very public at all, especially with people from the restaurant right next to it.

The park was nice, once upon a time. Now it's a dump, like everything else in this wonderful country. The security guard at the restaurant parking lot—I understand. But if you like living in a dump, good luck to you.

My son love this parking

Suan Phatthana Phirom is a public park located beneath the On Nut-Phatthanakan overpass. It faces both Sukhumvit Soi 77 (On Nut) and Pattanakan Road, but the entrance is on the Pattanakan Road side, near the U-turn under the overpass.

The park is a large concrete courtyard surrounded by gardens, often used for flea markets.

The Prawet Food SAL Fest is currently taking place from September 12-23, 2025.

Parking is available opposite the park entrance. The entrance to the parking lot is located at the U-turn. Limited parking is available.

reviews.text: There's a food sale going on until August 2nd, 2025. There's a huge variety of food, and it's all cheap.

reviews.text:
Setthasiri by Sansiri is one of the most impressive housing villages I’ve visited. 🏡
It’s located in a prime area, not too far from the CBD, yet the atmosphere feels calm, private, and peaceful — a perfect escape from city chaos while staying close to convenience.

The management and maintenance are excellent, reflecting Sansiri’s standard in every detail — from layout planning and design to cleanliness and upkeep. There are many house types to choose from, each with a homey yet luxurious vibe that truly feels special. The neighbor and hood are elite!

Everything here speaks of quality living and thoughtful design. 🌿✨
Just 20mins drive to Thonglor.It's such as a pleasant village.
Nice atmosphere, decent location

Easy to get Setthasiri.

reviews.text: Great place to escape from busy bangkok city.not too far. I go from hotel near bbk shopping mall 600 baht as toll 50 baht. Better tell the taxi to come back to pick up. Or use grab. As inside small street hard to get taxi.

One of the most beautiful places in Bangkok! Extremely romantic and the food is excellent. The extremely hospitable Soda was our server and he took some amazing pics of us too! Just beyond great service. We did the dessert class with Joy and he was a great teacher and such a sweetie. After, Joy sent us on our way for a boat excursion. Won’t forget this experience!!

Came here for a weekend date — romantic vibes, beautiful garden with wooden arches and sculptures, and a cool breeze. Had an orange Americano while chilling by the river view. Great photo spots, friendly staff, good service, and reasonable prices. Definitely coming back!

I never thought a place like this existed in Bangkok! Today we arrived by boat and it was so convenient with its own pier. The atmosphere is calm and refreshing, not hot at all, with plenty of trees and shaded areas. There are different zones to explore and so many activities to enjoy. It feels warm and welcoming...

a perfect garden spot for the whole family  time :)
LOVED our visit to Poomjai for a delicious brunch. And then the coconut farm boat tour. Brilliant experience of learning about coconut production and life along the river. Thank you so much

It also features a section leading to the Green Bridge connecting Lumphini Park and Benjakitti Park, making it a great track for running and walking, and it offers free gym access.

Ploenchit Park
A pet park
approximately 2.5 kilometers long, running along the expressway opposite Benjakitti Park.

Pets are allowed to run and play in this area (as pets are not allowed inside Benjakitti Park).

In addition to the pet play area, there are restrooms and clean drinking water available free of charge.

Since Saphan Khiew is currently renovating the area, I just found out that this is a park for walking animals from Google Maps. The guards were not very knowledgeable and only let me get off my bike and then I had to ride on a road that I didn't know where the entrance to Benjakitti Park was. I want bicycles to be able to use this area temporarily until the Saphan Khiew area is renovated. Thank you for the space that allows us to spread the news and information for all the cyclists who really use it.

reviews.text:
💧🌿 Mae Thoranee Shrine at Sanam Luang – The Spirit of the Earth in the Heart of Bangkok 🇹🇭

In the bustling heart of Bangkok, near Sanam Luang, stands the graceful Mae Thoranee Shrine — a small but powerful symbol of purity and generosity. 🕊️✨
This sacred site features Mae Thoranee, the Earth Goddess, beautifully sculpted in the pose of wringing water from her hair — a gesture that symbolizes cleansing, renewal, and compassion. 💧🌸

Originally built during the reign of King Rama V, the shrine was once a public drinking fountain called “Uthok Tharn,” offering clean water to passersby. 🚰💛
Over time, it became a spiritual landmark — a place where locals come to pay respect, seek blessings, and make merit. 🙏🌼

The atmosphere is calm yet deeply spiritual. 🌿
You’ll see people lighting incense, offering flowers, and touching the sacred water as a symbol of hope and fortune. 🌸💫
Many believe Mae Thoranee helps remove obstacles and bring success, especially for those starting new ventures or seeking stability. 🌻🪷

The statue itself is stunning — a serene face, flowing hair, and gentle water streaming down, representing the Earth’s endless kindness. 🌎💧
It’s surrounded by greenery and traditional Thai architecture, making it a peaceful contrast to the busy streets nearby. 🌳✨

Though small, the shrine carries great cultural meaning — it reminds everyone to stay humble, kind, and connected to the Earth. 🤍🌾
Locals, office workers, and travelers alike often stop here to pray before continuing their day. 🙌

If you visit Sanam Luang or the Grand Palace, take a few minutes to stop by Mae Thoranee Shrine. 🏯💖
You’ll leave with a calm heart, clean spirit, and perhaps a little blessing from Mother Earth herself. 🌏🙏💫

Phra Mae Thorani (Earth Goddess) – Uthokkan Statue, Sanam Luang
This iconic statue near Sanam Luang and the City Pillar Shrine represents one of the most meaningful symbols in Thai Buddhist culture. Phra Mae Thorani, the Earth Goddess, is shown wringing water from her hair, creating a sacred stream that protects the Buddha from Mara’s army. The sculpture captures this powerful moment of purity, virtue, and the triumph of good over evil.

The area around the statue is peaceful despite being in the heart of Bangkok. Visitors often stop by to pay respect, make a wish, or simply take a quiet moment during their city walk. The statue’s intricate details, combined with the surrounding greenery, create a picturesque spot for reflection and photography.

Discovered this park by just searching parks near me (I live on rama IX). Took a short bike ride and yea I was quite impressed with the cleanliness and beauty of this park. It’s like a little oasis among the chaos of Bangkok city. There’s a lake with a jogging and walking track around it. You can also feed turtles 🐢 in the lake - purchased at the little beverage and snack shack. I just loved walking around, taking photos, jogging now and then, and simply soaking in the fresh clean air + beauty of the skyline. This is going to by my new spot for relaxation and fitness.

I love Thailand for making use of the space for such a nice park. This park is open for public now. Hope it remains open for public. It is inside MRTA office compound and it is very well maintained.
1 round on the trail is equal to 1 km. There is also picnic table.

reviews.text:
Millennium Park is one of the best places to visit in Chicago. It’s a beautiful and lively park located in downtown, perfect for walking, relaxing, and taking photos.

The most famous attraction is Cloud Gate (also known as “The Bean”), which reflects the skyline and makes for amazing pictures. You can also check out Crown Fountain and the stunning Jay Pritzker Pavilion.

The best part is that it’s completely free to visit. I recommend going on a weekday to avoid the crowds and get better photos without too many people in the background. It’s definitely a must-see spot in Chicago.

Located in the heart of Chicago, Millennium Park is a stunning blend of art, architecture, and natural beauty that captures the true spirit of the city. This iconic urban oasis offers visitors a refreshing escape from the busy streets while still being surrounded by Chicago’s impressive skyline. The park’s design seamlessly combines modern creativity with open green spaces, making it one of the most unique public spaces in the United States.

One of the most recognizable attractions is Cloud Gate, affectionately known as “The Bean.” Its polished, mirror-like surface reflects the skyline and the people around it, creating a magical and interactive experience. No matter how many times you see it, the reflections always feel new and fascinating. Nearby, Crown Fountain adds a playful and artistic touch, with its digital faces and cascading water delighting visitors of all ages.

The park is also home to the magnificent Jay Pritzker Pavilion, an architectural masterpiece that hosts concerts and cultural events throughout the year. On summer evenings, the atmosphere becomes especially enchanting as music fills the air and the city lights begin to glow.

Seasonal changes bring their own charm to Millennium Park. In warmer months, lush gardens bloom beautifully, while winter transforms the space into a snowy wonderland. Whether you’re strolling through the pathways, relaxing on the lawns, or capturing photos of the skyline, Millennium Park offers a unique beauty and charm that leaves every visitor inspired and refreshed.

A Chicago Must-Visit: Iconic Art and Family Fun!
​Millennium Park is easily the highlight of downtown Chicago. You cannot visit the city without stopping by "The Bean" (Cloud Gate). It is absolutely wonderful—photos don't do justice to how cool the reflections of the skyline look on its surface. It’s a great spot for family photos and just mesmerizing to walk under.
​The park is also incredibly kid-friendly, especially the skating area. We spent a lot of time at the McCormick Tribune Ice Rink (and the nearby Maggie Daley Skating Ribbon). The kids absolutely loved the skating experience; it’s well-maintained and provides a magical atmosphere with the city skyscrapers towering all around you.
​Whether you're there for the art, the skating, or just to let the kids run around the nearby play gardens, it’s a perfect (and mostly free!) day out. Highly recommend it for families!

Was there on january during the coldest month in chicago, as asian i was freezing 🤣
However the place was clean and very nice for a sight seeing
And definately the beans! The must have venue to take pict at
And we experience chicago hospitality during our walk there… amazing experience

Millennium Park is one of the most famous parks around the world. Finally I saw the Bean which I have seen many times in movies. Surprisingly it is smaller than what I imagined. There are a lot of great spots to take pictures in or near this square. Here is Chicago.

reviews.text: close to the train but totally quiet. Areally good place to sit for a while and relax. Toilets are there, benches and the sound of singing birds calm down. thanks for keeping it clean , green and refreshing.

Nice and small public recreational park. It's total area wouldn't be over 1000 square yards which surely doesn't fit into the definition of a public park. It has a good jogging track and the small artificial water pond surrounding green plants and flowers. There's also a corner for old ladies to paddle and kids to see-saw. The oval shaped bituminous jogging track is about 500-700m in circumference.
A tiny park you can cover in 15 minutes.

Well design park for people near Bangkok Yai. Lots of people work out here.
The space is not that big for long run, however we can walk more round instead.
When it's night, the automatic lights can help. There's toilet inside the park as well.
Ok Ok

reviews.text:
I lived at Park Origin Thonglor for a while, and my experience was truly exceptional. The facilities are outstanding and far above what you typically find in Bangkok.

This view makes you feel on top of the world!
This building structure is made with japanese standards that made me always
feel safe, especially after the earthquake.

One of the safest building to be thanks to its anti-seismic construction.

The staff is incredibly caring and proactive, the facilities are the best in the city and the Thonglor area is one of the most enjoyable places to live in the city. I couldn’t have asked for a better.

Will be back soon!

This condo has the most beautiful view in the Thonglor area, but after the earthquake in March 2025, things got much worse.

Since the incident, they started repairing the connecting bridge during the rainy season, and the repair has dragged on for months — it’s almost the end of 2025 and the bridge is still unresolved. Because of that, the pool in Building B is locked, the pool in Building C cannot be used, and the rooftop common area in Building C is closed as well. The repair noise is also very loud.

The bridge that was supposed to bring convenience has now become a reason residents pay high prices and maintenance fees while not being able to use the facilities for nearly a year.

Another problem is that after the earthquake, the building structure doesn’t feel solid anymore. On windy days, the room shakes, and even when the elevator moves fast, you can feel the vibration.

Also, when safety officers came to inspect after the earthquake, they didn’t check the back of Building C and sidewalk where the ground had sunk a lot. And now, there’s new construction by Origin project right next to the building — which makes it even more worrying..
I really hope there won’t be a ground collapse like in other parts of Bangkok, as seen in the news in September 2025.

Other issues I’ve noticed that should be improved:
•	Too many mosquitoes in the front garden — no idea why they don’t spray.
•	The sauna room in Building A breaks down often.
•	Some cleaning staff stay in the restroom for a long time instead of working.
•	The storage cabinet in the waterfall room is full of mold and very dirty.
•	The onsen water is not warm enough.
•	The elevators break down too often.

I stayed at Park Origin Thonglor for a while and I can say that the building is truly solid, safe, and well mantained. The facilities are some of the best you can find in all of Bangkok, modern, clean, and always in excellent conditions. I highly recommend in to anyone looking for a quality rental in one of the best and livable areas of the city. Absolutely recommended!

One of the best luxury condos in Bangkok. The facilities are top-notch, including multiple pools, a large gym, and co-working spaces. The design is modern, and the units have high-quality finishes. The location in Thonglor is excellent, close to great restaurants and nightlife. Perfect for those looking for a high-end living experience in the city.
So sad it was damaged during the earthquake.

One of the most materialistic condominium in Bangkok. The location is good for ones who don't usually use skytrain systems. If yes, there is a shuttle service as well but thinking of Thong Lor or Ekkamai traffic, it is always jammed. The project itself is decent materials, providing so many amenities on the high floors of three buildings. The service is right as they have Hampton to take care of the management. The project hasn't sold out, but looks like it is good for an investment since the high demand from its reputation. The materials for the sound proof system is alright. The design is super aesthetic.

reviews.text:
The ferry dropped us off on the mainland from Pak Nam ferry market and it was a short walk to the footbridge and walk to the fortress.
The main path when we arrived was flooded so we had to walk across a stone wall to get to the fortress.
It was well worth the visit.
Nice views and history of the fortress. Everything was written in Thai, however pointing my camera with Google translate soon translated everything for me 😁

One of the places in Greater Bangkok that are not at all touristy but are of interest. This is a fortress that began to be built by King Rama II about two hundred years ago, and was expanded by Rama V and Rama VII. It guarded the sea entrance to Bangkok. The statue of Rama VII is placed in front of the fortress. It has 3 cannons and you can see the rooms where the shells were stored.
A beautiful walking path nearby the river with a lot of trees. The view point at the museum is very good. Generally a worth-visit place for people loving nature and history. It’s empty though

To get here take the small ferry from Pak Nam market pier. From the landing stage on the otherside walk up to the right and near the Chedi you'll find a bridge onto the island. The fort is abandoned but interesting to see as it represents an important part of the defence of the river mouth during King Rama Vs reign. The military statue at the front of the fort facing the river is of King Rama V's son. Great views of the river & mudflats from here.

Phi Suea Samut Fort Museum is an incredible hidden gem just a short trip from Bangkok. The journey itself is an adventure—I took the BTS Green Line to Pak Nam Station, followed by a charming ferry ride from Pak Nam Market Pier. Crossing the suspension bridge near Wat Phra Samut Chedi into the mangrove forest feels like stepping into another world.

The fort is exceptionally well-maintained by the Thai Navy and offers a fascinating look into the Ratanakosin Era and the 1893 Paknam Crisis. Seeing the rare "disappearing guns" and the statue of King Rama V’s son was a major highlight. It’s a peaceful, quiet escape where you can watch boats pass on the Chao Phraya River and enjoy the local wildlife, like mudskippers and crabs in the mudflats.

Best of all, entry is free! It is a perfect spot for history buffs, nature lovers, or anyone looking for a serene walk away from the city noise. Highly recommended for a half-day trip!

**Pro Tips:**

* Visit on a weekday to have the fort almost entirely to yourself.
* Bring water and snacks, as there aren't many cafes directly inside the fort area.
* Wear comfortable walking shoes for the riverside trail.

reviews.text: Perfected

A new public park in Samut Prakan province.
Built by the South Nakhon Power Plant, EGAT (Electricity Generating Authority of Thailand).
Features running tracks, cycling tracks, a football field, a playground, and exercise equipment. It can get a bit hot during the day because the trees have only recently been planted; it's best to visit in the morning or evening. Suitable for all ages, especially children and families.
The place is well managed. There is free outdoor WiFi available for use.

reviews.text: Kanchanaphisek Ring Road residents will discover Kanlapaphruek Park to be the best public park for recreation and jogging. Only BMA buses and Songthaew trucks serve as public transportation to this location. The finest recommended mode of transportation is a personal vehicle. In addition to the playground, there is a jogging track and gym apparatus. Highly recommended for those who prefer exercise space, as it is not overly crowded even in the evening or after work.

A good place for exercise in the evening.

You can jogging in this park (estimate 800 m / 1 round). Some parent and their kids do some activities in the weekend.

Quite nice and shady . There are a few courts for different sports like basketball , football.   Also has A play ground for kids. I loved the huge trees covering the park . It's like a little oasis in the centre of very busy roads!
Beautiful park for exercise. Beatiful bathroom. Have many different sport including basketball, takraw, even badminton. The only bad thing is badminton court is old and a little bit windy sometimes.

Small but nice park. Large enough for jogging.
Nice playground for kids. Also, 1 basketball court and football field, next to the park.

Nestled in the prestigious Nantawan Village, Bangkok Krittha, Rama 9 area, this exceptional fully-furnished property offers a rare opportunity for discerning buyers seeking a luxurious living experience. Spanning a generous 138.90 square wah, this corner plot boasts a prime northern orientation, embraced by lush greenery and in close proximity to the exclusive club facilities.

With an impressive 449 square meters of living space, this elegant fully-furnished residence features:

- 5 beautifully appointed bedrooms
- 6 exquisitely designed bathrooms
- Ample parking for 4 vehicles
- A dedicated relaxation room
- A maid's quarter
- A private prayer room
- An upstairs lounge area
- A cozy work nook

Meticulously crafted with uncompromising attention to detail, this remarkable fully-furnished home is available for the exceptional price of 67 million Baht, inclusive of transfer fees. Don't miss this extraordinary opportunity to elevate your lifestyle in one of Bangkok's most coveted neighborhoods.

reviews.authorName: รัตติยา ปริปุนนัง
reviews.text: We arrived to deliver something and forgot our ID card in another vehicle. We asked if we could use a photo instead, and he said yes. But then he grabbed our phone without saying anything, taking it into his booth. Is this acceptable? (Time: 3:12 PM)

Pros: Great location with future potential. It's on a beautiful new road, surrounded by several international schools. The project itself is attractive with meticulous workmanship. The common areas are beautiful, and the master plan is well-designed and not overly crowded. The sales staff are enthusiastic and provide good follow-up.

Cons: There's a persistent garbage smell from the On Nut waste plant, a long-standing problem in the area. The smell is particularly strong in the evenings and at night, especially during the rainy season. Sometimes the smell enters homes if windows aren't closed. Locals are aware of this and advise checking wind direction. There's also some noise from the mosque. This project is popular with Chinese investors; many rent or buy units here. There might be some undocumented Chinese residents or a small number of Thai residents living in the village.
Private, beautiful, high-standard security system.

reviews.text:
Nice quiet park! Not many people, cozy atmosphere, nice view to the nearest condos. Minuses:
- the park has only one entrance (!) which is really hard to find because you need to go through the construction area to get to the park
- due to the fact that there is a construction area right next to the park - there is a lot of construction waste at the entrance, I think it is unsafe and creepy

This Healthy park is really amazing with a big running track through big and small trees.

I really enjoy visiting here to fo exercise as this place is not near to the public place and you can get a good relaxation from the   Carbon or air pollution.

Wheel chair is also accessible and have a small parking outside the park.

You can do yoga as well under the trees or many other good places as well.

A good place to do your daily routine. I recommend this place.

Visited on April 9, 2025.
The construction is still ongoing, and the entrance is hard to find since the access road is right next to the construction site.
Some streetlights are not working, making certain areas quite dark.
There are restroom facilities available.
The track isn't very wide, so if you're running, you need to be cautious of people walking or taking a stroll.
Also, I spotted a large lizard (called hia in Thai) in the area.
One lap is approximately 1 km.
Excellent, 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️ never too crowded love it
A nice little workout park. It would have been good if they had maintained the park a bit more. The entrance is through the active construction site so not very easy to find.

reviews.text:
Today's visit to view the house was an unexpectedly wonderful experience. From the start, Mr. Kawin explained everything in an easy-to-understand way, guiding us through each detail. This gave us a much clearer picture of the house and life within the project. When we had questions, he answered them directly, without beating around the bush or causing confusion.

During the house tour, we felt very comfortable because he gave us ample time to examine everything. We could ask for more details about any area we wanted to see, and we had a relaxed conversation without feeling rushed or pressured to make a decision. He even shared many things that most people wouldn't know, such as the advantages of the house layout, suggested furniture arrangements, ease of living, and points to pay special attention to. This greatly broadened our perspective.

Throughout the tour, Mr. Kawin was very friendly, understanding, and genuinely dedicated to helping us find the perfect home. He wasn't just showing us around as a job; he made the visit feel comfortable and reassuring, like having someone to help us think and evaluate the property alongside us.

Overall, we were very impressed. His attitude, communication skills, and explanations were excellent. If any friends or acquaintances are looking for a house, we would highly recommend them to contact Mr. Kawin.
💗💗💗💗💗

For anyone looking to buy a house, I highly recommend Salesperson Khun Kawin. He provides excellent service, handling everything from the house inspection to after-sales support. Khun Kawin is truly wonderful. I wish there were more salespeople like him. I have to say, Khun Kawin is truly capable of handling every situation. I highly recommend him to anyone considering buying a house.
Khun Kawin alone manages the entire project!

reviews.authorName: ชอบกิน ชอบเที่ยว
reviews.text: The house is beautiful, but the after-sales service is terrible. It takes a long time to contact the residents. The care and attention given to the residents is terrible. The various facilities are also terrible. The security is even worse. Sometimes they do it, sometimes they don't. Sometimes they're strict, sometimes they're not. It depends on the occasion. It's like they're just trying to get by and get the day over with. There's no village line. If there was, I would know whether the problems I'm facing are just me or others.

After-sales service is terrible. The juristic person is terrible and can't handle anything. It's very difficult to contact them. They disappear into thin air. The common area swimming pool lights are broken and not fixed. I went swimming at night and they haven't turned on the pool lights for almost a year. Safety is not as good as it used to be. Some people are strictly inspecting, some days they just open the door to let people in. Sometimes I see strange people walking in the village and I don't know how they let them in. Some houses have parking spaces in front of their houses even though the project has a rule that says no parking. I've informed the juristic person about this but they can't do anything about it, to the point where I'm fed up. I really feel like I made the mistake of buying a house from this project. It's terrible!!!

The service was excellent. Mr. Gavin took great care of me from the moment I arrived, providing complete information, answering all my questions clearly and honestly. He showed me every type of house without holding back, explaining every detail, and offering professional advice. I felt comfortable and more confident in my decision. I was extremely impressed with Mr. Gavin's attentiveness and service. It was a truly wonderful experience.

reviews.text: What a nice place to stay in Thailand
The house is nice, and the environment is simple and peaceful. The only drawback is the smell, because it's near the Praksa landfill, and the odor comes and goes.

The location is peaceful and the house is beautiful and livable, but the price is a drawback. It's a bit far compared to my previous place, so the price might be a little high. However, I like the atmosphere with the lush garden; it's perfect for a life of exploration.
It's Pruksa Village 126 Theparak-Kingkaew, not The Miracle.

Very nice, although the entrance is a bit far in. The house itself is very well-built, with a well-designed living space. It's a private village. Coming out of the soi (alley), you'll find Theparak Road, which has Global House and lots of food options in the evenings.

reviews.text:
Easily one of the most beautiful green spaces in Bangkok.

The park is very spacious, clean, and well maintained. Walking around the large lake was incredibly relaxing. The atmosphere feels peaceful despite being in the middle of a busy city.

I especially loved the dedicated paths for walking and cycling. It’s a perfect place for a morning jog, or just to sit and unwind.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for fresh air and a calm, scenic spot in Bangkok.

A well-maintained and spacious park in the heart of Bangkok, perfect for walking, jogging, or just taking a break from the city. The park is especially nice in the early morning or late afternoon when the weather is cooler and the atmosphere is calmer.

The skywalk and green bridge connecting different sections of the park are highlights, offering great views of the lake, greenery, and surrounding skyline. Plenty of space for casual exercise, cycling, or simply sitting by the water.

Facilities are clean and well-organized, including restrooms and seating areas. The park feels safe and is popular with both locals and visitors, without feeling overly crowded.

Very accessible by public transport, within walking distance from BTS and MRT.

Overall, a great urban park for relaxation, light exercise, or a quiet escape in the middle of Bangkok.

A wonderful park right in the center of Bangkok! I came here for a early morning run, and had such a nice experience. The park is perfect for running/walking and I truly appreciate the separate running lane! The park is also well maintained and very clean.

Not too busy when I arrived, around 7-8pm! I'd definitely recommend this for runners and people who just want to relax and enjoy some greenery and beautiful scenery in the center of BKK. ✨

Great park to relax, enjoy the greenery and exercise.

Good views of the city clean safe shaded environment for walking or jogging.

Opens early and closes late, about 10 pm.

Very accessible, close to BTS Asok or MRT Sukhumvit.

Suitable for all ages and families as well.
At the end your visit you can visit the convention center to have an Air Condition dose and have a drink or meal.

The elevated walkway is really nice and great place to take pictures and see people taking pictures as well.

Benjakitti Park is my favorite park in Bangkok. It is peaceful, green, and refreshing, like an oasis in the middle of a busy city. The park is very convenient because it is close to BTS Asok or MRT Queen Sirikit and other public transport. You can exercise, walk, jog, or enjoy a relaxing picnic with friends or family. I love the beautiful landscapes, modern architecture, and calm lake views. The wetland restoration of trees and birds make the park feel natural and alive. It is a must-visit place in Bangkok. The only negative point is that there are fewer restrooms and has to walk far.

reviews.text:
I bought a unit here, lived here, studied, and then happily worked here over 40 years ago. Then I moved to work in another province until I retired.
Now I've returned to spend my twilight years here again. Although the building is a bit old, it still feels as good as before. This condominium is in the heart of the capital city, with convenient transportation, parking, a health garden for exercise and activities, and shops selling fresh and cooked food around the building, making my retirement life quite convenient.
I hope to live here until my final days.

reviews.text:
One Bangkok is an impressive Landmark in Bangkok, a remarkable addition to the city and clearly positioned as one of Bangkok’s future lifestyle landmarks.

The overall master planning is modern, spacious, and thoughtfully designed, making it very comfortable to walk around even during busy hours.

The architecture feels contemporary yet refined, with wide walkways, greenery integrated throughout the complex, and a strong sense of openness that sets it apart from traditional malls.

The F&B selection is particularly strong, offering a good mix of international brands, quality dining concepts, and stylish cafés suitable for both casual meals and business meet-ups.

What stands out most is the atmosphere — it feels premium without being overwhelming. The layout is easy to navigate, the environment is clean and well-maintained, and the overall experience feels curated rather than crowded. As more tenants and brands continue to open, One Bangkok has great potential to become a must-visit destination for shopping, dining, and leisure in the city.

Highly recommended for anyone looking to experience Bangkok’s next-generation urban lifestyle.

It’s so vast that we need to know entry and exit, otherwise we shall just be roaming around exploring. They have buggy service too. It’s one of the iconic architectural master piece - very spacious and luxurious. The KingPower floor for perfumes cosmetics is a must visit. Unique bakery shops in the food court. When you are in Bangkok do not miss visiting this place.

It was a shame we didnt have enough time to explore the whole mall but this massive complex was  such a delight to see and experience in person. The gorgeous towers were the main draw for me and they did not disappoint. Day or night, sunny or gloomy the looked gorgeous and stunning! The areas of the mall we got to see were equally impressive. The generous amount of natural light really brought to life the colors of the interiors and it didnt even feel warm inside despite the sweltering heat outside. Airconditioning in Thai malls are really a whole new level. I loved the numerous artwork spread inside and outside. There's always a piece of art to admire everywhere. Great mix of luxury to high-end stores and plenty of F&B outlets to choose from. One thing I loved above all was how peaceful the mall is. Probably it was just the time that we were in there but the crowd was tolerable and the mall felt very spacious. The entire complex is still not fully built with a supertall tower in the pipeline and Andaz opening soon to compliment the existing Ritz Carlton. Looking forward to see it develop fully soon.

One Bangkok is a new and modern mixed-use development in the heart of the city, combining shopping, dining, offices and green spaces in one area. The design feels spacious, clean and contemporary, with wide walkways and stylish architecture. It’s a great place to explore and enjoy a premium urban atmosphere in Bangkok.

📍 How to get there:
You can take the MRT (Blue Line) and get off at Lumphini Station, then walk a few minutes to reach One Bangkok. If you travel by BTS, get off at Sala Daeng Station, then transfer to MRT Silom Station and continue to Lumphini. Both options are convenient and help you avoid traffic in the city center.

amazing beautiful building with interesting eateries concept especially the food court that that provides customer relation service that serve to you the food you ordered. the aeating we had out door waa so private and romantic. just perfect for Christmas eve celebration. reasonable price in hotel-like buffet style environment. we enjoyed the food. very high quality.

reviews.text:
This park has a great atmosphere, with seating and a pavilion.
But there were also a lot of people fishing.

The view is beautiful, with a glimpse of the Bangkok Bridge and Bhumibol Bridge in the distance. The atmosphere is great, and the garden has a variety of flowers.

Walk from the front of the park to the waterfront, and you can turn left and walk along the river for a long distance. It's really nice, so we decided to walk all the way to the end. We saw a large group of fishermen, so we turned back. Haha.

Overall, the atmosphere is great.
Parking: Available in front of the park.
We went on a public holiday, June 3, 2014.
We went in the evening, so there were a lot of people fishing.
If you go on a weekday, there probably won't be many people.
Bathrooms: None.

15-minute park has been open for a year. It is located inside the same alley as a luxury condo that is under construction. This park in Rat Burana district stands out from other 15-minute parks in two ways: the riverside atmosphere, open and breezy, and the creation of a slight Japanese atmosphere by replicating the Otorii gate, the gate in front of a Japanese shrine. You can park in the alley. Walk along the waterfront and you will see beautiful flowers. At the end of the road you will find the Shrine of the Goddess Tubtim located on the waterfront. There are no restrooms, so it is not suitable for walking dogs because there are several local dogs guarding the alley.
Sitting by the water is lovely, and the Japanese-themed garden is beautiful too.
A quiet park on the banks of the Chao Phraya River. In the evenings, you can see people fishing, strolling, and jogging.

reviews.text: It's a water park that's almost beautiful and shady, cool and pleasant to the eye. It's a good place to run or walk for exercise in the morning or evening. However, the place is quite small, and the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration (BMA) has let it go on a bit neglected and doesn't maintain it much. If you visit during the day, there are hardly any visitors, and it looks a bit scary, like an old, dilapidated place that nobody uses or visits. It's a little frightening. There are also no restrooms. But I heard that the BMA is going to renovate, improve, and widen the running paths and other areas to make it look nicer and less scary. They're currently piling up a lot of soil on the sides.

reviews.text:
Prem Prachawanaruk Park is a new public park in the heart of Bangkok, located in the Laksi district. It is a strategic location that connects people, communities, and innovation under the concept of creating a park that brings happiness to everyone—reflecting the meaning of its royal name graciously bestowed by His Majesty King Rama X.

The park is situated along Vibhavadi Rangsit Road and is directly connected to the SRT Red Line (Thung Song Hong Station), making access to the park exceptionally convenient.
A variety of activities are held weekly, thoughtfully designed in an Immersive Nature format. The park is open daily from 5:00 AM to 8:00 PM.

In December,  we enjoy the event “Still on My Mind: Forever in Our Hearts,” held in remembrance of Her Majesty the Queen Mother, honoring the everlasting bond of royal grace that will remain in the hearts of the Thai people forever.
One highlight is the outdoor musical performance, transforming the park’s green space into an open-air theater. Surrounded by nature at night with a gentle winter breeze, the experience feels truly exclusive and memorable.

Visitors can also explore the exhibition “Garden of Queen Light,” join hands-on workshops to create handmade souvenirs, and enjoy a carefully curated food and live music zone.

• January 10–11
• January 17–18
• January 24–25

reviews.text:
This park has a great atmosphere, with seating and a pavilion.
But there were also a lot of people fishing.

The view is beautiful, with a glimpse of the Bangkok Bridge and Bhumibol Bridge in the distance. The atmosphere is great, and the garden has a variety of flowers.

Walk from the front of the park to the waterfront, and you can turn left and walk along the river for a long distance. It's really nice, so we decided to walk all the way to the end. We saw a large group of fishermen, so we turned back. Haha.

Overall, the atmosphere is great.
Parking: Available in front of the park.
We went on a public holiday, June 3, 2014.
We went in the evening, so there were a lot of people fishing.
If you go on a weekday, there probably won't be many people.
Bathrooms: None.

15-minute park has been open for a year. It is located inside the same alley as a luxury condo that is under construction. This park in Rat Burana district stands out from other 15-minute parks in two ways: the riverside atmosphere, open and breezy, and the creation of a slight Japanese atmosphere by replicating the Otorii gate, the gate in front of a Japanese shrine. You can park in the alley. Walk along the waterfront and you will see beautiful flowers. At the end of the road you will find the Shrine of the Goddess Tubtim located on the waterfront. There are no restrooms, so it is not suitable for walking dogs because there are several local dogs guarding the alley.
Sitting by the water is lovely, and the Japanese-themed garden is beautiful too.
A quiet park on the banks of the Chao Phraya River. In the evenings, you can see people fishing, strolling, and jogging.
