reviews.text:
New York City is a place of constant energy and contrast, where towering buildings, busy streets, and diverse cultures come together to create a unique atmosphere. Amid this fast-paced environment, Central Park stands out as a peaceful and beautiful retreat at the very heart of the city. Its presence gives New York a balance that few other cities can match.
Central Park offers a refreshing escape from the surrounding skyscrapers and traffic. Wide green lawns, tree-lined paths, and quiet lakes create a sense of calm that feels almost surprising given the city around it. Visitors can walk, relax, or simply sit and observe, enjoying the slower rhythm that the park provides. Each season brings a different kind of beauty, from blooming flowers in spring to colorful leaves in autumn.
The park is also rich in character and variety. Bridges, fountains, and open spaces are thoughtfully designed, blending natural beauty with careful planning. Musicians, artists, and families add life to the park, making it feel welcoming and vibrant without overwhelming its peaceful nature. Central Park reflects the diversity of New York itself, bringing people from all backgrounds together in one shared space.
Overall, New York City is impressive for its scale and ambition, but Central Park is what gives it warmth and balance. It is a reminder that even in the busiest cities, there is room for nature, reflection, and quiet beauty.

Central Park is truly one of the greatest urban parks in the world. No matter the season, it offers something special — beautiful walking paths, peaceful lakes, open green spaces, iconic landmarks, and endless spots to relax or explore. It’s amazing how you can step away from the city’s energy and immediately feel calm and refreshed.

Whether you’re walking, running, biking, visiting attractions like Bethesda Terrace, Bow Bridge, or Strawberry Fields, or simply sitting on a bench enjoying the views, Central Park never disappoints. It’s perfect for families, couples, solo walks, and tourists alike. A must-visit every time I’m in NYC — timeless, beautiful, and unforgettable.

Best time to visit:
April last two weeks
November first two weeks
January last two weeks

Central Park is love!
• I’m so glad I booked an accommodation near the park. I could walk to the southwest entrance in less than 10 minutes. Such a dream!
• I witnessed the park both before the snowstorm and right after the snowfall. Truly magical. The trees and views were magnificent, but I especially loved it the day after the snow when everything was beautifully snow-capped.
• I got to take gorgeous photos before the storm too — the colors were vibrant and stunning in their own way.
• The walkways were cleared quickly after the storm, though there were still some slippery, icy spots, so be careful!
• The park is massive, yet it’s the perfect place to find stillness in busy, noisy NYC. There are so many quiet, uncrowded corners — especially during winter.

Winter in Central Park just hits different ❄️🤍

Breathtaking. A must-see while visiting New York. It’s huge! 843 acres is a lot of walking. Wearing comfortable shoes. Felt safe. Lots of people. My pictures are taken shortly have the February 2026 snowstorm. Some snow was removed. Sand was sprinkled on the steps. Some walk ways were not shoveled unfortunately and surprisingly. Still enjoyed walking through.

Central Park is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places in New York City. In the middle of the noise and fast pace of Manhattan, the park feels like a breath of fresh air. Whether you want to go for a walk, have a picnic, read a book, or just sit and think, it offers the perfect environment to slow down.

One of my favorite things about Central Park is how diverse it is. You can find open green fields like Sheep Meadow where people relax and sunbathe, scenic spots like Bethesda Terrace and Fountain that feel almost cinematic, and quiet wooded paths that make you forget you are in one of the busiest cities in the world. Every season completely transforms the park. In the fall, the trees turn warm shades of orange and red. In the winter, snow makes everything look magical. Spring and summer bring flowers, street performers, and so much energy.

It is also a great place for movement. You will see runners, cyclists, people rollerblading, and families playing with their kids. The atmosphere feels welcoming and safe, even when it is busy. There is always something happening, but it never feels overwhelming.

What makes Central Park special is that it belongs to everyone. Tourists, students, families, artists, and locals all share the space. It feels democratic and alive. No matter how many times you visit, you can always discover a new corner.

If you are in New York City, Central Park is not just a place to visit. It is a place to experience.

reviews.text:
From my exploration, this place truly offers a "one-stop shop." No need to run between multiple stores. I've divided the highlights into four main zones:

1. Plants and Auspicious Plants Zone
They have a wide variety of ornamental plants, especially Aglaonema varieties with auspicious names like "Jewel Plant," "Super White," "Golden Chain," and "Super Red." Prices start at only 60-100 baht. They also have small potted plants for ground cover or desk decoration, such as Bluebells or Money Plants, at very affordable prices of 25-35 baht. The plants here look healthy and well-cared for.

2. Designer Pots Zone
Home decor enthusiasts will love this section, as they offer a huge selection of pots in various styles. From classic, tall terracotta pots that improve ventilation to modern, trendy grey concrete pots, and stylish graphic ceramic pots perfect for the living room. They have every size imaginable, from minimalist designs to large ones for front yards.

3. Flooring and Structural Materials
The highlight of this store is their extensive selection of paving blocks. There are various wall cladding patterns, octagonal floral designs, and natural-patterned stepping stones in gray and brick-orange tones. Anyone wanting to do their own garden pathways (DIY) can find a good selection here.

4. Decorative Stones and Religious Items
For those who like Zen-style gardens or water gardens, the shop has bagged gravel in various sizes and colors (white/gray river stones). They also have Naga (serpent) statues and pottery for decorating fountains to add auspiciousness to your home.

Overall Summary:
• Shop Layout: Easy to navigate, clearly categorized, and not crowded.
• Prices: Fair, with clear price tags almost everywhere (I really appreciated this; no need to keep asking the owner).
• Value for Money: Suitable for both those looking for small plants to grow as a hobby or contractors needing a complete range of landscaping materials.

If you're passing by this area, I recommend stopping by. You're sure to leave with something useful!

reviews.text: i love you

I found this place because I met some guys from a vocational school who recommended it, so I decided to try it. The camping fee is 100 baht per person.
The campsite by the waterfall starts at just 100 baht.
The owners, an elderly couple, are very kind and have individual houses available for rent.
Bringing your own tent is the best! Sleeping to the sound of the waterfall is so relaxing. The air is cool, you can swim in the clear water, there's a cooking area, a dishwashing area, restrooms, showers, and super long power cords! There are power outlets at every tent. It's peaceful and not crowded.

There's a pavilion for pitching a tent, so don't worry about your tent getting wet. It's right next to the water. In the morning, when you open your tent, you'll be greeted by flowing water. The atmosphere is wonderful, and the weather is nice. However, the rafts that extend into the river are all broken. The park hasn't renovated or rebuilt them yet. The water is so strong that you can't swim. When I went, it was the end of August 2015.

The uncle and aunt who owned the place were very kind. I went on a weekday and had the whole campsite to myself for one night. The atmosphere was amazing. I give it 10/10, no complaints. I will definitely go again if I have the chance.

The atmosphere is wonderful; quiet, peaceful, shady, and the water is clear and cool. It feels like camping at a relative's house with beautiful natural views. The restrooms are clean, and there's a restaurant run by an elderly lady that you can call to order food from (if you're hungry late at night or need anything, you can just walk over, pick it up, and write it on the bill). There are power outlets on the platform above, which is very convenient. If you come during the fruit season, such as with rambutan, mangosteen, or durian, the owner said you can pick and eat them in the orchard.

reviews.text:
Mae Chi Bunreun (แม่ชีบุญเรือน) – Sacred nun of Wat Awut Wikasitaram

A deeply revered spiritual figure known for her purity, compassion, and strong metta. Mae Chi Bunreun is widely respected for blessings related to protection, kindness, harmony, and inner peace. Many devotees visit to pray, make merit, and seek calmness of mind.

The atmosphere around her shrine is peaceful and comforting, making it a meaningful place for sincere prayer and reflection. Highly recommended for those seeking gentle yet powerful spiritual support

This temple is famous about Mae Boonruen Tongboonterm which having many amulet to help people for lucky. Nowaday this temple havingLuang Pu Sia image and amulet for to support good fortune too.
They have fish releasing regularly on every sunday at 3pm.
They have a class for meditation learning which open twice a year too.
Parking are available around its temple.
Wat Awut Wikasitaram,. Khun Mae, a legendary figure known for her spiritual achievements and her ability to guide disciples, attracts a large number of devotees to the temple.
Here is able to reach by walk from mrt. It is beautiful temple, there is not too much people. The temple is located next to the river and there is a ferry pier near by.

Wat Awutvikasitaram" is a third-class royal monastery affiliated with the Dhammayuttika Nikaya sect. It is situated in the Bang Phlat district of Bangkok. In the year 2532 B.E., the Royal Thai Army was granted permission to use the temple grounds as a spiritual center to serve army personnel and government officials stationed on the Thonburi side. Wat Awutvikasitaram functions as a school, providing Buddhist education to novices in both Dhamma and Pali, starting from the year 2530 B.E.

Originally known as "Wat Pak Khlong Bang Phlat" or "Wat Bang Phlat Nok," the temple has a history dating back to around 2310 B.E. when a group of people migrated from Ayutthaya after the fall of the city. They settled in the area and eventually established the temple in 2422 B.E. The temple received its current name, "Wat Awutvikasitaram," in 2442 B.E., bestowed by Phra Uppatham, the director-general of the Treasury in the reign of King Rama VI, who sponsored the construction and restoration of the temple. The name means "Temple of Phraya Awutthapant and Lady Yem."

Wat Awutvikasitaram was first granted royal sponsorship for renovations on April 19, 2441 B.E., and received a second royal sponsorship on April 12, 2511 B.E. On April 22, 2539 B.E., the temple was elevated to the status of a third-class royal monastery. Presently, it serves as the location for the Wat Awutvikasitaram School, affiliated with the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration. The temple was selected by the Department of Religious Affairs, Ministry of Education, as a model development temple in 2539 B.E.

reviews.text: Visited in June 2025. The park is in Siriraj Hospital area, next to the Siriraj Bimuksthan Museum in one side and Chao Pharaya River on the other. There is a temple like building and an old locomotive here also. The park has a very wide open space with good air.

IMO, this place is optimal for cardio exercise and outdoor activities both in the morning and evening. The vigorous one should be avoided in the afternoon, unless you think heatstroke is a joke. At the center of park, there’s a magnificent pavilion you’ll probably need to see with your own eyes.

Most of the other reviews and pics are about the park across the river. Not this one.
My video captures the entirety of the park.  Don't bother going here.

Park looks cool and quiet.

I think it would be perfect for a lite jogging.

It's a bummer that they don't allow any bike here. I brought my 3 year old with a tiny balance bike. By the park's rule, it is a no-no.

Now I address it here on the internet.
So you don't repeat my mistake.
A quiet place to relax. Nice view along Chaophaya River.

Running at night, there are no lights in the park, there are a lot of stray dogs, but the air is good.

reviews.text:
Nice local tennis court near my home — quiet, peaceful, and not too crowded.
The court surface is in decent condition; some areas could use a bit of maintenance, but overall it’s good for a community sports park.
Plenty of parking space, with a few shaded spots and benches to relax after playing. The sunset view here is surprisingly beautiful.
Best time to come is early morning or late afternoon when it’s cooler and less busy.

I’m giving it 4 stars mainly because the court surface and fencing could use a bit of renovation. Still, it’s a great place to play casually around Ramintra.

After being indoors all day yesterday in Bangkok, my daughter surprised me with a visit to a beautiful forest park  and what a delightful escape it was! The park is filled with lush trees, calming pathways, and fresh air that instantly made me feel revived.

One of the highlights of my visit was accidentally bumping into a group of cheerful elderly locals who were practicing gentle stretches, almost like Tai Chi. They welcomed me warmly and even taught me a few moves. It was a heart-warming moment surrounded by smiling faces and friendly spirits.

The atmosphere was peaceful, uplifting, and perfect for reconnecting with nature. If you’re looking for a serene spot in Bangkok to refresh your mind and body, this park is absolutely worth a visit!

This park is a hidden gem, not too crowded and filled with lush greenery. It’s a peaceful oasis where you can relax and unwind.

Parking is a breeze, and I’ll definitely be back.

Oh, and the staff is super friendly and helpful. They even have a community hall where you can bring your kids or work with an air conditioner.

Nice park, but swimming pool needs medical certificate before allowed to use… the park however is so beautiful and well maintained. Every time I go there is giving me a breath of fresh air.  Thailand needs more trees, and this park is a very good start.  Congrats to the staff. Many new plants and trees planted.  And note the more tolerable temperatures. I loved the progress seen today. ❤️
It's perfect for relaxing, walking, running, or cycling. There are large shady trees and the air is fresh.

reviews.text:
Nice quiet park! Not many people, cozy atmosphere, nice view to the nearest condos. Minuses:
- the park has only one entrance (!) which is really hard to find because you need to go through the construction area to get to the park
- due to the fact that there is a construction area right next to the park - there is a lot of construction waste at the entrance, I think it is unsafe and creepy

This Healthy park is really amazing with a big running track through big and small trees.

I really enjoy visiting here to fo exercise as this place is not near to the public place and you can get a good relaxation from the   Carbon or air pollution.

Wheel chair is also accessible and have a small parking outside the park.

You can do yoga as well under the trees or many other good places as well.

A good place to do your daily routine. I recommend this place.

Visited on April 9, 2025.
The construction is still ongoing, and the entrance is hard to find since the access road is right next to the construction site.
Some streetlights are not working, making certain areas quite dark.
There are restroom facilities available.
The track isn't very wide, so if you're running, you need to be cautious of people walking or taking a stroll.
Also, I spotted a large lizard (called hia in Thai) in the area.
One lap is approximately 1 km.
Excellent, 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️ never too crowded love it
A nice little workout park. It would have been good if they had maintained the park a bit more. The entrance is through the active construction site so not very easy to find.

reviews.text: This is a great square with various arts on the walls! It’s amazing place!! You can see great artworks from all around the world!! There are many people who were taking a photos not only local people but also tourists!! And when I visited there, a media crews were taking any photo shootings in the park. Looks like very famous place!! I found an art with letters “Bangkok” so I think it’s great place for a memory with this place!! Just a couple walk from big cross in the city so easy and not dangerous area at all!! Enjoy!!
Some great street art and nice shade from the trees. A small play park for kids. Has 2 pieces by Alex Face a prominent Bangkok street artists

The graffiti was stunning and unusual. We went there as part of morning walking routine. If you are into arts then I recommend you have a look at this street art. It is not far from Ratchatthewi MRT. Fenced and safe.

Oh, I just burned one, so this is the perfect time to run into this place. I'm inspired to be here. I'm inspired not to be like these guys over here. It's starting to rain so I'm going to leave. Hey, I took pictures.
This park was decorated by Thai's well known graffities on the walls and buildings. The location is not far from Siam center you can walk from it or BTS Rajthevi station.

reviews.text:
Old prison converted to public park for exercise and PE purposes.
In the heart of the town near chinatown.

You can take a walk or run good spaces.
Have Skateboarding 🛹
Have Basketball Courts
Good atmosphere

Lot of people exercising during morning and evening.

A peaceful place in the heart of the city, we stepped into this park  on our way back to the hotel. It is quite large with lots of greenery and many squirrels scurrying up trees! A great place to just take a break of a few minutes and enjoy nature.
Nice little park. It’s quite cute and relaxing, good for a short walk if you're nearby.

What a lovely park in Bangkok! I took my lunch here after the market to sit and enjoy it in nature.  It was lovely. Very clean and full of beautiful plants and flowers. Check it out if you find yourself in the area!

Nice park in Bangkok to get some rest, it was quite quiet there, not too much people. Some were playing football, some were just chilling or visited with their kids.
A nice little green oasis in the big city of Bangkok.

reviews.text:
The excellent location to release your emotions and excersier along and family. The location quite convenient as per rest room there but some where need attention to fix.

Location is safe with neat and clean the whole areas. However the toilet areas need to be look after. As per damage point a lot. However is good place to visit under pervious King Rama 9 victory monument.

Well maintained park catering to families and fitness enthusiasts... Dedicated to King Rama the 9th.  Walking / running trails, bespoke play areas for kids and 4 bathroom areas...(Only two appeared operational 22 July 2024) With security posted at the entrance and sheltered by the expensive Bumibol bridges flying overhead it is certainly catering to locals.

Out of the way for tourists and not well connect from the northern parts of the city. But still picturesque.

Lat Pho Park
this is the Park for Outdoor Exercise Lovers , or you can just relax.

The industrial architecture of the Lat Pho Canal is impressive. The Bhumibol Bridges 1 & 2 curve and soar above you, testament to the ingenuity of engineers. There's a tranquil park below and here you'll find a wonderful little museum that celebrates this legacy of the late King Bhumibol Adulyadej.
Nice and quite park. Great for running.  Serious Photography need a permission. There is toilet near the facility but not allow to use. You have to go to another small building.

Some green around the bridge, very difficult to find a taxi also with grab to come back on bts direction, just 5km from home but isolated. Nice for a Walk and a snack around. If you visit Bangkok for the first time don't lose time to go there.

reviews.text: Pretty decent park although there's quite a lot of construction occurring right now. Good amount of greenery and flowers. Best part is this serves as the entrance to the sky park which is stunning.

Park tucked away near a police station!  Very easy to miss this park due to a renovation project that obscures the entrance.  This is actually part of a complex called Sky Park to link different commuting sides of the Phraya river and should have different types of lanes.  However, it is clear the renovation project is behind schedule (should have been done by 2020).  I am sure once completed Sky Park will be glorious.  I strongly recommend climbing the stairs (there is a lift too) either before or after viewing the green section of the park.  The view of the Chao Phraya River, the sky above, surrounding structures and buildings on its banks is lovely.
A lush, green park that offers convenient motorcycle parking and serves as a primary access point to the scenic Chao Phraya Skypark, thus the 5-star rating
BEAUTIFUL SUNSET'S SKY VIEW ON PHRAPOKKLAO BRIDGE OVER PHRAPOKKLAO GARDEN
Nothing much here, just a good pit stop under the shades.

reviews.text: For such a small park there's a lot of activities going on. The park is under the highway bridge right next to a river, giving the park lots of protection from rain and keeping the park cooler with lots of shade. A short jogging, walking track is made from rubber adding some cushion. There is an exercise area with weights, basketball courts, soccer court, badminton, volleyball, muay thai ring, and takraw. Children's play area, and restrooms. Car park.

A clean and  pleasant medium sized park. The park is filled with walking/jogging paths and basketball, volleyball, soccer, and tennis courts (all in playable condition). The park sits on the river and you can sit on the wall and watch the water or walk along by the boats. There are stairs down to the park at the end of the bridge.

Great place to go visit if you want to experience how the locals socialise and exercising. It’s a small little green area in the middle of a busy area of the city and once you find yourself here you won’t hear traffic and the loud noise Bangkok provides. The vibe is friendly and as a foreigner you can easily get invited to take part of the locals different sports activities

Best park but watch out during morning time have open karaoke very loud and after work hour there open loud music for exercise. Other then that love the Big tree and good wind feel at peace here cause of greenary. If it rain don't worry cause of bridge overhead is like roof.

A cute little park. The whole park is covered by trees or the bridge so you always have shades and are covered in case of rain. The running track is rather small. 480m. But you can exit the park and run some sois to bring some variety to your run.

A lot of Thai kids use the park for ball games. There are also aerobic classes like in most other Thai parks.

reviews.text: Jardin  de poche. Very quiet , a variety of  rare trees and plants with name cards. Birds sing different dirtiest. No crowds. Other than employees I found 2-3 visiters. Clean and free toilets. Sculpture and exhibition of Princess Mother who was born a commener but become Royality.

A Quiet Tribute to a Remarkable Woman

We found the Princess Mother Memorial Park completely by accident – and what a serene discovery it was. Tucked away from Bangkok’s hustle and bustle, this peaceful and lovingly kept garden feels like a sanctuary. The entire space is dedicated to Somdet Phra Srinagarindra, the Princess Mother of King Rama IX, known for her modesty, deep compassion, and tireless social work, especially for Thailand’s rural and mountain communities.

The small museum and traditional-style buildings give quiet insight into her life and values. Everything about the park seems to reflect her gentle character: unpretentious, nurturing, and full of grace.

Entrance is free, and there were barely any other visitors when we went – a true hidden gem. It felt like walking through someone’s story, carefully preserved and shared with deep respect. Highly recommended if you’re looking for a meaningful, peaceful stop during your stay in Bangkok.

Took a steady walk from Chinatown across Chao Phraya Sky Park and through a local neighborhood to find this lovely park. No fee to stroll through well landscaped gardens, a small oasis and very peaceful. There is a small charity shop, restrooms, and a museum commemorating the Princess Mother. Lots of places to sit, relax in the shade.

Princess Mother Memorial Park (Suan Somdet Ya) Soi Somdej Chao Phraya 3 community

It's not a large park, with about 400 meters of walking paths, but the atmosphere is very nice because the trees are mature. The park is very clean because there are staff members who take care of it constantly. There are exercise areas and a play zone for children. There are security guards on duty at all times. The parking lot is excellent because it's under a bridge, always shaded, and can accommodate dozens of cars without charge.
